name: History of Medicine Club
description:
From leeches to lobotomies, the road to modern medicine has never been easy. If you’re interested in the evolution of medicine as a field, come learn about the history of medical procedures, education, beliefs, and more with the History of Medicine Club.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: From leeches to lobotomies, the road to modern medicine has never been easy. If you’re interested in the evolution of medicine as a field, come learn about the history of medical procedures, education, beliefs, and more with the History of Medicine Club.

name: NYU APAMSA (Asian-Pacific American Medical Student Association)
description:
APAMSA is an organization devoted to cultural, social, and health awareness concerning the Asian-American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) community. Our organization also offers programs such as volunteer opportunities with Asian populations and resources for clinical or research experiences in Asian countries.

 

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: APAMSA is an organization devoted to cultural, social, and health awareness concerning the Asian-American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) community.

name: Cardiology Interest Group
description:
We are a group of students interested in pursuing careers in Cardiology. We host workshops, information sessions, and panels about different aspects of Cardiology and how other students can get more involved.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: We are a group of students interested in pursuing careers in Cardiology. We host workshops, information sessions, and panels about different aspects of Cardiology and how other students can get more involved.

name: LGBTQMed
description:
QMed is a student group dedicated to the LGBTQIAP+ community within NYUGSoM. We hold forums and events related to LGBTQIAP+ health and awareness and aim to educate all medical students in LGBTQIAP+ health and health disparities. Mentors: Richard Greene, MD  

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: QMed is a group dedicated to the LGBTQIAP+ community within the medical school. We hold forums and events related to LGBTQIAP+ health and awareness and aim to educate all medical students in LGBTQIAP+ health and health disparities.

name: Pediatric Interest Group
description:
The Pediatric Interest Group hopes to foster an interest in infant, children, and adolescent health. Through panels, discussions, and community service, students can become actively involved. Much of our medical curriculum is focused on adult health and care, so we hope to educate students about the pediatric experience and manifestation of disease.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Pediatric Interest Group hopes to foster an interest in infant, children, and adolescent health. Through panels, discussions, and community service, students can become actively involved. Much of our medical curriculum is focused o..

name: Hepatitis Project
description:
The Hepatitis Project was started in 1999 in response to an epidemic of hepatitis C virus infection among substance users on the Lower East Side of Manhattan. Through the hepatitis project, medical students volunteer their time at the OnPoint Harm Reduction Center in East Harlem where they provide education on hepatitis virus transmission and engage in community events such as foot baths for the underserved. Our site reaches a diverse population of at risk individuals. Though our service, we work to not only reduce the transmission of hepatitis A/B/C, but also to educate medical professionals on how to effectively interact with and educate drug users and other at risk populations. Through this, we aim to rebuild trust between individuals with high risk and the medical community and encourage access to needed medical care.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Hepatitis Project was started in 1999 in response to an epidemic of hepatitis C among substance users on the Lower East Side of Manhattan. Medical students volunteer their time at the LES Harm Reduction Center to administer vaccines free of charge.

name: Jewish Students Association
description:
A group dedicated to providing a social, cultural, and religious home for students at NYUSOM and sharing our culture with the medical school community.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: A group dedicated to providing a social, cultural, and religious home for Jewish students at NYUSOM and sharing our culture with the medical school community.

name: Bioethics Interest Group
description:
Our mission is to explore values in medicine as they appear in the clinical setting, and to cultivate an interdisciplinary approach to the moral and social complexity of health care. Close scrutiny of the much-celebrated principles of Autonomy, Beneficence, Non-maleficence, and Justice will expose the difficult balance of medical duty in practice, and invite broader consideration of relevant concepts in law, sociology, history, religion, and philosophy. By examining real-world cases in medical ethics, we hope to promote awareness of medicine's necessary intersections with other institutions, ideas and values, and to enrich our understanding of contemporary issues in biomedical ethics.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Our mission is to explore values in medicine as they appear in the clinical setting, and to cultivate an interdisciplinary approach to the moral and social complexity of health care. Close scrutiny of the much-celebrated principles of Autonomy, Benefic...

name: Black and Latino Students Association
description:
BALSA is a student-led organization that seeks to provide a welcoming atmosphere to encourage multiculturalism and increase recruitment of a diverse physician workforce at NYU with the hope of decreasing health disparities in the communities we serve as well as in the underserved communities at large.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: BALSA is a student-led organization that seeks to provide a welcoming atmosphere to encourage multiculturalism and increase recruitment of a diverse physician workforce at NYU with the hope of decreasing health disparities in the communities we serve a...

name: High School BASIS
description:
BASIS (Brothers And Sisters In Science) was established in 2002 as a joint undertaking between the NYUSoM students and administration as part of a new medical school initiative: the Programs for Preparatory Education in Science and Medicine (PPESM). These programs envision the creation of a seamless pipeline through which students across the educational spectrum -- from middle school through college -- are guided towards pursuing higher education, particularly in the science and health professions.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: BASIS (Brothers And Sisters In Science) was established in 2002 as a joint undertaking between the NYUSoM students and administration as part of a new medical school initiative: the Programs for Preparatory Education in Science and Medicine (PPESM). Th...

name: Quiz Bowl at NYU - All-University
description:
Quiz Bowl is a competition in which teams compete to answer questions in various academic disciplines. We hold practices twice a week, once on Discord and once in person, and typically attend a few tournaments throughout the year. 

 

Follow our Tumblr for Quiz Bowl SNACKS (Some New Academic Centric Knowledge Snippets) and our Instagram for updates about practices and tournaments! 

 

Reach out to our secretary Rishi to get added to our active mailing list!

 

Keywords: quiz bowl, quizbowl, trivia, academic bowl, history bowl, bowl
summary: Quiz Bowl is a competition in which teams compete to answer questions in various academic disciplines. We meet twice each week to read practice questions, and attend regional and national tournaments throughout the year.

name: Muslim Medical Students Association
description:
An inclusive organization dedicated to establishing a vibrant social, cultural, and religious space for Muslim students and those interested in learning about Islam at NYUGSOM. Our mission extends beyond our community, as we enthusiastically share the richness of our heritage, traditions, and values with the entire medical school.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: An inclusive organization dedicated to establishing a vibrant social, cultural, and religious space for Muslim students and those interested in learning about Islam at NYUGSOM.

name: Christian Medical + Dental Association
description:
Christian Medical & Dental Association (CMDA) exists as a safe environment for students and healthcare professionals of all faith backgrounds to support one another and to explore matters of life, meaning, and faith from the Biblical perspective. CMDA at NYUGSoM has a special heart for those in the NYU community and NYC at large. Come join us at our weekly meetings, especially if you've never been—we'd love to get to know you!

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Christian Medical & Dental Association (CMDA) exists as a safe environment for students and healthcare professionals of all backgrounds to support one another and to explore matters of life, meaning, and faith from the Biblical perspective.

name: Young At Heart
description:
The Young at Heart Club intends to ignite interest in Geriatrics in the student community at NYU as well as interact with and care for the older adults in the New York Area. We aim to have a variety of educational events built around learning to care for older adults. Also, our group has a commitment to assisting the older adult population, through education and interpersonal interaction. We intend to foster a spirit of enthusiasm and compassion in our students who will be caring for this expanding population of patients in our health care system.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Young at Heart Club intends to ignite interest in Geriatrics in the student community at NYU as well as interact with and care for the older adults in the New York Area. We aim to have a variety of educational events built around learning to care f...

name: Vocollision (a cappella) - All-University
description: Vocollision is an award-winning competitive acapella group at NYU focused on experimental work in diverse musical genres. Since being founded in 2008, this co-ed, all-inclusive group has performed nation-wide, released singles, an EP, and music videos. Vocollision has earned accolades in many ICCAs, being named semifinalists in 2025, 2024, 2023, and 2021 and winning Outstanding Arrangement, Choreography, Videography, & Vocal Percussion awards. Above all, we're a family that loves to chill and have fun but is ready to kick ass when the time comes! Our performances are on Instagram @nyuvocollision, TikTok @nyu.vocollision, and YouTube!
summary: Vocollision is an award-winning competitive acapella group focused on exploring diverse genres. Founded in 2008, this all-inclusive, co-ed group has performed and competed nationwide. They were ICCA Semifinalists in 2024, 2023, & 2021.

name: Animal Rights Collective - All-University
description:
We are an NYU student collective working to promote animal welfare and rights who believe that every sentient being deserves a life free from violence.

Through speaker events, music and food festivals, activism, and volunteering we engage students on different topics regarding animal rights and welfare. Our approach unites artists, activists, and academics, recognizing that these are not rigid or mutually exclusive categories. We foster a collaborative and inclusive environment and have something for everyone: we support a variety of approaches to the work, from welfare oriented harm-reduction to rights oriented radical activism.

We have a wonderful community of humans and nonhumans who are available to provide guidance on how to live a life free of cruelty and are excited to meet you :)

 

Sign up for our mailing list, access our event calendar, and learn more about our board on our website!
summary: The Animal Rights Collective is a community of students who are working to promote animal rights and welfare, grounded in the belief that all sentient beings deserve a life free of violence and a life with freedom to flourish.

name: Heart to Heart - All-University
description: We raise awareness of hypertension and its health effects by conducting free blood pressure screenings in target NYC communities. We visit sites such as the Mexican Consulate and perform blood pressure screenings.
summary: We raise awareness of hypertension and its health effects by conducting free blood pressure screenings in target NYC communities.

name: Shuruq - Theme Month - All-University
description: SHURUQ is a "Theme Month" club at NYU that hosts events from mid-March until mid-April every year. Meaning "sunrise," Shuruq sheds light and exemplifies the diversity of life, culture, art, music, literature, religion, cuisine, and politics in the Muslim world, defined as representative and inclusive to people from all over the globe, including Africa, Asia, the Middle East, Europe, and the Americas. Shuruq intends to highlight the various aspects of the Muslim world and its culture. This is done through various ways - such as political charged debates, trips to exhibit Muslim art, events catered around foods associated with the Muslim world, personal vignettes about everyone's experience with Islam and many more events. Shuruq aims to highlight the beauty of a culture that we can all call our own and intends to invite everyone to see the various dimensions that the Muslim world has to offer. Shuruq seeks to provide an outlet for the celebration of Muslim identity as well as a platform for the real intellectual voices on Islamic thoughts and beliefs. We seek to foster an attitude of greater harmony and understanding within the NYU and broader NYC community.
summary: SHURUQ is a "Theme Month" club at NYU that hosts events from mid-March until mid-April every year.  Meaning "sunrise," Shuruq sheds light and exemplifies the diversity of Muslim life, culture, art, music, literature, religion, cuisine, and politics.

description:
Aloha!

We are an 'ohana (family) comprised of students from Hawaii and for those interested in Hawaiian culture. Through our club we appreciate, share, and enjoy Hawaiian culture and traditions. Join the ʻohana today!
summary: We are an ohana (family) for students from Hawaii and for those interested in the Hawaiian culture. We are dedicated to teaching the language, history, and culture of the Hawaiian people. Our ultimate goal is to appreciate, share, and enjoy in authenti...

name: N'Harmonics
description: The N’Harmz are NYU Tisch’s premier co-ed a cappella group and the 2019 Varsity Vocals ICCA Champions. A unique and versatile vocal band, the N’Harmonics blend genres and push the boundaries of college a cappella through a fusion of pop, rock, R&B, and jazz music. Our repertoire includes songs by The Allman Brothers, Kimbra, Michael Blume, Jazmine Sullivan, and several other prominent recording artists. Our alumni have been seen and heard on “GLEE” and The Glee Project, and have performed on Broadway and in national tours of Wicked, Spring Awakening, School of Rock, Mamma Mia, Hair, Lysistrata Jones, Mean Girls, and Bring it On! We have performed at venues including Boston Symphony Hall, Lincoln Center, The Playstation Theater, The Beacon Theater, Joe's Pub, 54 Below, The Bowery Poetry Club, and many colleges in the Northeast.
summary: The N’Harmz are NYU Tisch’s premier co-ed a cappella group and the 2019 Varsity Vocals ICCA Champions. A unique and versatile vocal band, the N’Harmonics blend genres and push the boundaries of college a cappella through a fusion of pop, rock, R&B, and...

description:
The Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers (SASE) is a professional organization that aims to empower Asian heritage scientists, engineers, and technologists in the United States by fostering social mixer, career development, and cultural events! This club is not exclusive to Asian heritage students. Everyone of any background is warmly invited to come to our events and engage with our community!

name: Stern Business and Law Association
description:
The NYU Stern Business and Law Association is dedicated to exploring the intersection of business and law. We connect students with leading professionals in corporate law, strategy, and entrepreneurship to help them understand how legal frameworks shape industries and careers.

We bring in speakers from top law firms, corporations, and financial institutions to share their insights, experiences, and answer any questions.

In addition to professional panels, we host interactive workshops where students can engage directly with case studies, explore how legal issues affect business decisions, and gain practical skills for both law school and the corporate world writ-large. 

Meetings are held regularly during the semester & locations may vary, so we encourage you to subscribe to our mailing list to stay updated on events and opportunities.

Contact us at: sbla@stern.nyu.edu
Join our mailing list: wp.nyu.edu/sbla
summary: The Stern Business and Law Association is created to help NYU students understand the intersection of these two large fields and to explore the changing legal field, whether you are an aspiring pre-law student business professional. Come learn about th...

name: STEMinist
description: STEMinist's mission is to empower students to combat sexism in all forms, both within STEM fields and everyday life. STEMinist works to foster a safe space on campus through intersectioinal feminist activism that is informative and supportive. This club is social justice oriented, and is welcoming to all identities, including queer, trans* and non-binary students.

name: Two Birds One Stone - All-University
description: Two Birds One Stone is a club that takes the leftover dining hall food every night and transports it to nearby food pantries such as the Essex and 6th Street Community Fridges. We are one of the largest clubs at NYU, with a ListServ of over 1,000 students. Two Birds One Stone coordinates with Cafe 181, Lipton, Downstein, Kimmel, Third North dining halls, and the Student Government Association (SGA) and the Bronfman Center, and has also worked with NYU Stern Executive MBA program and Argo Tea. To continue our successful expansion around our campus, we need your help. Please let us know if you are willing to help our campus and NYC become more sustainable and more connected with our community. Thank you!

The magazine is typically 80-100 pages filled by historical, critical, and creative writing about photography in addition to interviews with student and professional photographers. The magazine also features a Gallery, which curates student images around a predetermined theme. To join just request through NYU Engage!

name: Stern Real Estate Group
description: Stern Real Estate Group: Who We Are We are a student run, undergraduate club that provides students in the NYU community with the opportunity to come together and engage with the real estate industry. We approach real estate with a finance focus, and we hold weekly meetings where members get to hear from professionals and experts in the field. Our speakers have represented diverse sectors of the industry such as private equity, the REIT sphere, commercial brokerage, and real estate within the banking world. Our upperclassmen are readily available to mentor and advise our younger members regarding any concerns they may have. We connect current students with alumni in the industry, helping bridge gaps for those in NYU real estate community. We welcome new members and value our place in the Stern community as the only club that focuses on the real estate aspect of business within our larger business school. Additionally, we partner significantly with the NYU Stern Center for Real Estate Finance Research to facilitate undergraduate engagement with Stern's real estate community at large.
summary: Stern Real Estate Group: Who We AreWe are a student run, undergraduate club that provides students in the NYU community with the opportunity to come together and engage with the real estate industry. We approach real estate with a finance focus, and we...

name: Society of Undergraduate Physicists
description:
The mission of the Society of Undergraduate Physicists (SoUP) is to provide a space that builds a community for students who have passion for physics, enriching members’ lives academically, socially, and professionally. We welcome all students who have interest in physics, especially Applied Physics majors, Math and Physics majors, and students pursuing Applied Physics or Quantum Technology minors.

If you would like to receive emails from us, please fill out this google form!
summary: SoUP strives to encourage and assist students interested in physics to develop the knowledge, understanding, enthusiasm, and social responsibility that are essential to the advancement of physics.

name: Medical Students for Choice
description:
Medical Students for Choice is dedicated to ensuring that all people receive the full range of reproductive healthcare choices. MSFC recognizes that one of the greatest obstacles to safe and legal abortion is the absence of trained providers. As medical students, we work to make reproductive health care, including abortion, a part of our standard medical education. Join us for informative dinner lectures on topics such as reproductive justice and the legal aspects of abortion, clinical skills workshops, and more. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact any of the club leaders.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Medical Students for Choice is dedicated to ensuring that women receive the full range of reproductive healthcare choices. MSFC recognizes that one of the greatest obstacles to safe and legal abortion is the absence of trained providers. As medical stu...

description: The role of the committee is to provide feedback, constructive criticism, and practical suggestions to the faculty and deans about the pre-clinical curriculum.  In addition to our monthly meetings, the course liaisons facilitate communication between the student body and module directors during each block.

name: Student Activities Board (SAB)
description:
The Student Activities Board supports and improves club life, makes recommendations concerning the policies, facilities, and services administered by the Center for Student Life (CSL). We have three sub-committees: Allocation; Development; and Governance. Each committee is composed of members of various student-led clubs and the Student Government Assembly.

 

All NYU student clubs and organizations, including identity-focused groups, are open to all students regardless of a student's background or identity (e.g. race, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender identity, religion, disability, or any other protected characteristics).

description: Chinese Mei Society, as one of the largest Chinese cultural organizations on campus is dedicated towards spreading awareness and promoting Chinese culture. We aim to create cultural events, allowing interested students to experience Chinese culture, as well as social events, allowing students with similar interests to find friends, form communities, and find their NYU family. We encourage all students, regardless of gender, religion, or ethnicity, to attend our events. Becoming a member of the Chinese Mei Society is non-binding and attendance at events is not required. Come and go as you please! Oh, and we also have food.

description:
PLAY: Games at NYU is the place for any and all games enthusiasts to gather, play games, and be social. We meet on a regular basis and have a variety of games to pick and choose from to play with others. Our focus is primarily on board games and card games, but all forms of tabletop gaming are welcome! name: APC Rhythm - All-University
description:
APC Rhythm is NYU’s premier co-ed a cappella group. We practice twice a week in preparation for performances throughout each semester. Our membership spans across NYU’s schools.

Above all, APC Rhythm provides community and a creative outlet for students who love to sing together. We hold auditions at the beginning of each semester. No musical experience is required to join — just a passion for singing!

Follow us on social media to stay up to date with our performances and for more information about upcoming auditions.

IG: @apcrhythm

TikTok: @apc.rhythm

name: Students for Sexual Respect - All-University
description: Students for Sexual Respect is a survivor-driven student group that promotes discourse about sexual respect on campus. We supply the NYU community with survivor-centered programming and policy change, in pursuit of reproductive justice, sexual autonomy, and access to education free from gender-based discrimination for all students. We work to ensure that NYU maintains prevention programs, crisis services, and disciplinary procedures in compliance with relevant state and federal law.
summary: Students for Sexual Respect is a survivor-driven student group that promotes discourse about sexual respect on campus. We supply the NYU community with survivor-centered programming and policy change, in pursuit of reproductive justice, sexual autonomy...

name: Alumni Hall
description: Located in the heart of East Village, Alumni Hall has approximately 450 upperclass and graduate residents. We share our programs and staff with our East Village neighbors in the Green House at 7th Street. Together these two buildings make up the 7A Partnership. Alumni consists of 100% single bedrooms - every resident has a bedroom to themselves and shares a suite with 1-3 other suitemates. Bedrooms have a bed, desk, closet, and dresser. Common areas include a table with 2 chairs, fridge, stove, sink, and cabinets. Alumni has two recently renovated lounges: a TV lounge with a pool table, and a study lounge with plenty of space. There are also smaller study lounges on floors 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 13, 14, and 15. Laundry is in the basement. There are plenty of activities to get involved with at Alumni. The 7A Partnership Hall Council is a great opportunity that allows you to become a leader in the community, by either running for elected office or serving as a floor representative. The 14 RAs and hall council plans a variety of programs for the buildings and it is an excellent way for residents to get to know one another. Also, there are programs planned by the Faculty Fellows in Residence, Jim Calder and Joan Harmon. These events range from Broadway shows, food tastings, to discussions with distinguished alumni.
summary: Located in the heart of East Village, Alumni Hall has approximately 450 upperclass and graduate residents. We share our programs and staff with our East Village neighbors in the Green House at 7th Street. Together these two buildings make up the 7A Par...

name: Stern and Tisch Entertainment Business Association
description: The Stern Tisch Entertainment Business Association brings together a diverse and talented group of students at New York University who share a common interest in the entertainment industry. STEBA provides a forum for student interaction with industry professionals as well as a grounded platform for students to gain experience in their chosen field. Emphasis is placed on the ever-changing worlds of film, television, music, print, and theater. Though STEBA is firmly grounded in the prestigious Stern and Tisch schools, membership is open to students from all NYU undergraduate colleges. One of STEBA's most successful ventures has been to introduce a new curriculum to the Stern School of Business, Tisch School of the Arts and Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development, which is a cross-school minor in the Business of Entertainment, Media, and Technology. Through these efforts, STEBA is creating a heightened presence in the world of academia while simultaneously encouraging entertainment firms to take part in higher education by utilizing entertainment executives as professors.
summary: The Stern & Tisch Entertainment Business Association brings together a diverse and talented group of students at New York University who share a common interest in the entertainment industry. STEBA provides a forum for student interaction with industry...

name: Brittany Hall
description:
The newly renovated Brittany Hall has a diverse community of 579 first-year students with three “Explorations” floors where students can apply to live based on the themes. This beautiful and historic building includes the Rhodes Room, which is a large lounge and performance space, as well as a recreation room, music room, dance room, Penthouse Study Lounge, and laundry room.

Our Brittany Hall team consists of two full-time live-in professional staff members (Residence Hall Director and Residence Hall Assistant Director), one Residence Hall Resource Manager, fourteen Resident Assistants, one Resource Center Assistant, and two Faculty-Fellows in Residence and their family. We are also known for Brittany's Official Student Student (B.O.S.S.), which is the community’s active hall council that advocates for and enhances each student’s residential experience.

We encourage you to learn more about the Brittany Hall community and our team by checking out the building's social media posts on Twitter, Facebook, and more!
summary: The newly renovated Brittany Hall has a diverse community of 579 first-year students with three “Explorations” floors where students can apply to live based on the themes. This beautiful and historic building includes the Rhodes Room, which is a large ...

name: Israel Journal at NYU - All-University
description: The Israel Journal at NYU is an explanatory journal dedicated to clearing up the conversation around Israel. Check out our website at theisraeljournal.com.
summary: The Israel Journal at NYU is a nonpartisan explanatory journal dedicated to clearing up the conversation around Israel, focusing on showcasing Israel as more than just conflict.

name: Construction Management Association of America
description: The Student Chapter of the Construction Management Association of America (CMAA) serves as a dynamic platform for Civil Engineering and Construction Management students. We offer hands-on learning opportunities through construction site tours, guest speaker events, conferences, and interactive workshops. We are committed to bridging the gap between the classroom and the field, empowering future leaders in construction with industry exposure, professional connections, and practical experience.
summary: The CMAA Student Chapter connects Civil Engineering and Construction Management students with the industry through site visits, guest speakers, and hands-on events, bridging the gap between classroom learning and real-world experience.

name: Dermatology Interest Group
description:
The medical student-interest group in Dermatology aims to provide information and exposure to the specialty of Dermatology through multiple initiatives: organizing a lecture series to update students on current advances in skin disease diagnosis, treatment and research, establishing opportunities to shadow dermatologists, arranging for students to meet with Dermatology residents to learn about the residency application process, and facilitating participation in national dermatological conferences. Additionally, this club intends to provide both NYU medical students and members of the local community with information regarding important issues on cutaneous diseases, such as the importance of protection from sun exposure, bioterrorism related skin diseases, and the increase in sexually transmitted diseases involving the skin. As a special project, a program was established with a local public school to educate adolescents about the importance of skin protection as well as to discuss anatomy and pathophysiology of the skin in a relevant and age pertinent manner to increase the interest of students in science.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The medical student-interest group in Dermatology aims to provide information and exposure to the specialty of Dermatology through multiple initiatives: speaker panels, journal clubs, skin safety education outreach events, and a faculty lecture series.

name: New York Water Environment Association Student Club
description: The NYWEA Student Chapter strives to promote student interest in the environment; provide opportunities for the exchange of information and ideas between students, professors, and professionals; provide a common ground where students from various disciplines related to the environment can express their understanding through organized exchange of knowledge.
summary: The NYWEA Student Chapter strives to promote student interest in the environment; provide opportunities for the exchange of information and ideas between students, professors, and professionals.

name: Mixtapes, The (co-ed a capella group) - All-University
description: We are The Mixtapes, a high-energy co-ed a cappella group at NYU! As our name suggests, we perform a variety of pop, funk, rock (nostalgic and modern), and r&b music; songs you might put on a mixtape for your friends! We take pride in intricately arranging all of our own music. Our love for performing the songs themselves and for each other comes through every time we take the stage. We've competed at the ICCA only five times and have won two quarterfinals, received special awards for outstanding arrangements and choreography, and have proceeded to compete at semifinals three times. If you'd like to learn more about The Mixtapes, please check out our Facebook, tweet us @TheMixtapesNYU, or email us at themixtapes@gmail.com for more information.
summary: We are The Mixtapes, a high-energy co-ed a cappella group at NYU! As our name suggests, we perform a variety of pop, funk, rock (nostalgic and modern), and r&b music; songs you might put on a mixtape for your friends! We take pride in intricately arran...

name: Internal Medicine Interest Group
description:
The purpose of this group is to organize events within the Internal Medicine department, provide shadowing opportunities and encourage interest in this very exciting and diverse field.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The purpose of this group is to organize events within the Internal Medicine department, provide shadowing opportunities and encourage interest in this very exciting and diverse field.

name: Stern Political Economy Exchange
description: SPEX explores the intersections between business, politics and economics. We dissect these complex issues through open group discussions and speaker-led dialogues. We also have a professional development program, a research journal, and a podcast. Interested in learning about the global political economy, expanding your business acumen, and joining an inclusive community? SPEX is the club for you!
summary: SPEX explores the intersections between business, politics and economics, with insightful general meetings and multiple professional development opportunities. If any of this interests you, SPEX is the club for you!

name: Disability Student Union at NYU - All-University
description: Comprised of students with and without disabilities, the Disability Student Union (DSU) at NYU works to improve accessibility on campus, promote inclusion, and build community.
summary: Comprised of students with and without disabilities, the Disability Student Union (DSU) at NYU works to improve accessibility on campus, promote inclusion, and build community.

name: South Asian Students Association (SHRUTI) - All-University
description: NYU SHRUTI was established in 1993 as a means to serve the growing South Asian population at New York University in New York City. Since then, the association has grown beyond imagination. With over 600 participating members, an average of 4 major events hosted a year, and a nationwide presence among other South Asian organizations, festivals, and charities, NYU SHRUTI has become a phenomenon. Dedicated to promoting cultural awareness, activism, and unity among the South Asian community at New York University, SHRUTI makes a sincere effort to work hand in hand with both Asian and Non-Asian organizations to encourage diversity and advance communication among disparate groups.
summary: NYU SHRUTI was established in 1993 as a means to serve the growing South Asian population at New York University in New York City. Since then, the association has grown beyond imagination. With over 600 participating members, an average of 4 major even...

name: Emergency Medicine Interest Group
description:
The Emergency Medicine Interest Group (EMIG) is primarily responsible for introducing medical students at NYUGSOM to topics in emergency medicine, and teaching key clinical hands-on skills such as phlebotomy, suturing, reading chest x-rays, etc... Students are also invited to shadow residents at the Bellevue Hospital Center Adult Emergency Service. Classes, lectures, and hands-on workshops are offered throughout the school year.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: EMIG is primarily responsible for introducing medical students at NYUGSOM to topics in emergency medicine, and teaching key clinical hands-on skills such as phlebotomy, suturing, reading chest x-rays, etc.

name: Supporting Excellence & Advocating Diversity
description: Supporting Excellence and Advocating Diversity (SEAD) is the premier campus collective for underrepresented business students. The organization is dedicated to fostering a community of educated, innovative, and engaged business leaders through professional development, academic enrichment, and leadership development. SEAD was founded at Stern in 2003 as an organization for first generation, minority college students. Since then the club has grown to become a tight-knit community of diverse students interested in pursuing careers in business.
summary: Supporting Excellence and Advocating Diversity (SEAD) is the premier campus collective for underrepresented business students. The organization is dedicated to fostering a community of educated, innovative, and engaged business leaders through professi...

name: Design for America at NYU
description: Design for America at NYU is founded on our passion and belief that we can use human-centered design to tackle local social issues to develop solutions that have an impact locally and globally. As a DFA Studio, we are part of a movement, which tackles America’s most daunting issues using our abilities at the local level. DFA NYU is a powerful platform to connect students from different schools and develop a community of socially mindful change makers. We provide opportunities for students to go outside of the classroom and put their learning into action through social innovation and entrepreneurship. We transform students from thinkers to doers by providing a space to collaborate and bring ideas to life.
summary: Design for America at NYU is founded on our passion and belief that we can use human-centered design to tackle local social issues to develop solutions that have an impact locally and globally.

name: Muslim Graduate Student Group (MGSG) - All-University
description:
Welcome to the Muslim Graduate Students Group! We pride ourselves on being a space for Muslim Graduate students. We are inclusive and hope to ease your transition at NYU. Being a graduate student is difficult with all the academic and personal responsibilities that follow. Just know that you have a space with people who you can relate to and study with! 

We hope you can gain from even attending one of our events or taking part in any resources we have for you. 

We are cool and very nice! Don't be intimidated and we hope we can benefit from the blessing that is uniquely you in our spaces!
summary: Welcome to the Muslim Graduate Students Group! We hope to be a factor in easing your transition at NYU. Being a graduate student is difficult with all the academic and personal responsibilities. Just know that you have your space at NYU :)

name: Science Fiction & Fantasy Club - All-University
description: Science-Fiction and Fantasy Club serves to help the nerdier side of NYU find community! We meet weekly in TBD, and we also have frequent events that are guided by our members' interests. Please join our Discord (you can find it through the NYU discord) to engage with other members and for more information!
summary: Science-Fiction and Fantasy Club serves to help the nerdier side of NYU find community! We meet weekly in GCASL or Kimmel on Thursdays (5:30 PM on into the night), and we also have frequent events that are guided by our members' interests.

name: NYU Fraternity and Sorority Life
description: Fraternity & Sorority Life (FSL) has been in partnership with New York University since 1847. Though much has changed over the years, the core values of fraternal life has remained the same. There are over 1,100 students who are members of our greek-lettered organizations. These organizations are run by 3 self-governing councils called the Panhellenic Council, Multicultural Greek Council, and the Interfraternity Council. Interested in joining? Email fsl@nyu.edu.
summary: Fraternity and Sorority Life at New York University

name: Student Animation League
description:
The Student Animation League (SAL) is a student organization that coordinates events for students studying in the Animation Area at New York University. These include guest lectures, screenings, drawing exercises, and more. We also forward opportunities to our subscriber list, aiding students in finding collaboration for their films.

 

​SAL also organizes, with great support from the staff and faculty of the Animation Area, the annual Spring Animation Festival, which displays a variety of student work, including a collection of animated films from that year's Intro, Intermediate, Experimental, and Advanced Animation production classes.
summary: The Student Animation League (SAL) is a student organization which coordinates events for students and alumni interested in animation. These include guest lectures, screenings, workshops, panel discussions, mixers, and more.

name: Kesher: Reform Jews at NYU - All-University
description:
Kesher means connection. It is on these grounds that we form our organization, whose central mission is to bring Jewish students together through ritual practice, communal programming, and social action. Kesher’s door is open for all students to come as they are. We are a space in which members and non-members alike are invited to shape their Jewish identity, pursue social justice through Jewish values, observe the Jewish calendar, and live Torah. We are a space for exploration, education, and critical discourse. We believe in adaptation and in recognizing that it is not only possible but in fact more holy to preserve our traditions while ensuring that they are inclusive and never prohibitive, exclusionary, or demeaning towards any person.

Statement of Inclusion Kesher welcomes the full participation and protection of all who seek community. In accordance with our values, we do not discriminate or permit discrimination or harassment against any person or group of persons on the grounds of race, religion, color, sex, age, disability status, ethnicity, ancestry, national origin, socio-economic status, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, marital status, familial status, pregnancy, veteran status, citizenship and/or genetic information. Furthermore, we welcome the full participation of Jews of all backgrounds including those from multi-faith and interfaith families, LGBTQ families, families with single parents, and those who are Jews by choice. We are proud of the diversity in our community.
summary: Kesher means connection. It is on these grounds that we form our organization, whose central mission is to bring Reform Jewish students together through ritual practice, communal programming, and social action. Kesher’s door is open for all students to...

name: BAEDEKER - All-University
description:
Baedeker Magazine is NYU's biannual print and online travel magazine. Every semester, Baedeker receives incredible photos and tales from around the world, submitted by NYU students, alumni, and faculty. Our dedicated teams work hard to publish a polished magazine that showcases the unique stories of each of our writers and photographers.

 

Baedeker Magazine was founded in 2004; however, it was deactivated in 2019 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. In the Spring of 2023, Baedeker was restarted and has since grown larger than ever, inspiring students to travel abroad and incorporating new events, club collaborations, and more.

 

For more information and to get involved, visit www.nyubaedeker.org and follow @nyu.baedeker on Instagram.
summary: Baedeker is the biannual print & online travel magazine of NYU. The publication features writing and photography from around the world submitted by NYU students, alum, and faculty. Visit www.nyubaedeker.org for more info.

name: Carlyle Court Residence Hall
description: Carlyle Court is a dynamic community of 740 primarily sophomore residents. Made up of three apartment buildings that overlook Union Square Park and center around a large, tree-filled courtyard. There are two newly renovated lounge spaces; the C1 Study Lounge in Tower 1 and the C2 Entertainment Lounge in Tower 2. In Tower 3 (C3) we have a practice room complete with full length mirrors, a ballet bar and multiple pianos. [Please note: the practice rooms will be closed for the summer for maintenance purposes.] Within each tower there are two elevators and one laundry room. Carlyle Court has two types of room accommodations available to residents one-bedroom suites that accommodate three to four residents and studio apartments that accommodate two residents. We have two live-in faculty members who do programming (Kristin Horton and Lorena Llosa), two Explorations communities: Food for Thought and Serve the City, an award winning student-run hall government (Embassy), and 15 Resident Assistants who will spend the year providing fun ways for residents to get out and enjoy NYC!
summary: Carlyle Court is a dynamic community of 740 primarily sophomore residents. Made up of three apartment buildings that overlook Union Square Park and center around a large, tree-filled courtyard. There are two newly renovated lounge spaces; the C1 Study ...

name: Gramercy Green Residence Hall
description: Located in the Gramercy Park area of NYC, this residence hall offers NYU students a unique living experience with a variety of amenities and living-learning opportunities. Primarily housing sophomore students, the building maintains 20 resident assistants, two Faculty Fellows In Residence (Philip Kain with partner William Crow and Olivia Birdsall), several Explorations Living Learning communities, and two Chaplains in Residence (Rabbi Yehuda Sarna and Imam Khalid Latif). The facilities include TV, computer, study lounges, and a fitness center, as well as a large programming space for a variety of events and activities. The area in which Gramercy Green is located draws upon its connection with numerous historic landmarks nearby, including the Beaux Arts Appellate Courthouse, the Flatiron Building, the MetLife Clock Tower, the New York Life Building, and the Empire State Building. The Greenmarket at Union Square is the favorite local shopping destination for the Gramercy residents but the area also houses a variety of grocery stores and local delis. Stores of all descriptions can be found on Park Avenue South, and there is an expansive choice of restaurants in the area. Today, the Gramercy Park area is a thriving neighborhood with the right mix of stores and residential buildings.
summary: Located in the Gramercy Park area of NYC, this residence hall offers NYU students a unique living experience with a variety of amenities and living-learning opportunities. Primarily housing sophomore students, the building maintains 20 resident assista...

name: Washington Square News
description:
The Washington Square News is independent, student-run newspaper at NYU. WSN does not process join requests through NYU Engage. Instead, visit nyunews.com/join or email mgmt@nyunews.com to learn more about getting involved.

Subscribe to WSN at nyunews.com/subscribe.
summary: The official, independent, student-run newspaper at NYU. Find us online at nyunews.com or at @nyunews on social media.

name: Earth Matters - All-University
description: EarthMatters is NYU's campus-wide environmental club. Founded in 1982, Earth Matters is the oldest environmental club at NYU and is home to many interests and initiatives. We have general meetings on Tuesdays at 6:30 pm in NYU's Kimmel Center.. Mission: To create active, positive, ecological change on campus and across the world. Description: We are an open community for the future environmental leaders of tomorrow. We believe in providing the resources and training to those interested in making a difference. We are a training ground and a space to incubate ideas. Our Instagram: @earthmattersnyu
summary: EarthMatters is NYU's campus-wide environmental club. Founded in 1982, Earth Matters is the oldest environmental club at NYU and is home to many interests and initiatives. We have general meetings on Tuesdays at 6:30 pm in NYU's Kimmel Center.. Mission...

name: Stern & Tisch Entertainment Business Association
description: The Stern Tisch Entertainment Business Association brings together a diverse and talented group of students at New York University who share a common interest in the entertainment industry. STEBA provides a forum for student interaction with industry professionals as well as a grounded platform for students to gain experience in their chosen field. Though our programmings are grounded in the Stern-Tisch-Steinhardt collaborative BEMT minor, our membership is open to students OF ANY SCHOOL (not Stern or Tisch exclusive). Join us to find your community!
summary: The Stern Tisch Entertainment Business Association provides a community for the diverse and talented group of students at New York University who share a common interest in the entertainment industry. Our membership is open to students of any school.

name: Broome Street Residence Hall
description: The Broome Street Residential College is located directly between New York City's historic Little Italy and trendy SoHo neighborhoods. Broome Street is home to NYU's Upperclass Residential College for sophomores, juniors, and seniors. Implemented in the Fall of 2007, this living-learning community prides itself with its two Faculty Families in Residence, eight Faculty Affiliates connected to various streams, strong student-led BEAST Hall Council, and nine graduate and undergraduate Resident Assistants. As residents of the Residential College at Broome, students may opt to live here from their sophomore through senior years. Check out our blog to learn more about Broome (www.nyuresidentialcollege.wordpress.com)!
summary: The Broome Street Residential College is located directly between New York City's historic Little Italy and trendy SoHo neighborhoods. Broome Street is home to NYU's Upperclass Residential College for sophomores, juniors, and seniors. Implemented in th...

name: Tisch New Theatre
description:
Tisch New Theatre (TNT) was born out of a pressing need for a participatory theatre outlet for all NYU students. Over the nearly twenty years since, TNT has become the premier student theatre organization at NYU. It has established itself as an avenue for students to work in all facets of the theatre, including performance, management, direction, production, and design.

 

Engage is not our primary contact method. Please do not hesitate to email us at tischnewtheatre@gmail.com with any questions. Updates regarding auditions, applications, and upcoming shows can be found on our website or Instagram! We are an application-based club. 

 

Spring 2026:

Join us, and enter a world of mystery and magic. This spring, Tisch New Theatre presents Pippin Off-Broadway at the A.R.T./New York Theatres (502 W 53rd St) April 25th through May 3rd.

 

Pippin follows a restless prince pulled into a world of spectacle by the Leading Player as he searches for something “completely fulfilling.” Along the way, he clashes with his swaggering father Charlemagne, his power-hungry stepmother Fastrada, his reckless brother Lewis, and his wise and humorous grandmother Berthe, and finds unexpected joy with young widow Catherine and her son Theo. A vibrant journey of purpose and temptation, Pippin is an exploration of what it really means to live an extraordinary life.
summary: Tisch New Theatre (TNT) is committed to developing technically and dramaturgically ambitious musical theatre works. At TNT, students are invited to work in all facets of the theatre, including performance, management, direction, production, and design.

name: Anesthesiology Interest Group
description:
A medical student group for those interested in the clinical, academic, and scientific aspects of anesthesiology at NYU Grossman School of Medicine.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: A medical student group for those interested in the clinical, academic, and scientific aspects of anesthesiology at the NYU Grossman School of Medicine.

name: Aftab: Rays of Light - All-University
description: Aftab is the literary magazine of the Islamic Center at New York University. This publication is meant to serve as a creative outlet for members of the community to express and exchange their thoughts and ideas about issues facing our community through poetry, prose, stories, essays, artwork, illustrations, photography, etc.
summary: Aftab is the literary magazine of the Islamic Center at New York University. This publication is meant to serve as a creative outlet for members of the community to express and exchange their thoughts and ideas about issues facing our community through...

name: Chess Club - All-University
description:
Welcome to NYU's one and only Chess Club! Look out for our weekly email reminders, and join our discord HERE.

Our meetings consist of both casual teaching and playing, including friendly competitions! Come develop your skills, meet and play fellow chess enthusiasts, and eat free pizza!

We have members of all skill levels and encourage all to join! We're a super chill, low-pressure environment (though, for the more skilled players, we can promise that you'll meet your match (or your better) here, too).

If you have any questions or ideas for future chess meetings, please let us know! Hope to see you there :)
summary:
Welcome to NYU's one and only Chess Club! Look out for our weekly email reminders, and join our discord here: https://discord.gg/NVrxrGZEpS

We have members of all skill levels and encourage all to join!

name: FOCUS (Ophthalmology Interest Group)
description:
FOCUS connects students interested in ophthalmology with each other as well as with residents and faculty in the department. We facilitate shadowing, research, and volunteer work related to ophthalmology. We provide exposure to research projects, chances to learn basic clinical skills, and opportunities to network with faculty. Past events included student research presentations, faculty subspecialist panels, and hands-on workshops. If you are at all interested in ophthalmology, come join us at our next event!

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: FOCUS connects students interested in ophthalmology with each other as well as with residents and faculty in the department. We facilitate shadowing, research, volunteer work, and hands-on workshops related to ophthalmology.

name: Undergraduate Stern Women in Business
description: USWIB strives to promote female and non-binary students' personal and professional development by providing networking and social interaction opportunities with students, faculty, administrators, alumni, and female leaders in business. By laying the foundation for a powerful network of female business leaders, USWIB creates a supportive community where students can share diverse experiences, perspectives, and resources. USWIB welcomes all NYU students.
summary: USWIB strives to promote female and non-binary students' personal and professional development by providing networking and social interaction opportunities with students, faculty, administrators, alumni, and female leaders in business.

name: WNYU Radio (89.1 FM)
description: WNYU’s mission is to provide our tri-state listening community, defined by our signal’s reach, with educational alternative radio programming. The FCC mandates that non-commercial college radio licensees must provide such an alternative. Alternative is relative to your market, including the commercial market: people listen to the radio, not exclusively commercial or non-commercial radio. So when we program the station, we consider the entire radio market and determine if programming of a similar nature exists within our listening community. We want to make programming sense by offering listening alternatives. If you hear it on another station, you probably won’t hear it on WNYU. WNYU is a 24 hour-a-day, 365 day-a-year student managed and staffed operation: we maintain and transmit a signal constantly. We are committed to addressing the needs and concerns of our immediate NYU student and village population through direct participation and interaction. We educate and entertain our community with unique music, news and student-produced shows exclusive to WNYU but with interest beyond the campus and the Village. WNYU encourages student participation from all schools within NYU. Drawing from this diverse environment, WNYU remains fresh and daring, uninhibited by commercial pressures. We don’t have to get a “ten-share” to stay on the air. That is not to say that we don’t have a professional attitude, but it is to say that we take full advantage of our non-commercial obligations and opportunities. We can program what other stations can’t even imagine.
summary: WNYU’s mission is to provide our tri-state listening community, defined by our signal’s reach, with educational alternative radio programming. The FCC mandates that non-commercial college radio licensees must provide such an alternative. Alternative is...

name: Student Producers Association
description:
The Student Producers Association (SPA) at Tisch School of the Arts brings together a diverse and talented group of students across NYU who share a common interest in film producing. SPA is committed to making its resources accessible to both experienced and inexperienced students for a variety of projects, ranging from narratives to animation to documentary. Join us at our weekly meetings, Tuesdays at 5pm.

Join our weekly Newsletter here!

Want to pitch at our upcoming meeting? Fill out our crew call form here!

Want to learn more about producing, from one-on-one time with upperclassmen? Interested in the different sectors of producing? Want to mentor students and see their producing skills grow? Sign-up for our peer mentorship program, either as a mentor or a mentee!
summary: People First. Every Story Matters.

name: Hellenic Heritage Association - All-University
description:
NYU Hellenic Heritage Association (HHA)

Since 1918, the Hellenic Heritage Association at NYU has been more than a student club — it is a tradition carried forward for over a century. From the glory of Athens to the endurance of Constantinople and the spirit of the modern diaspora, we embody a heritage that has shaped civilizations and continues to thrive today.

We welcome anyone who values history, heritage, and community. Through panels with diplomats and leaders in law, finance, and medicine, we connect our members to the wider world. Through taverna nights, parades, and cultural workshops, we celebrate the living traditions that keep us united.

Each spring, we march in the New York City Greek Independence Day Parade — standing not just as students, but as heirs to a story that transcends generations.

HHA is a home for those who want to belong to something lasting, to carry forward a legacy, and to leave their mark within the NYU community and beyond.
summary: Where Hellenic tradition meets the next generation of leaders.

name: First Performance (performance outlet for composers) - All-University
description: Concert, Music Composition, and Performance Workshop Club.
summary: Concert, Music Composition, and Performance Workshop Club.

name: Indian Cultural Club - All-University
description: Welcome to the NYU Indian Cultural Club, also known as NYU ICC! Our goal is to provide a unique, cultural support system for all students within the NYU community. Our organization is centered around collaboration, community development, and cultural education as we work together to raise awareness and support for the Indian community. Along with holding large cultural events such as Garba and Spring Bazaar where everyone has the opportunity to celebrate, eat delicious Indian food, and watch cultural performances, our organization also holds smaller, more personal events like chai chats where everyone has the opportunity to meet and get to know each other. We hope to see you soon!
summary: Welcome to the NYU Indian Cultural Club! Our goal is to provide a unique, cultural support system for all students within the NYU community. Along with large events like Garba and Spring Bazaar, we also hold smaller chats to meet new people!

name: Stern Student Council
description: NYU Stern Student Council acts as the official student governing body of Stern Undergraduate College. Our mission is to foster community at NYU Stern, provide an open forum for all of its constituents, and promote leadership.
summary: NYU Stern Student Council acts as the official student governing body of Stern Undergraduate College, whose purpose is to foster NYU and Stern community, provide an open forum for all of its constituents, and promote leadership.

name: The Gaming Collective @NYU
description:
Check us out on the TGC Discord server! We host online events and regularly post announcements for club activities, so don't miss out!

Bluesky

We are NYU's gaming club! Despite being based in Brooklyn, we invite any and all NYU members to come hang out and play in a variety of different games. Our mission is to create and foster a local gaming community for NYU students to both casually participate, compete in, and engage with. We organize a variety of online and offline events including tournaments, social hangouts, and casual game appreciation events. You are welcome to join no matter your skill level or favorite game. With over 850 club members, we are the largest student organization at Tandon! We encourage all those with a love or passion in games to join!

 

Here's a list of games that we currently have student run teams/organizations for:

- League of Legends

- Valorant

- Overwatch

- Counter-Strike: Global Offensive

- Super Smash Bros. Ultimate

- Super Smash Bros. Melee

- the Fighting Games Community

- Final Fantasy 14

- Magic the Gathering

- APEX: Legends

- Game Design/Development

- RTS Games

- Tetris

 

If your game is not on this list, please reach out to us! We are always looking for new people to help organize grassroots community events and support a growing NYU gaming scene
summary: We are NYU's gaming club! We play, discuss, and learn a variety of games ranging from League of Legends and Valorant to Hollow Knight and Getting Over It. If you want to play or develop, we will to! Join our Discord: https://discord.com/invite/FJNR452.

name: Graduate Indian Student Association
description: The main mission of our organization is to foster understanding about India and its culture among the NYU community at large, provide an avenue to bridge the social gap among the graduate students here at Tandon School of Engineering and the entire NYU, in order to enhance the diversity and to provide a helping hand to the new incoming students in getting oriented to the United States of America.
summary: The main mission of our organization is to foster understanding about India & its culture among the NYU community at large and provide an avenue to bridge the social gap among the graduate students here at Tandon School of Engineering and the entire NYU.

name: Coral Twr Res Hall-129 3rd Av
description: Coral is great community of students located in an exciting area of NYC. We are within a few blocks of Union Square, Gramercy Park and the East Village. Coral has approximately 400 students with a variety of smaller communities within. We offer low-cost living options as well as single and double spaces, and we also have two Explorations floors, Batter Up and Geek NYC. We have a staff of 13 resident assistants and Faculty Fellow in Residence (Bob Boland) who offer programming to get residents involved in the community and encourage them to explore the city. Our hall council, Coral All Student Assembly (CASA) also provides residents with an opportunity to make their mark on the Coral community.

Founded in 1950, SWE is the driving force that establishes engineering as a highly desirable career for women through an exciting array of training and development programs, networking opportunities, scholarships, outreach and advocacy activities.

name: Greenwich Hotel
description: Greenwich Residence Hall is situated in the attractive West Village area close to the Hudson River and Park. This residence hall houses approximately 320 residents, has seven Resident Assistants, and is home to the "NYC in the 60's" Explorations Floor. The community is enriched by an eclectic mix of residents with varied backgrounds, academic programs, ideas and interests. The student population is comprised primarily of returning undergraduate sophomores, transfer and international students, though some undergraduate juniors and seniors also choose to live in Greenwich Hall. The building is an air-conditioned apartment-style residence hall with bath, kitchen, and dining area in each unit. Among our facilities we have a newly renovated lower lounge with a study room, practice rooms, dance room, pool table, and television. Check out our blog, follow us on twitter, and like us on Facebook to learn more about Greenwich Hall!

description: Russian Club at NYU welcomes Eastern European descendants, Russian speakers of all levels, and everyone interested in the region's culture and politics. We are committed to creating an open environment that fosters community and brings home a little bit closer. In the 2023/24 academic year, we have organized tea parties, board game nights, and traditional holiday celebrations. Feel free to come by any of our meetings and events!

name: American Medical Women's Association
description:
The American Medical Women's Association is an organization which functions at the local, national, and international level to advance women in medicine and improve women's health. We achieve this by providing and developing leadership, advocacy, education, expertise, mentoring, and strategic alliances. At the medical school level, we sponsor mentoring luncheons and dinners on topics in women's health. We also try to build a sense of community among women in medicine at NYU by providing a supportive atmosphere. NOTE: This club is for students enrolled at NYU Grossman School of Medicine only. 

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.

description:
The mission of the Global Medicine Club is to contribute to the improvement of health worldwide through the interests and activities of students at the NYU School of Medicine. Specific goals of the club are to promote awareness and education on issues pertaining to international health through lectures and conferences; to engage students in service activities that benefit global health both locally and abroad; and to facilitate access to international health opportunities for summer and elective periods.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.

name: Founders Hall
description: Welcome to Founders Hall, NYU’s newest residence hall and home to over 700 first-year students, located in the Union Square/East Village area of NYC! Built on the site of the former St. Ann’s church, the historic façade and fence have been preserved as part of the front courtyard. To help facilitate the community, enhance your academic experience, plan events, and provide support, there are 25 Resident Assistants, one Resource Center Assistant, three student affairs professionals (Ashley, Paula, and Jerry), and one Faculty-Fellow in Residence (Crystal Parikh) who lives in the building along with her family. In addition, Founders has a Writing Affiliate that helps first year students learn and understand how to succeed in writing their first college level papers while at NYU. With 26 floors, Founders features a large outdoor courtyard, a community garden, two TV lounges, one study lounge with computers, wi-fi access throughout the lobby and lounges, recycling/trash rooms on every floor, a laundry room, as well as energy and sustainability focused features. Throughout the year there will be many chances to get involved, especially with the Founders Advisory Board (FAB), our very own hall government, where you can run for an elected position or serve on the general council. Check out our blog to learn more about Founders!

name: Headless Society, The - All-University
description:
The Headless Society is a club founded on the moment of inspiration. We are about letting loose and writing without constraints. Original prompts run by a different member each week spark the creative process. We write for about 20 minutes and members are encouraged to share their work at the end of the meeting. The Headless Society combats writer's block in a creative environment that is not preoccupied with valuation. Here, the process is the product. The Headless Society is a passionate community that publishes a literary magazine each semester, entitled The Guillotine. Members that wish to may showcase their un-edited writings from meetings in this magazine. While the products remain unchanged, prepared to be surprised by what you can write in 20 minutes!

Follow us on Instagram @headless.society or visit theheadlesssocietynyu.wordpress.com for updates!

Submit to the Guillotine with this link: 

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScMVmDgHGHuRvyco0poBCi90xZTAyPRGWuPHlcX2MDAzPV3mw/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=110587785912429506066

description: GlamourGals is a nonprofit organization whose mission is to inspire and organize youngsters to provide ongoing complimentary beauty makeovers and companionship to elderly women living in senior homes. The purpose of this club is not only to make the nursing home residents feel more beautiful and alive, but also to bridge the generation gap between the younger and the older generation. Through conversations and makeovers, beauty, gratification and kindness are understood in terms of their similitude across generations. Visit http://glamourgals.org/ for more information of the overall organization. Join our GroupMe for updates on events & visits! https://groupme.com/ join_group/70771893/kpKdCDkv.

name: Nu Alpha Phi Fraternity, Inc. - All-University
description: The fraternity professes to create a bond of brotherhood passed down from generation to generation, including lasting relationships between alumni and currently active members. Externally, it seeks to promote Asian-awareness, as well as to serve as a liaison between the Greek community and the Asian community. This is done through a supportive network of alumni and matriculated brothers who actively engage in participation in community service, cultural workshops and events, university programs, and various other on-campus student activities. Additionally, the fraternity strives to work with various other Asian Greek organizations in an effort to bring unity and recognition to the Asian-American community. Internally, the fraternity emphasizes brotherhood and promotion of self-development for all of its members, as encouraged by its seven fundamental principles of brotherhood, discipline, leadership, pride, professionalism, service, and unity. Members are taught to use these principles in everyday life to promote personal growth. These ideals are brought upon prospective members, as well. Additionally, the fraternity promotes brotherhood through events such as alumni networking, job banks, sports competitions, and road trips. The fraternity actively seeks to integrate its members into the local community through social events, on-campus performances, and participation in various cultural organizations. Although the fraternity asserts itself as an Asian-interest organization, it openly welcomes members from any ethnicity or cultural background, and its membership has continued to increase in diversity over the years.
summary: The fraternity professes to create a bond of brotherhood passed down from generation to generation, including lasting relationships between alumni and currently active members.Externally, it seeks to promote Asian-awareness, as well as to serve as a li...

name: Asian Cultural Union - All-University
description:
What is ACU?
 
Established in 1972, Asian Cultural Union remains the only Asian umbrella group at NYU and is one of the largest and most prominent organizations in the history of the university. Our mission is to celebrate and promote Asian cultures through cultural, social, and business-oriented events, as well as community service projects.
 
Why should I join?
 
Because of our diverse membership, ACU seeks to promote the learning and understanding of individual heritages, while simultaneously creating unity among students of all ethnic backgrounds. Through networking with professionals and various organizations, ACU provides its members with extensive resources and connections that transcend the NYU community. ACU presents students with a place to voice opinions, develop a sense of community, and to explore ideas that are highly relevant to an increasingly diverse world.
 
Who can join?
 
ACU is an all-inclusive organization that welcomes members of all backgrounds. YOU DO NOT HAVE TO BE ASIAN TO JOIN! ACU was founded upon and continues to operate under the precepts of racial and social tolerance, indiscrimination, and equality.
summary: Established in 1972, Asian Cultural Union remains the largest Asian umbrella organization at NYU. Our mission is to celebrate and promote Asian cultures through cultural and social events. ACU is an all-inclusive organization that welcomes all members.

name: Comic Book League - All-University
description:
What is the NYU Comic Book League? We are NYU’s bridge to the world of comics, manga, and other media, providing an environment where you can meet like-minded individuals dedicated to discussing, reading and creating comic books. In addition to our weekly meetings, we host comics related activities like convention trips, cartoon movie nights, and most importantly, produce a bi-annual publication featuring your work!

Meetings for Fall 2025 are every other week on Fridays from 6-7 PM in the Kimmel Center; more details on meetings are usually sent through email announcements a few days before each event!

New to the club? Follow our Instagram (@comicbookleague) for updates on meetings, contests, and events! Feel free to join our Discord to meet new people or to ask questions about the club more casually!
summary: What is the NYU Comic Book League? We are NYU’s bridge to the world of comic books and graphic novels, providing an environment where you can meet like-minded individuals dedicated to discussing, reading and creating comic books. In addition to our wee..
