name: SPS Events Committee
description:
Get Involved! Join the SPS Events Committee!

Are you the person to make all the plans when going out with friends?

Have you ever wanted to be an event planner?

Do you like organizing and decorating parties and fundraisers? 

Do you have absolutely no experience in planning, but still want to get involved????


COME JOIN US every Thursday at 12.00pm!

No matter your academic interests,  the Student Council Event Committee might be right for you! We plan events for the entire SPS community that range from charity runs to huge parties to sports outings.

The Student Council Event Committee Members are responsible for: 

• Attending weekly, or as-needed meetings of the Committee. All meeting information can be found on the NYU Engage Portal under SPS Events Committee

• Assisting in designing, developing, and producing of all events appointed to the Committee by the Council 

• Promoting the Committee’s events amongst colleagues and peers through word of mouth 

• Communicating with the Event Committee Chair to remain knowledgeable about the events and programs of the Committee 

• Aiding the Chair with tasks as he/she deems necessary 

• Producing SPS school wide events that are to the satisfaction of the attending student body.

If you are interested in being involved fill out the following form.

We look forward to seeing you at our meetings. Please feel free to contact us for any further information at the NYU Engage portal Or e-mail us at sps.studentlifeevents@nyu.edu.
summary: The Events Committee plans the school’s major events and traditions such as Spirit Week, Global Village and end-of- semester parties such as Winter Ball and the Spring Dinner Cruise.

name: Organization of Black Women - All-University
description: The Organization of Black Women's mission is to empower Black women at NYU. We seek to create a community of socio-politically aware, self-actualized members. We serve as facilitators of real dialogue - through the intersection of race and gender and the unique perspectives of our members. We know that knowledge is power! Aside from our general meetings, OBW crafts various entertainment events, invaluable networking opportunities, community service projects and outings. Please email organizationofblackwomen@gmail.com for inquiries.

name: Indonesian Students Association - All-University
description:
Indonesian Students Association at New York University (henceforth shall be referred to as ISA or NYU Indonesia)

 

NYU Indonesia shall serve as a focal point for all students, as well as other community members of the New York University who are interested in the culture and general affairs of Indonesia. With the objectives of fostering a solid Indonesian community and promulgating the presence of Indonesian culture throughout New York University, ISA shall herein organize activities that are geared toward fulfilling these objectives. ISA shall act as the primary contact and point of reference for the New York University community and external organizations.

For 2025/2026, NYU Indonesia has made an organization statement:

 
"Put Indonesia on the Map!":
1. Showcase Indonesia to the world
2. Foster a caring and inclusive community
3. Empower students for the future
 
We are currently not only focused on developing our internal members but also committed to expanding our network and making significant contributions to the community both in NYC and Indonesia.

We aim to explore our current potential and intend to provide programs that can enhance our members' capacities as preparation for their professional careers.
summary: Indonesian Students Association at NYU (ISA or NYU Indonesia) shall serve as a focal point for all students, as well as other community members of the New York University who are interested in the culture and general affairs of Indonesia.

name: Health Policy and Advocacy Interest Group
description: With rapid changes in national and global health policy, debates over access, equity, and healthcare reform affect the patients we are already caring for as medical students. As future physicians, we will have a unique opportunity to help our patients navigate policy decisions that directly impact clinical practice, patient outcomes, and community well-being. This proposed Interest Group will help students explore these challenges and learn how to translate our knowledge into effective advocacy. Our mission is to educate students about the policy forces that shape healthcare, develop practical advocacy skills, and provide experiences that turn learning into impact. Through seminars, journal clubs, workshops, and collaborations with faculty, policymakers, and community partners, we will examine issues from structural determinants of health to emerging health system reforms. Hands-on opportunities such as legislative outreach, public comment writing, and advocacy campaigns will empower students to use their voices and help shape the future of health policy. The group will foster an inclusive community where students can learn, collaborate, and grow together while advancing evidence-based, patient-centered, and equitable health policy. We aim to cultivate the next generation of physician leaders who not only deliver excellent clinical care but also actively engage in improving the systems that influence it.
summary: With rapid changes in national and global health policy, debates over access, equity, and healthcare reform affect the patients we are already caring for as medical students.

NYU Medical Student Advocates (MSA) is a student organization in which medical students partner with clinics at Bellevue, such as the Program for Survivors of Torture (PSOT), to advance patient care within each program. Through partnering with patients, students will assist them with navigating the medical, mental, social, and legal aspects of their care, as well as learn about patients and their experiences. 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.

description:
Spin Doctors is NYU SOM's dance interest group. Our goal is to teach you how to dance in a variety of styles, such as hip hop, salsa, contemporary, etc. We have outings to established NYC studios taught by experienced professionals to make sure you learn the steps in a fun and useful way. Additionally, we provide opportunities for students to appreciate dance as an art form through attending local performances. Dancers of ALL levels are welcome, especially beginners!

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.

name: Rubin Hall, 35 5th Ave
description: Located at a swanky address on 5th Avenue, Rubin is a bustling community comprised of approximately 680 first-year students. Rubin is part of the FYRE (First Year Residential Experience) and is served by 13 Resident Assistants (RA), a Resource Center Assistant (RCA), a Faculty-Fellow in Residence, Michael Dinwiddie and a Writing Affiliate, Mike Tyrell. The staff provides a plethora of programming, so you will find plenty to do in Rubin at NYU and in NYC! Rubin is home to the following Explorations communities: Human Rights and International Relations, Spanish, French, and Fashion as well as a Choices (substance-free) community. We have several great amenities for residents to take advantage of including a large air conditioned second floor lounge, a black box theatre, a study lounge, a dance room, a piano room, a ping pong room, and a billiards room. Rubin also has a water bottle filling station on the second floor which provides filtered and cool water that helps keep our residents hydrated. At Rubin, we have a tradition of having the most spirited hall government, which we affectionately refer to as SUG@R (Student Undergraduate Government At Rubin). We encourage everyone to get involved in the Rubin community, whether that means running for President of SUG@R, being a floor representative, or attending our fun programs.

name: Goddard Hall / 45 West 4th St.
description: Goddard Hall is the University's only Residential College for first-year students, located right on Washington Square Park - steps away from three different dining halls, Bobst Library, most classrooms, and the Kimmel Student Center. We're at the crossroads of NYC's liveliest neighborhoods: Greenwich Village, SoHo, the East Village, and the Lower East Side! The Residential College at Goddard is organized around the vital theme of citizen engagement and our students affiliate with one of six themed "streams," which range from social justice issues to "Poverty & Affluence" to affinity-based streams devoted to "Writing New York" or "All the World's a Stage." Find out more about the residential college here. Six amazing Resident Assistants and Goddard’s “Faculty-Fellow-in-Residence” Carley Moore are visible and active members of the Goddard Residential College community. Participating in the Residential College at Goddard will provide first-year students a terrific home to establish a solid footing for a successful NYU student career. Check out our blog to learn more about Goddard!

Hayden Hall, located right on Washington Square, is a dynamic community of almost 700 first-year students from all of NYU's schools. Within the building we have 15 Resident Assistants here to help you connect to NYU, your classmates, and many of the great experiences this university and city have to offer. Hayden is also home to two Faculty Fellows in Residence (Michael Dinwiddie with partner Vincent Parham, and John Moran with partner Kennith French) who live in the hall and plan programs and events to meet and regularly engage with residents. We have four Explorations communities within Hayden this year: the NYU Show, The French House, The Spanish Floor, and Artists in Development. Each Explorations community has its own Faculty Affiliate who works closely with the floor. As for some of our amenities, Hayden has wi-fi throughout, 2 common room lounges, and 2 study rooms. Additionally, the cellar contains a laundry room, a dance practice/exercise room, a music room with piano, and a game room with pool, foosball, and air hockey. name: SPS Community Service Committee
description:
Do you enjoy donating your time and giving back to the community?

Do you like organizing and planning fundraisers for different causes?

 

No matter your academic interests, the Student Council’s Community Service committee might be right for you! We help in planning community service events, fundraisers and activities for the entire SPS community to help benefit the local community. Not only do we help in creating or arranging these events, but we also work together to promote them on a school wide level with the Council. In the Community Service Committee, there is something here for everyone to be a part of and enjoy as we all give back together!

 

Student Council Community Service Committee Members are responsible for:

• Attending bi-weekly, weekly, or as-needed meetings of the Committee

• Assisting in brainstorming, developing, and producing of all fundraising and community service related events appointed to the Committee by the Council

• Promoting these Committee’s events, fundraisers or benefits amongst colleagues and peers through word of mouth and social media

• Communicating with the Community Service Committee Chair to remain knowledgeable about the ongoing events and programs of the Committee

• Aiding the Chairs with tasks as he/she deems necessary

• Producing SPS community service events, fundraisers or activities that are to the satisfaction of the attending student body.
summary: This committee is open to all SPS Students interested in Community Service activities.

name: Humanistic Medicine Program
description:
The Master Scholars Program in Humanistic Medicine at NYU Grossman School of Medicine promotes inquiry and scholarship at the intersection of medicine and the arts, humanities, and social sciences. We enable students to pursue creative and intellectual interests beyond the scope of the traditional medical school curriculum.

Our program fosters well-rounded students, who have the ability to develop insight into the human condition and become engaged and reflective physicians. It consists of noncredit interdisciplinary seminars, called “mini-courses”; a research fellowship in medical ethics and humanities; Agora, a student arts and literature magazine; and events on humanistic medicine topics.

Students take part in the program while fulfilling their other medical school commitments. Several activities are open to all members of the NYU community, allowing for interprofessional collaboration.
summary: The Master Scholars Program in Humanistic Medicine at NYU Grossman School of Medicine promotes inquiry and scholarship at the intersection of medicine and the arts, humanities, and social sciences.

We desire to engage students in the good news of Jesus Christ, and to provide a loving community where people can grow in their faith or safely explore Christianity. AACF is a chapter of InterVarsity Christian Fellowship USA, and we aim to see students and faculty transformed, campuses renewed, and world changers developed.

name: Project Sunshine at NYU - All-University
description:
Project Sunshine is an international nonprofit organization that delivers the healing power of play to children with medical needs through trained and dedicated volunteers.

For over 20 years, Project Sunshine volunteers have brought joy and play directly to pediatric patients through in-person programming with our network of hospitals and medical facility partners across the country.

At NYU, we participate in Kit for Play (Sending Sunshine through assembly of activity/craft kits), Here to Play (in-person volunteering at hospitals), and Teleplay (virtual technology to bring play and activities to children remotely).

Please refer to our "Documents" section to understand the steps it takes to become trained as a volunteer.
summary: Project Sunshine is a nonprofit organization that provides free educational, recreational, and social programs to children facing medical challenges and their families. At NYU, we do direct service, volunteer programming with children at local hospitals.

name: Student Collective for Ear, Nose and Throat
description:
Otolaryngology is a specialty that is often underemphasized in medical school. The field varies from treating head and neck cancer to cochlear transplants to facialoplastics surgery. The mission of Student Collective for Ear, Nose, and Throat (SCENT) is to introduce medical students to the vast and dynamic specialty of otolaryngology. Through lectures, mentoring programs, resident panels, and other methods of student/faculty interaction, SCENT aims to facilitate student exploration of otolaryngology. Additional Resources: Helpful book for understanding the basics of otolaryngology: "Primary Care Otolaryngology."

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: SCENT is the ENT Interest Group at NYUGSOM. Our goal is to introduce medical students to the field of ENT through faculty panels, lectures, and workshops.

name: Global Programs New York City
description: GPNYC (Global Programs New York City) is the team that supports NYUAD and NYUSH students during study away in New York. This organization is for NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU Shanghai students studying away in New York.
summary: GPNYC is a collaborative effort between NYUAD and NYUSH administrators to provide support and guidance for students studying away in New York City. We will be hosting various programs as well as collaborating with student organizations and the Office o...

name: Ski & Snowboard Club - All-University
description: This is a club for ski & snowboard lovers. We are a chill group of people that go on weekend trips as well as a longer Winter and Spring Break trips. It's a great place to make friends; all levels are welcome and encouraged! See you on the slopes!
summary: This is a club for ski & snowboard lovers. We are a chill group of people that go on weekend trips as well as a longer Winter and Spring Break trips. It's a great place to make friends; all levels are welcome and encouraged! See you on the slopes!

name: NYU Harm Reduction Alliance
description:
The goal of the NYU Harm Reduction Alliance is to promote harm reduction in our community. We provide Naloxone training to students and, COVID-permitting, to the 30th Street Men's Homeless Shelter in partnership with the Department of Homeless Services. We are currently developing an exciting new program in partnership with Bellevue in which medical students would provide motivational interviewing and safe needle practice education to Bellevue inpatients.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The purpose of the NYU Harm Reduction Alliance is to promote harm reduction in our community through medical student volunteering to provide Naloxone training and sterile syringe programs.

name: Tachycardia Interest Group
description:
The NYU SoM Running Club hopes to bring together medical students who enjoy running--whether in competitive races or just for thirty minutes every now and then along the East River--in a fun, social environment. As a group, we would like to participate in health-related charitable races in New York and raise awareness about preventing running-related injuries in our concrete-filled home through occasional dinner meetings with NYU physicians of sports medicine. We also plan on scheduling weekly runs and hope that members of the group will contact each other when they are planning on their own runs and looking for company. In essence, we believe running is a fun and healthy way to deal with the physical and mental stresses of medicine and hope to bring together our peers who share this sentiment.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The NYU SoM Running Club hopes to bring together medical students who enjoy running--whether in competitive races or just for thirty minutes every now and then along the East River--in a fun, social environment. As a group, we would like to participate...

name: Jewish Student Union
description: The Jewish Student Union (JSU) serves to educate the NYU Tandon community about Jewish culture and religion, as well as provide a space for Jewish students to come together and support each other on campus.
summary: The Jewish Student Union (JSU) serves to educate the NYU Tandon community about Jewish culture and religion, as well as provide a space for Jewish students to come together and support each other on campus.

name: NYU Reaching Out
description:
The NYU: Reaching Out Program serves to promote positive personal development and create emerging young leaders that are ready to tackle the problems of their worlds with an open mind and an open heart.
 
At NYU: Reaching Out, we collaborate with inner-city high school students and provide a safe space to explore challenges and also a peek into the college experience. Our organization’s goals are to help our participants develop leadership skills to become advocates of their dreams as empowered, college-bound young women.

Our bimonthly workshops feature activities surrounding college readiness and identity empowerment. We cultivate a safe, productive and trusting environment that embodies the support and care needed to explore any challenges hindering the realization of our potential. We take care of each other. Mentors and mentees alike help lift each other up towards our goals as self-advocating students.

Everyone can benefit from care and support to reach their potential. NYU: Reaching Out encourages the realization of that potential, through camaraderie, resources, assistance, and care.
summary: The NYU: Reaching Out Program serves to promote positive personal development and create emerging young leaders that are ready to tackle the problems of their worlds with an open mind and an open heart. At NYU: Reaching Out, we collaborate with inner-c...

name: Soon Movement - All-University
description: We are an evangelistic Christian ministry on campus that holds weekly large groups. Meetings include fellowship, prayer, and biblical discussions. We welcome anyone interested in getting to know more about Christianity and our purpose.
summary: We are an evangelistic Christian ministry on campus that holds weekly large groups. Meetings include fellowship, prayer, and biblical discussions. We welcome anyone interested in getting to know more about Christianity and our purpose.

name: oSTEM
description: The purpose of oSTEM at NYU is to foster a campus and community environment that is safe, supportive, and informative to all students regardless of physical gender, perceived gender, sexual orientation, or any other physical or physiological attribute; As well as challenge negative stigmas against the LGBTQ+ community. In addition, oSTEM at NYU will offer support services and information to the NYU student body, specifically those members who identify as LGBTQ+. The club will focus on providing information about LGBTQ+ issues in STEM related fields as well as to the general public.
summary: The purpose of oSTEM at NYU is to foster a campus and community environment that is safe, supportive, and informative to all students regardless of physical gender, perceived gender, sexual orientation, or any other physical or physiological attribute;

name: Plastic Surgery Interest Group
description:
The Plastic Surgery Club will serve as a forum for common interest and as a starting point for student involvement in plastic surgery. As such, the club aims to explore the numerous surgical and reconstructive aspects of plastic surgery through hosting a series of dinner lectures, providing students with a better understanding of the diversity of niches occupied by plastic surgeons, and presenting the latest developments in surgical research.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Plastic Surgery Club will serve as a forum for common interest and as a starting point for student involvement in plastic surgery. As such, the club aims to explore the numerous surgical and reconstructive aspects of plastic surgery through hosting...

name: SPS Undergraduate Student Council
description: The Undergraduate Student Council (USC) is the School's student governance organization for undergraduate students. The mission of the USC is to promote and stimulate school spirit and community. The USC organizes school-wide activities and events, which bring together the student body for networking opportunities, career seminars, and school club events. The Council also serves as a resource for students on School and University services and procedures. General meetings are open to all undergraduate students.
summary: The Undergraduate Student Council (USC) represents all the undergraduate students at SPS in matters affecting student life and student affairs. The USC mission promotes community by bringing students together for social gatherings and community service.

name: Student Interest Group in Neurology
description:
The purpose of the Student Interest Group in Neurology (SIGN) is to provide opportunities to explore the field of neurology through seminars, journal clubs, and workshops.

Dr. Jonathan Howard, MD, Faculty Advisor

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The purpose of the Student Interest Group in Neurology (SIGN) is to provide opportunities to explore the field of neurology through seminars, journal clubs, and workshops.

name: NYUGSOM Military and Veteran Association
description:
Our mission will be to connect members of the NYU Grossman School of Medicine community - including both M.D. Students as well as faculty - who have served or are currently serving in the U.S. Armed Forces in order to provide social support, academic guidance, and professional mentorship. Further, we will work with the Department of Admissions to conduct organized outreach for future accepted students with prior experience of military service.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Our mission will be to connect members of the NYU Grossman School of Medicine community.

name: Women of Excellence, Strength, and Tenacity - All-University
description: We, the Womxn of Excellence, Strength, and Tenacity, are a sisterhood dedicated to upholding the principles of leadership and service. We seek to provide a sense of community both within and outside NYU. We recognize our individual strengths while honoring the power of sisterhood. Striving to embody these principles, we hope to inspire our community while empowering ourselves and the women around us.
summary: We, the Womxn of Excellence, Strength, and Tenacity, are a sisterhood dedicated to upholding the principles of leadership and service. We seek to provide a sense of community both within and outside NYU. We recognize our individual strengths while hono...

name: Department of Athletics, Intramurals and Recreation
description:
The Department for Athletics, Intramurals, and Recreation enhances the NYU experience through programs and facilities designed to inspire active and healthy lifestyles. Our NCAA Division III varsity teams, club sports, intramurals, and recreational programs complement each other by meeting the needs of diverse students and contribute to the development of such intangible attributes as leadership, initiative, and a passion for working cooperatively toward a goal. 

We pursue daily, intentional actions to continue to improve on all levels from varsity athletics, clubs, intramurals and recreation to customer service, operations, and student wellness and success.

We nurture thriving groups connected by shared interests reflecting the diversity of NYU and New York City. Beyond the physical benefits of sport and fitness, we seek to stimulate the intellect of our students and staff by awakening in them an awareness of social issues that inspires them toward action to create a more just, equitable world. 

We empower students to take leadership roles to instill in them such qualities as confidence, risk management, and critical decision making that will serve them throughout their lives in any endeavor they choose to pursue.
summary: NYU Athletics fosters an important and integral part of the NYU experience. We work with student groups, faculty, and community partners to create a campus environment in which students can flourish intellectually athletically, and personally.

name: Obstetrics and Gynecology Interest Group
description:
This club will serve as a forum through which medical students can gain further insight into OB-GYN and its subspecialties. Information will be presented in the form of lectures, workshops, and panel discussions. Our goal is to give pre-clinical students exposure to the field of OB-GYN and provide those who are interested with opportunities for mentorship. In addition, we seek to help students better understand and prepare for careers in OB-GYN. The OB-GYN interest group will teach relevant clinical skills, raise awareness about issues related to women's health, and serve as the liaison between OB-GYN faculty and NYUSOM medical students.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: This club will serve as a forum through which medical students can gain further insight into OB-GYN and its subspecialties. Information will be presented in the form of lectures, workshops, and panel discussions.

name: 80 Lafayette St Residence Hall
description: The 80 Lafayette Street Residence Hall is located in Tribeca and is in close proximity to the culturally rich neighborhoods of City Hall, Chinatown and Little Italy. Lafayette opened in Fall 1999, and houses nearly 1100 upperclass students. Lafayette is home to 32 Resident Assistants and two Faculty Fellows in Residence, Olivier Berthe (CAS) and his family as well as Aisha Khan (CAS). Lafayette also hosts the Fraternity and Sorority Life Community, housing 12 fraternities and sororities on the upper floors of the building, and the Rainbow Roster explorations community focusing on LGBTQ culture in the city. Visit the Lafayette Tumblr page to learn more about this vibrant community!
summary: The 80 Lafayette Street Residence Hall is located in Tribeca and is in close proximity to the culturally rich neighborhoods of City Hall, Chinatown and Little Italy. Lafayette opened in Fall 1999, and houses nearly 1100 upperclass students. Lafayette i...

name: NYU Project OutReach
description:
Project OutReach helps new undergraduate students explore the Social Change Model of Leadership Development while connecting with New York City's diverse community service scene. Project OutReach alumni who are also current undergraduate students at NYU, share their extensive leadership experience as small group facilitators for this program.

Project OutReach is held immediately prior to Welcome Week and consists of workshops, fun activities and a variety of community service projects. Previous Project OutReach projects include delivering hot meals in the West Village, hosting a carnival for the families of children with serious medical conditions, and cleaning and painting a city park.
summary: Project OutReach helps new undergraduate students explore the Social Change Model of Leadership Development while connecting with New York City's diverse community service scene. Project OutReach alumni who are also current undergraduate students at NY...

name: Tandon Muslim Students Association
description: The Tandon Muslim Students Association provides NYU Tandon students the opportunity to come together at the Brooklyn campus in an Islamic environment with the goal of learning about Islam, its faith, and practices. All students and community members are welcome to Tandon MSA activities and events. Regular activities organized by Tandon MSA will be held at the Brooklyn campus, which include daily prayers at the designated Tandon MSA Prayer Room located at 2 Metro Tech Room 806, weekly Jummah, social activities, and academic lecture events throughout the semester.
summary: Tandon MSA provides Tandon students the opportunity to come together at the Brooklyn campus in an Islamic environment with the goal of learning about Islam, its faith, and practices.

name: Armenian Students Association at NYU - All-University
description:
We are the Armenian Students Association at NYU, your place for everything Armenian. Join our monthly gatherings to learn more about the culture, traditions, watch Armenian movies, get free food and prizes!

Most importantly, feel part of the Armenian community at NYU by meeting Armenians and others interested in the country and its culture. We can’t wait to have you join us!

Make sure to also follow us on Instagram @asa.nyu for news and updates!
summary: The Armenian Students Association at NYU

name: United Sikh Association at NYU - All-University
description: The United Sikh Association at New York University is a student-run religious and cultural organization with a mission to encourage an on-campus community that spreads awareness of the Sikh religion and inspires others to engage in community service. USA at NYU aims to achieve these goals through religious, cultural, and social activities open to the entire NYU community. With the support of over 40 members, USA at NYU strives to achieve this mission by living closely to the principles of Sikhism, the religion which the organization represents. These principles are those of Seva, or self-less service, Sangat, or community and spiritual support, and Simran, or remembrance of God at all times. These three principles and ideals of Sikhism are incorporated throughout USA at NYU’s endeavors.
summary: The United Sikh Association at New York University is a student-run religious and cultural organization with a mission to encourage an on-campus community that spreads awareness of the Sikh religion and inspires others to engage in community service.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description: To increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers, who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.
summary: To increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers, who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.

name: Gallatin Business Society
description: The Gallatin Business Society (formerly the Gallatin Business Club) serves members by offering resources for education in business and career development, events for networking with peers and professionals, and bringing the business endeavors of students to fruition. Our club caters to all students interested in business and entrepreneurship - from fashion business to finance. For more info like us on instagram @GallatinBusinessSociety or email us at gbsnyu@gmail.com.
summary: The Gallatin Business Society (formerly the Gallatin Business Club) serves members by offering resources for education in business and career development, events for networking with peers and professionals, and bringing the business endeavors of studen...

name: SPS Graduate Student Council
description:
The mission of this governing organization shall be to represent all NYU SPS graduate students to the NYU Administration, build a school community, develop programs focused on improving student life, and oversee and fund all graduate student clubs and organizations within NYU SPS.

 

As a governing organization, the NYU SPS GSC looks:

1. To serve as a representative voice for the NYU SPS graduate student body by maintaining open lines of communication between students, GSC advisor, NYU SPS administration, faculty (includes full-time and adjunct), and the NYU Student Government on school-wide issues

2. To offer school-wide networking opportunities for students seeking to enrich their professional careers and expand their network

3. To provide school-wide professional, community service and social events to build a strong and well-connected student community

4. To fairly distribute funds to NYU SPS graduate student clubs and organizations and oversee that all matters comply with university and legal regulations

 

Weekly Committee Meetings Schedule for Fall 2025:

1. Recreation Committee (Mondays 12 pm - 1 pm)

2. Events Committee (Mondays 3:30 pm - 4:30 pm)

3. Marketing Committee (Wednesdays 3:30 pm - 4:30 pm)

4. Community Service Committee (Thursdays 1:30 pm - 2:30 pm)

 

G-Chat Group for Announcements:

Join here: https://chat.google.com/room/AAQACX6YxZw?cls=7
summary: The Graduate Student Council (GSC) represents all the graduate students at SPS in matters affecting student life and  student affairs. The GSC mission builds school community by developing programs that focus on improving student life.

name: Imbroglio Improvisation Club - All-University
description:
Home Improvment (formerly known as Imbroglio...ish) is NYU's most premier improv comedy team. We are dedicated to bringing laughs to the NYU community and beyond!

With a core team of 10 goofballs and a full community of hilarious students, we come together to do shows, improv jams, play games, and laugh!
summary:
Home Improvment (formerly known as Imbroglio...ish) is NYU's most premier improv comedy team. We are dedicated to bringing laughs to the NYU community and beyond!

Keep an eye out this fall semester for audition info!

name: Student Leadership and Policy Forum - All-University
description: The Student Leadership and Policy Forum @ NYU is student-run think tank that participates in policy-writing and leadership workshops, and develops student-led local activism projects. Our chapter attracts diverse members from eight undergraduate schools in order to write public policy for possible publication in national journals, participate in policy-writing and leadership workshops, create student-led local activism projects, and engage in thoughtful political discussions in an open-minded and comfortable setting.
summary: The Student Leadership and Policy Forum @ NYU is student-run think tank that participates in policy-writing and leadership workshops, and develops student-led local activism projects.

description: Founded in 2007, we are a student association at New York University for both Brazilians and those who appreciate Brazil. We have three driving principles: to show the Brazilian culture to our NYU community, to create a tight-knit community of people with a shared interest in Brazil, and to create connections for our members in the Brazilian world outside NYU.

An organization that aims to promote oncology and all specialties related to it from a clinical and research perspective.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: An organization that aims to promote oncology and all specialties related to it from a clinical and research perspective.

name: La Herencia Latina - All-University
description: La Herencia Latina, the Latino Heritage Month Planning Committee at New York University, was established in 1990 by a group of students from different clubs and organizations, who saw the need to create a one-month celebration dedicated to the Latino Culture and experiences. The committee's task is to coordinate and host several programs, events, panel discussions, lectures, concerts and cultural activities during the month of November, the official month in which New York University recognizes Latino heritage and its international impact. The pupose is to teach the NYU community all about Latino history and culture. We learn, but at the same time celebrate in union our past, present and look forward to the future.

description:
The NYUGSOM Surgery Club is dedicated to inspiring the next generation of surgeons by fostering mentorship, encouraging clinical exposure, and identifying research opportunities for medical students. 

Officer: Zander Harpel 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The NYUGSOM Surgery Club is dedicated to inspiring the next generation of surgeons by fostering mentorship, encouraging clinical exposure, and identifying research opportunities for medical students.

name: Taiwanese Student Association - All-University
description: The Taiwanese Students Association at New York University is a non-profit and non-political student organization which focuses on enriching the college experience of all our Taiwanese International students. Our goal is mainly to represent the Taiwanese community and strengthen its voice on and beyond the campus. Fostering those who have common interest in the celebration of Taiwanese culture, we hope to spread this cultural awareness to the entire student body and reinforce our unification in the American society. The NYU TSA is the largest and most active Taiwanese student organization in the US East Coast. All Taiwanese students enrolled in NYU classes are automatically granted TSA membership. Each year, the NYU TSA hosts a variety of social, artistic, and cultural events – each of which contributes to its main goals: gathering and enhancing relationships among students and promoting the learning and awareness of Taiwanese culture throughout New York University and other surrounding communities All of our members on the NYU TSA board will strive to provide our fellow Taiwanese students with a socially-enriched year. We are more than delighted to welcome all of the Taiwanese students to join our organization and participate in our group activities. Most importantly, we like to hear from all of our members voice their ideas, opinions, and concerns about the organization, in order for us to better fulfill your experience at New York University.
summary: The Taiwanese Students Association at New York University , is a non-profit and non-political student organization, which focuses on enriching the college experience of all our Taiwanese International students. Our goal is mainly to represent the Taiwa...

name: Headspace Hub (Psychiatry Interest Group)
description:
Psychiatry Interest Group hopes to expose the many different sides of psychiatry to students and allow them a greater understanding of what it means to become a part of this field. Through interesting lectures, workshops, and discussions, this club hopes to show off all the many aspects and opportunities in psychiatry and also help students of all specialties learn about effectively and compassionately caring for patients who may also have a need for psychiatric care.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Psych IG hopes to expose the many different sides of psychiatry to students and allow them a greater understanding of what it means to become a part of this field. Through interesting lectures, workshops, and discussions, this club hopes to show.

name: Newman Club (Catholic UG) - All-University
description:
Whether you prefer Broadway or Venture Capital, the Newman Club at NYU offers opportunities for you. The Newman Club connects students with Catholic public figures, entrepreneurs, and creatives to get practical advice for their career fields and learn how to pursue their goals with the Catholic faith. As an official NYU club, the Newman Club also offers leadership opportunities that stand out in interviews and mentorship opportunities with professionals in your field.

The Newman Club supports NYU students who desire to live their Catholic faith while pursuing their education and career goals at the same time. There’s no need to forget your faith for your career. Whether you are pursuing business at Stern, acting at Tisch, or engineering at Tandon, the Newman Club is your way of keeping Jesus Christ in focus while you pursue and achieve your professional goals. As St. John Henry Newman once said, “Knowledge is one thing, virtue is another.”
summary: To provide student social, educational, and community service opportunities in a Roman Catholic setting.

name: SPS Schack Real Estate Club
description:
The undergraduate SchackReal Estate Club (SREC) at the NYU SPS Schack Institute of Real Estate is a student-run organization that offers networking and professional development opportunities through events and targeted initiatives.

 

The primary purpose of the SREC is to share knowledge and promote interest in the study of real estate, to enhance the educational process, to encourage appreciation for the complexities of the real estate industry, and to increase opportunities for advancement in the real estate profession.

 

The SREC plans and participates in various types of events including, but not limited to, building and property tours, lectures and guest speaking engagements, training programs, seminars, conferences, meetings with real estate industry leaders and political officials, and social and charitable events.
summary: This club is for SPS Undergraduate students interested in the field of Real Estate.

name: Radiology Interest Group
description:
The Radiology Interest Group is a student led interest group geared at fostering medical student's interests in Radiology. We plan dinner lectures, hands-on workshops, and mentorship opportunities that open the world of NYU Radiology to the student body. Please join us as we prepare to take you on a tour of the world of medical imaging!

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Radiology Interest Group is a student led interest group geared at fostering medical student's interests in Radiology. We plan dinner lectures, hands-on workshops, and mentorship opportunities that open the world of NYU Radiology to students.

description: Asian Heritage Month at NYU is a celebration of the experiences and histories of various Asian and Pacific American cultures. We connect the diverse Asian and Asian American groups on campus as well as in the surrounding communities, by spreading awareness of social issues and of the benefits each group has to offer.

description:
Dancers/Choreographers Alliance (DCA) is an Gallatin-affiliated club at NYU. We encourage process, artistry, and community, and welcome all dancers, regardless of background, technical level, or experience.

description: This club is for SPS Undergraduate students interested in the world of sports business and pursuing a career in the sports industry.

name: SPS Graduate Sports Business Society
description:
The NYU SPS Graduate Sports Business Society (GSBS) connects NYU SPS graduate students with the dynamic world of sports business. We create opportunities for professional growth through networking events, industry panels, venue visits, and collaborations with leading organizations and alumni.

 

Our mission is to empower students to build meaningful relationships, gain real-world insights, and become the next generation of leaders shaping the future of the sports industry. GSBS is proud to foster a diverse and inclusive community that unites students from across NYU SPS who share a passion for sports, business, and innovation.

 

At GSBS, students drive the experience. We center the student voice, encourage collaboration with alumni and industry, and offer hands-on learning through real projects and leadership roles.

 

GSBS Hub
Our central platform for everything GSBS - events, opportunities, benefits, photos, resources, sign-ups, and partner collaborations - all in one place.

 

GSBS Spotlight
Student stories from the field. Share internships, volunteer work, class projects, or study trips to inspire peers and highlight the paths into sports.
summary: Connecting NYU graduate students passionate about the sports industry through networking, professional development, and community events.

name: RISE - Residential International Student Engagement
description:
Our goal is to enhance international student life in the residence halls at NYU. We hope to help international students experience a smooth transition into the United States by providing opportunities for peer interaction, assisting with integration to U.S. society, and helping students stay linked to their own culture while connecting to the greater NYU community.

In order to achieve these goals, we will conduct on-going dialogue sessions, provide support at the hall level through RA involvement, and organize social events which foster the welcoming environment we hope to create.

With these goals and ideals in mind, we embody not only the potential but also the ability to create a positive and enjoyable experience for our international students at NYU.
summary: Our goal is to enhance international student life in the residence halls at NYU. We hope to help international students experience a smooth transition into the United States by providing opportunities for peer interaction, assisting with integration to...

name: The Feminist Society at NYU - All-University
description: The NYU Feminist Society is a community of students that aims to further the principles of feminism at NYU and beyond. We seek to foster community for all feminists on campus and create opportunities to discuss feminist topics, dissect the intersectionality of feminism, act upon feminist ideals, and volunteer for feminist initiatives in the NYC area. We meet Wednesdays at 5pm in GCSL Room 265. All students are welcome!
summary: Educating, engaging, and empowering feminists at NYU and beyond.

name: SPS Students
description:
Events and resources for ALL CURRENTLY ENROLLED School of Professional Studies degree students.

 

Throughout the semester, the Office of Student Life strives to enhance the student experience and foster community by organizing gatherings and special events. The Office also collaborates with the Undergraduate and Graduate Student Councils to produce marquee events.

 

Announcements about these and other events are emailed to your NYU emails throughout the Fall and Spring semesters from the SPS Office of Student Life and SPS Office of Student Affairs.

name: Korean International Students Organization - All-University
description:
KISO (Korean International Students Organization) strives to provide diverse opportunities to Korean international students in NYU. name: Vascular Surgery Interest Group
description:
The field of vascular surgery is an exciting, technically demanding, rapidly evolving surgical specialty. Vascular surgeons treat a variety of circulatory system pathologies, ranging from aortic aneurysms to peripheral vascular disease, using both non-surgical, open surgical, and minimally invasive endovascular techniques. The NYU Vascular Surgery Interest Group (VSIG) serves to introduce students to the field, educate them about the exciting potential of a career in vascular surgery, and connect them with clinical and research mentors among our outstanding vascular surgery faculty.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: A group dedicated to introducing students to the incredible field of vascular surgery.

name: Cross-Examination Debate Association - All-University
description: The New York University Cross Examination Debate Team for excellence at every level of competition with a focus on the Cross Examination Debate Association (CEDA) National Championships, the Novice National Championships, and the JV Nationals Championship. We attain those goals by striving for effective discourse and debate. In previous seasons, The team has also maintained an average GPA over 3.5 for five of the last six years. NYU Professor of Law Norman Dorsen explained, "Excellence goes far beyond intellectual quality and attainment and includes two additional factors that are absolutely necessary; diversity and heart. Heart is what it all rests on--reputation, quality, prestige, and success. It refers to judgment, morality, and higher goals and to the sense of community that comes with being united in a common pursuit." We fulfill Dorsen’s objectives through our competitive endeavors, our community outreach and our campus service.
summary: The New York University Cross Examination Debate Team for excellence at every level of competition with a focus on the Cross Examination Debate Association (CEDA) National Championships, the Novice National Championships, and the JV Nationals Champions...

name: Tisch Undergraduate Student Council
description: Tisch Undergraduate Student Council, or TUSC, is the student governing body for the Tisch School of the Arts at New York University. The Executive Board is made up of eight elected officers and one appointed position, the Freshmen Representative. Keeping our thumbs on the pulse of Tisch, we make decisions and serve as the students’ voice to the administration, faculty, and University Senate. TUSC is also responsible for allocating money to Tisch clubs, planning Freshman Orientation Week, running a number of events for students throughout the year, and sending a team to the All-University Games in February. Furthermore, over the past couple of years, we have worked hard to expand our advocacy role in the school through Town Hall Meetings that address issues and concerns raised by Tisch students. We regularly review proposals for emerging clubs and new events in an effort to contribute to an evolving, growing Tisch community.

name: SPS Social Media and Design Committee
description:
Get Involved!
Whether you are a graphic design expert, or simply interested in social media. All you have to do is come to our weekly meeting.

 

What do we do?
The Social Media and Design Committee plans, executes, and manages social media activity on Instagram, Facebook and Twitter for the SPS Student Council and other SPS committees. The Committee creates content and designs visuals for both online and print.

 

Your Responsibilities!
As a member of the Social Media and Graphic Design committee, you not only get to oversee the student council’s interactions with the public through implementing content strategies on social media platforms as well as using social media marketing tools to create and maintain the student council. You also get to work with other creative team members to produce top tier graphic content and help make design choices (e.g., fonts and colors) for all content we will use.

 

We look forward to seeing you at our meetings. Please feel free to contact us for any further information at the NYU Engage portal.

SPS Undergraduate Student Council

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/spsusc

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/NYUSPSUSC

 

SPS Graduate Student Council

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/spsgsc

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/NYU.SPSGSC
summary: The Social Media and Design Committee plans, executes, and manages social media activity on Instagram, Facebook and Twitter for the SPS Student Council and other SPS committees. The Committee creates content and designs visuals for both online and print.

name: NYU Students for a National Health Program
description:
PNHP is a non-profit, national organization consisting of physicians and medical students advocating for a universal, comprehensive single-payer national health program. A national single-payer system is one that is funded by the government, rather than private insurers, and ensures health care to ALL people. By educating students about single-payer health care and highlighting how this system alleviates some of the devastating downfalls of our current system we hope to inspire our students to explore ways in which we can make the health care system better for our patients. We also provide opportunities for students to actively advocate for single-payer health care in New York State at local and regional events. Significant political activity has begun behind a state single-payer bill titled the NYS Health Bill. Due to our location, NYU students are in a great position to advocate for this bill and potentially affect real change on the NY state level. For too long health policy has been decided by private insurance companies, whose financial motives impinge upon our ability to provide the best treatment to patients. Through advocacy we can place more control in the hands of the physicians, the people who actually care about the health of the patient.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: PNHP is a non-profit, national organization consisting of physicians and medical students advocating for a universal, comprehensive single-payer national health program. A national single-payer system is one that is funded by the government, rather tha...

We have a dining hall serving late-night food and super Sunday brunches, a state-of-the-art Athletic Facility, the Wasserman Center for Career Development on the second floor, study lounges all throughout the 3rd floor, and music practice rooms supplied with pianos. Among our fantastic facilities, we have a variety of program and community-based activities going on including the Palladium Unified Government where you can run for an elected position or serve on the general council. Check out our blog to learn more about Palladium!

The Association’s core values are:

1. Promoting and Improving Excellence in All Aspects of Dental Education.

2. Building Partnerships in Support of and Advocating for the Needs of Dental Education.

3. Serving the Individual Needs of Members and Institutions.

4. Encouraging Communication and Sharing of Information Among the Association’s Members.

name: Christians on Campus aka Zoe Christian Club - All-University
description: Christians on Campus (AKA Zoe Christian Club) is an NYU All-Square Club with believers in Christ from various backgrounds. We love the Lord Jesus Christ and endeavor to give Him "the first place in all things" (Col. 1:18). Through prayer, Bible reading, and fellowship we seek to grow "in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus. To Him be the glory both now and unto the day of eternity. Amen" (2 Peter 3:18). We warmly receive all believers in Christ and seek to maintain the oneness of the Spirit by keeping our focus on Christ alone.
summary: Christians on Campus (AKA Zoe Christian Club) is an NYU All-Square Club with believers in Christ from various backgrounds. We love the Lord Jesus Christ and endeavor to give Him "the first place in all things" (Col. 1:18).

description: Pulse Dance Project is a student-run dance company comprised of dancers and choreographers with a passion for creating art through movement. Our company provides an outlet for NYU students to express their love for dance, grow as an artist, and showcase their work to the community! For more info, check out our  website. Follow us on instagram for updates and audition information: @nyupulsedance

name: Minetta Review, The - All-University
description: The Minetta Review is a literary and arts publication managed by undergraduate students at New York University. Established in 1974, it is the oldest literary publication at the university. We publish prose of all genres, poetry, and art from both within and outside the NYU community. In addition to our editorial board, which reviews submissions, any currently enrolled NYU student is welcome to be a member of our organization! Members can expect literary outings, discussions, and to be part of a vital literary community at NYU! Those interested in joining our editorial board and participating in the publication process can fill out our application here: https://forms.gle/9Yv3bxUNiBsi2oba6

description:
Queer Union provides a location for LGBTQ students interested in activism and organizing to organize, regroup, and discover their own queer lens. By creating a social network and support system for LGBTQ students, we are able to provide more representation to radical and political organizations both on and off campus.

Links and information

name: West 13th St Residence Hall
description: Welcome to Senior House on W. 13th Street! Our vibrant close-knit community provides a great environment to get to know your neighbors, live independently, and establish a fantastic final year as an NYU student. We hope you will join us for the many educational and social opportunities hosted by our Building Leadership Team, Faculty Fellow in Residence, Resident Assistants, and our Hall Council At Thirteenth Street (HATS). Get the inside scoop on what is happening on our community by following us on Twitter, liking us on Facebook, and checking out our Blog. We are here to make your experience one that is memorable; so, let's make some memories together. Enjoy calling Senior House your home!

name: Zeta Beta Tau - All-University
description: Established in 1903, the Gamma Chapter at New York University is the oldest active chapter of Zeta Beta Tau Fraternity. The Fraternity's success is guided by its principles of intellectual awareness, social responsibility, integrity and brotherly love. Back-to-back-to-back ('17,'18, '19) winners of NYU's Greek Week tournament, the Gamma chapter has over 50 active brothers and is the predominant fraternity on campus. Outside of curating a flurrying social scene, Brothers work tirelessly year-round with corporations, charities, and the University to cultivate the Fraternity's campus-wide mandate.
summary: Established in 1903, the Gamma Chapter at New York University is the oldest active chapter of Zeta Beta Tau Fraternity. The Fraternity's success is guided by its principles of intellectual awareness, social responsibility, integrity and brotherly love....

name: Cleftomaniacs (women a capella group) - All-University
description:
The Cleftomaniacs are NYU’s premier all-treble a cappella group. We sing repertoire that ranges from current pop to classic tunes and R&B and features a fun flair that is competely our own!

In 2024, we placed 2nd at the ICCA Northeast Quarterfinals, earning a spot at Northeast Semifinals for the first time in the club's history! The Cleftos also won the special award for Outstanding Choreography at Quarterfinals for the second year in a row.

Instagram: @nyucleftos 
Facebook: NYU Cleftomaniacs

Stream our album, "Bittersweet," on Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Pandora or Deezer!
summary: The Cleftomaniacs are NYU's premier all-treble a cappella group! We sing repertoire that ranges from current pop to classic tunes and R&B, featuring a fun flair that is completely our own!

name: Actuarial Society
description: Welcome to the Actuarial Society! We're a group passionate about actuarial science. While under the Stern umbrella, we welcome students from all across the NYU community. Our aim is to provide students with an exposure to actuarial science as a discipline and as a potential career opportunity. We help students with professional development, technical knowledge, as well as career-related support. Most importantly, we are a tight-knit group and we invite everyone - regardless of career prospects - to learn more and join our actuarial community. Our meetings are Wednesdays 12:30-1:30PM and Fridays 3:30-4:30PM. Feel free to reach out to us at actrlsoc@gmail.com, or to this year's co-presidents Sally Chen sally.chen@stern.nyu.edu and Guruprasad Mukund gm3227@stern.nyu.edu if you have any questions. Hope to see you at our events!
summary: Welcome to the Actuarial Society! We're a group passionate about the discipline of actuarial science. While under the Stern umbrella, we welcome students from all across NYU. Our aim is to provide students with exposure to actuarial science.

name: African Students Union - All-University
description:
The African Students Union is an organization created by the African students of NYU that exists to provide a healthy space for meaningful discussion, exploration and celebration of all things politically, socially, and culturally African. It is dedicated to provide a forum for African Students, students of African Descent and students interested in the African Diaspora to come together and share their similar regional interests. We intend to foster and promote the preservation of African culture within the continent through our meetings and events. While our club focuses on African culture specifically, we welcome people of all backgrounds who have any interest in the continent.

 

 

We strive to create an environment where people who are educated on African topics or wish to be can come together to learn, discuss, and have fun!
summary: The African Students Union exists to provide a means for the meaningful discussion, exploration and celebration of all things politically, socially, and culturally African. We want to create an environment on campus where people who are educated on Afr...

Quadball is a co-ed full contact mix of sports like rugby, handball, and dodgeball. Partnered with other schools around NYC, our team provides camaraderie through a unique sport!

Come check out one of the country's top Quadball teams and see what it is all about!
summary: Are you a fan of Harry Potter? What about thrilling, physical sports? Quadball (Quidditch) at NYU has got you covered! Quadball is a co-ed full contact mix of sports like rugby, handball, and dodgeball.

description: Chinese Student Dental Association (CSDA) is a cultural organization that hopes to bring people from all different backgrounds with the common interest in Chinese culture. name: SPS Schack Real Estate Institute Student Association
description: REISA is the Schack Institute of Real Estate’s student-run organization that offers networking and professional development opportunities through events and targeted initiatives. The primary purpose of REISA is to share knowledge and promote interest in the study of real estate, to enhance the educational process, to encourage appreciation for the complexities of the real estate industry, and to increase opportunities for advancement in the real estate profession. Other purposes include promoting fellowship, camaraderie and support among members, and promoting cooperation among other organizations with similar interests and purposes.
summary: REISA is the Schack Institute of Real Estate’s student-run organization for graduate students which offers networking and professional development opportunities through events and targeted initiatives.

name: Commuter Student Council
description:
The Commuter Student Council addresses issues in the commuter student body, representing all undergraduate and graduate students at NYU. We ensure commuters have a voice in the University’s decision-making process and a community to lean on through fun programming events; advocacy initiatives that tackle basic needs, transportation, accessibility, and otherwise; as well as providing a channel to admin through student-led work. 

Our council and our council-led committees ensure that students have a way to participate, volunteer, and involve themselves in the well-being of our university.
summary: The Commuter Student Council addresses issues in the commuter student body, representing all undergraduate and graduate students at NYU. We ensure commuters have a voice in the University’s decision-making process and a community to lean on.

name: Phi Alpha Honor Society-Pi Pi Chapter
description:
The Phi Alpha Honor Society is a national honor society for social work students. Established in 1962, the purpose of the Phi Alpha Honor Society is to promote humanitarian goals and ideals while providing social work students with an opportunity to develop leadership skills and form closer bonds.

The Phi Alpha Honor Society invites into membership those who have attained excellence in scholarship and achievement in social work practice. The Pi Pi Chapter holds a variety of meetings and community service events throughout the academic year that serve to foster the professional development of its members while encouraging high standards of academic achievement and civic engagement. Programming includes monthly chapter meetings, group-oriented community service activities, and professional development events designed to encourage leadership in social work practice.
summary: The Phi Alpha Honor Society is a national honor society for social work students. Established in 1962, the purpose of the Phi Alpha Honor Society is to promote humanitarian goals and ideals while providing social work students with an opportunity to de...

name: Christian Medical and Dental Association
description: Christian Medical Dental Association is an aspiring group of dental students seeking to be more like Christ by loving others and each other as he first loved us. This involves fellowship, worship, discussion groups, prayer, new friendships, and plenty of outreach opportunities.
summary: Christian Medical Dental Association is a group of dental students seeking to be more like Christ by loving others and each other as he first loved us. In normal people's terms, this involves fellowship, worship, discussion groups, prayer and outreach.

name: SPS Society for Human Resource Management
description:
Welcome to the NYU School of Professional Studies SHRM Chapter, where tomorrow's HR leaders cultivate their professional journey. As an award-winning affiliate of the Society for Human Resource Management (SHRM) the world's premier HR professional organization.

Our chapter, recognized with SHRM's prestigious Merit Award, serves as the cornerstone of NYU's Human Capital Programs. Led by a dedicated eight-member executive board, we create dynamic opportunities for graduate students, alumni, and faculty to shape the future of human resources.

Our Impact by the Numbers:

  • 10+ professional events per semester
  • 8 student leaders driving innovation
  • A growing network of NYU HCM/ HCAT & ECOC graduate students, alumni, and faculty
  • Access to SHRM's global community of 300,000+ HR professionals

We deliver value through four key pillars: Professional Growth, Career Advancement, Leadership Development, and Community Building.

Learn more about SHRM here: https://www.shrm.org/home
summary: This club is for SPS Graduate Students interested in the field of Human Resources.

name: K-Nesis - All-University
description:
KNESIS is NYU's only K-pop dance performance club, focused on studying and appreciating the various elements of Korean pop culture and the performing arts through performances, random play dances, and collaborations with companies affiliated with Korean culture. We hold weekly workshops on Fridays teaching famous K-pop choreographies open to dancers of all levels of experience. Additionally, we hold open auditions for K-pop covers, which we perform and film in Times Square or other representative public locations as well as an end-of-semester showcase for the NYU Student Community. For more information about the club and to see our previous covers and performances, feel free to check out our Youtube, Instagram, and TikTok accounts below!

Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@KNESISatNYU

Instagram: @knesisnyu

TikTok: @knesisnyu
summary: Club Mission Statement:To study and appreciate the various elements of Korean pop culture and the performing arts through dance practices, cover videos, K-films and K-dramas.Club Uniqueness Statement:We would like to explore the various aspects of Kore...

name: University Hall, 110 E. 14 St.
description: University Hall (referred to by everyone at NYU as UHall) is home to 620 residents. We have 14 Resident Assistants (RA), two Faculty Fellows in Residence, and Explorations Communities. The staff and Faculty Fellows are here to create and maintain a vibrant and exciting community. If you are interested in shaping the community at UHall you should run for a position on the UHall Parliament (UP) or serve as a Floor Representative to the Council. UHall has a TV lounge, a music practice room with a piano, and a study lounge. It’s also home to Burger Studio and a branch of University Learning Center - NYU’s peer tutoring center. Check out our blog to learn more about UHall!

name: The Review and Debates - All-University
description: We are a student-run organization dedicated to creating a prominent public debate culture, and to developing an appreciation for political, social, and cultural exchange. We believe that the active competition of ideas is crucial to creating and maintaining free societies, and work to foster this attitude on the campus of the largest private university in the United States. Our Debate Series facilitates discussion and dialogue on topics across the spectrum, because we believe that our campus and the global community benefit from these. We invite guest speakers, academics, and intellectuals, to enhance our discussion and analysis of topics by sharing their expertise with the students. Past guest debaters have included the former President of NYU, Deans of several NYU schools, and prominent individuals associated with the New York Times, Wall Street Journal, Foreign Policy, American Enterprise Institute, and the Council on Foreign Relations, amongst others. The Review is our publication that establishes a platform for discourse among university students across the world. At The Review, we provide rigorous editorials on politics, culture, economics, and other important subjects by publishing focused content that is supported with detailed research and written by passionate individuals.
summary: We are a student-run organization dedicated to creating a prominent public debate culture, and to developing an appreciation for political, social, and cultural exchange. We believe that the active competition of ideas is crucial to creating and mainta...

name: Singapore Students Association - All-University
description:
We are a cultural club built around Singaporeans and where most of us hail from, Singapore. We celebrate all aspects of Singaporean culture - food, people, festivals and holidays, and most importantly - friendships.

Events we hold range from our annual Singapore Night production, Lunar New Year celebrations, career panels, game nights, photo booth nights, and many more.

Whether you were born and raised in Singapore, lived in Singapore, went to school in Singapore, or are just generally interested in Singapore, we welcome you with open arms to come join us for our events to learn more about Singapore, make some Singaporean friends and enjoy some tasty Singaporean food.
summary: We are a cultural club built around Singaporeans and where most of us hail from, Singapore. We celebrate all aspects of Singaporean culture - food, people, festivals and holidays, and most importantly - friendships. Events we hold range from our annual...

description: The Bengali Students Association at NYU was founded in the fall of 2005 and is committed to celebrating the richness of Bengali culture. Our programs strive to spread awareness of Bengali traditions both indigenous to the subcontinent and the diaspora population, explore issues pertaining to the region as it increases its presence on the world stage, and foster a sense of community among the Bengalis at New York University and in the New York City area.
summary: The Bengali Students Association at NYU was founded in the fall of 2005 and is committed to celebrating the richness of Bengali culture. Our programs strive to spread awareness of Bengali traditions both indigenous to the subcontinent and the diaspora.

name: Third Avenue North Dormitory
description: Third North provides a welcoming and inclusive community for over 950 first-year students. To help facilitate the community, enhance your academic experience, plan events, and provide support, there are 29 Resident Assistants, one Resource Center Assistant, one Resident Life Assistant, and two Faculty-Fellows in Residence (Robin Nagle & Mosette Broderick) with their families. Third North is home to four "Explorations" floors (a.k.a. theme floors or living-learning communities) where students can apply based on the themes. We also house a Choices, or substance-free floor, on which students commit to a lifestyle without substances. We have a wide range of facilities in Third North, including the Courtyard Café Dining Hall, a mini-theater, dance studio, music practice rooms, game room, laundry rooms, TV lounge, many study lounges throughout the building, a computer lab, and an incredible outdoor courtyard at the center of it all. Our hall also features a very active hall government, called Third Avenue Government (TAG), which advocates for their fellow residents while also planning great events throughout the year. Check out our blog to learn more about Third North! Each residence hall at NYU has a dedicated team of professional and student staff to ensure students' safety, strong sense of community and a smooth, efficient administrative operations. The professional staff is made up of a Residence Hall Director, Residence Hall Resource Manager and a Residence Hall Assistant Director. In collaboration with the Department of Public Safety and the Office of Facilities and Construction Management, this team will help shape your residential experience at NYU.

NYU's only intentionally funny publication.

We create a satire magazine every year, filled to the brim with humorous articles, visuals, and designs! We're always looking for new writers and graphic designers to create the best issues we possibly can. Meetings will be held weekly, with no application process whatsoever; this coming year (2025-2026), however, we encourage all members to come to meetings with at least one piece. Check out our website for past publications and plenty of laughs!

https://www.plaguemagazine.com/

The Violet Society Program combines academic coaching, career advising, and peer mentorship to support your wellbeing and personal growth, share career opportunities, and provide guidance. This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.

name: Thai Students Association - All-University
description: Thai Students Association had its humble beginnings in 2000 and was started by P'Warut and Thongchai. Noticing the lack of a Thai community for students in New York, they sought the advice of a few graduate students and decided to form Thai-SA. The main purpose of Thai-SA was to establish an organization to bring the entire Thai student community together at NYU, reach out to other students interested in Thailand, and to promote the Thai culture all around campus. Within the first year, membership rose to nearly 60-70 people with the majority as graduate students and a little over fifty percent were Thai. A few years later, Thai Students Association took a hiatus, but we're back! The mission statement since the start has changed very little since our founding -- Thai-SA serves to create a unified fellowship among the Thai student community and to promote the Thai culture to the entire student body. If you have any questions, don't hesitate to reach out to nm4659@nyu.edu!

description: The NYU College of Dentistry Student Government plays a vital role in the student experience. The Student Government works collaboratively with the Office of Student Affairs and NYU Dentistry Administrators to ensure that the student body has the necessary resources and tools to be successful. In accordance with our constitution, the Student Government serves the student body in the following ways: - Governs and supports Class Councils and other student organizations - Fosters community within the College of Dentistry - Represents the voice of the student body and advocates for student needs - Coordinates events & initiatives to enhance the student experience - Serves on College committees as a representative voice of the student body

description: Weinstein is a dynamic and diverse community consisting of almost 600 first year residents and a wonderful staff of two full-time live-in professional staff members (Residence Hall Director and Residence Hall Assistant Director), a full-time Residence Hall Resource Manager and 15 Resident Assistants (RAs). Our 2East Lounge (located on the second floor on the East Tower) has a big screen TV, foosball, ping pong, and pool tables and is used as both a study lounge and a space for the RAs to hold events and activities. In the sub-cellar there are music practice rooms with pianos, a laundry room, and three classrooms, which can also double as study lounges. We have many dining options: Passport Dining @ Weinstein Dining Hall (aka "Downstein"), the Food Court (aka "Upstein"), the Kosher cafeteria, and Peet's Coffee shop. Our hall also features a very active hall government called the Weinstein Hall Advisory Team, or better known as W.H.A.T., that advocates for fellow residents and also plans great events throughout the year.
