description: Alpha Phi Omega is a national co-ed fraternity dedicated to community service. Today, there are more than 17,000 male and female student members at 367 active college campuses nationwide. We are proud to be the largest Greek letter fraternity. Our mission is to develop leadership through service, to promote friendship, and to provide service to humanity. We look for those who are passionate about community service to join our fraternity. Our chapter, Beta Iota, focuses on the needs of others in New York City. Brothers participate in activities that include park clean-ups, bringing food to shelters, and volunteering with children in afterschool programs. There are no restrictions on our list of community service activities, and Brothers are encouraged to offer ideas for projects.

description: The Taiwanese American Student Society (TASS) is dedicated to showcasing and celebrating Taiwanese culture and heritage at NYU. Our mission is to connect students with each other through vibrant cultural events like Taste of Taiwan and our annual Night Market. We aim to foster a deeper appreciation for Taiwanese traditions while creating an inclusive and engaging environment where everyone can come together to learn, share, and enjoy.

name: Haitian American Students Association - All-University
description: The purpose of NYU HASA is to promote awareness, foster relationships between its Haitian and/or Haitian American members, and deepen the understanding of Haitian culture and history throughout New York University and the greater NYC. Through social and cultural programs, we strive to share the beauty of Haitian history, music and language. In doing so, we not only desire to create a safe space for Haitian/Haitian American students to voice their opinions and perspective, but also create an atmosphere of cultural acceptance and diversity on the NYU campus and the city itself.

name: Washington Square Village
description: Grad Housing is a diverse community of approximately 250 Masters and PhD candidates at NYU. We are located at private leased apartments at Washington Square Village (WSV) and Stuyvesant Town (Stuytown). Our Building Leadership Team and seven Resident Assistants strive to be a support system and important resource for the Grad Housing residential students. Throughout the year, we collaborate with many campus offices, such as the Office of Graduate Student Life at the Student Resource Center and the Office for International Scholars, to bring you social, academic, and cultural programming. Our staff members are eager to have students engage with one another and take advantage of the many opportunities found at NYU and in NYC. Both apartment complexes are located in the village. WSV is just steps from Washington Square Park and the main NYU campus, while Stuytown is near the East River. Home to students, faculty, and members of the greater NYC community, our living environments are dynamic, independent and supportive. Check out our blog to learn more!
summary: Grad Housing is a diverse community of approximately 250 Masters and PhD candidates at NYU. We are located at private leased apartments at Washington Square Village (WSV) and Stuyvesant Town (Stuytown). Our Building Leadership Team and seven Resident A...

name: Endodontic Society
description:
The Endodontics Society aims to:

1. Promote endodontics education at NYUCD

2. Host workshops to help students with endodontic procedures

3. Raise interest & awareness of endodontics

4. Create connections between students, specialists, and faculty

name: Swing Dance Society - All-University
description: The purpose of this club is to spread the joy of swing dancing to the NYU community! Swing dance is a term to describe the dances to swing era jazz music. Our club primarily does Lindy Hop, a Black American dance originating in Harlem in the swing era, but we also include other swing dances like vernacular jazz and balboa! We have lessons taught by experienced student peers and occasional guest instructors. We encourage members to go out social dancing around the city. It's a great way to improve your dancing, meet cool people, and have a CharlesTON of fun!
summary: The purpose of this club is to spread the joy of swing dancing to the NYU community! We have lessons taught by peers and professional instructors and we go out social dancing around the city. It's a great way to improve your dancing and meet cool people!

name: Capital Markets Club
description:
The Capital Markets Club serves to educate some of NYU's brightest and most talented students on how markets function both domestically and internationally. Our goal is to help students understand the major players and forces that shape our financial system, while also learning about breakthroughs in financial and economic academia. 

 

Stay up to date with CMC through out Instagram: CMC Instagram

Join our newsletter: CMC Newsletter

See our website: CMC Website
summary: The Capital Markets Club serves to educate some of NYU's brightest and most talented students on how markets function both domestically and internationally.

name: Graduate Student Association
description: The Silver Graduate Student Association is comprised of all students enrolled in an MSW program at the Silver School of Social Work at New York University. It is led by an elected council made up of the President, Vice President, Secretary, Treasurer, and Senator. In the fall, two First-Year Representatives, an Advanced Standing Representative, and an International Student Representative are elected by the incoming class to be their voices on the council. Additionally, representatives from our satellite campuses can be appointed to the council. What We Do We plan events to enrich our community, including two Common Days each year – day-long professional conferences – and Silver Ball. Through partnerships with student organizations and the larger NYU community, our goal is to bolster the Silver community and make sure the students are getting what they need from our school. The GSA’s primary purpose is to act as the liaison between our peers and the Silver administration. We are your representatives, your advocates, and your voice on campus. Reach Out to Us! We need to hear your voices! Reach out to us at anytime for anything; shoot an e-mail to silver.gsa@nyu.edu or visit us in our office off of the lounge on the Garden Level at 1 Washington Square North.
summary: The Graduate Student Association is comprised of all students enrolled in an MSW program at the Silver School of Social Work at New York University.

name: SPS International Club
description: The International Club serves as a social and professional networking hub for SPS international students but is open to all students interested in the mission of the Club. The mission of the International Club is to enhance the international student experience at NYU SPS through student representation in matters affecting student life, the planning and facilitation of events and activities, and by promoting improved intercultural communication.
summary: This club is open to all International and domestic students at the School of Professional Studies

name: Latinx Social Work Student Organization
description: The Latinx Social Work Student Organization (LSWSO) welcomes and supports all students attending NYU Silver School of Social Work and aims to build community among Latinx students. LSWSO helps students expand their understanding of current health and social welfare issues facing the Latinx population. The group fosters cultural competence by deepening our own self-knowledge and works towards increasing our school’s effectiveness in providing services to this diverse and multifaceted community.
summary: The Latinx Social Work Student Organization (LSWSO) welcomes and supports all students attending NYU Silver School of Social Work and aims to build community among Latinx students. LSWSO helps students expand their understanding of current health and s...

name: Entrepreneurial Exchange Group
description: The Entrepreneurial Exchange Group (EEG) is the leading resource for entrepreneurial interests at New York University. By hosting weekly meetings that feature distinguished individuals to speak of their experiences and workshops designed to get your business off the ground, EEG aims to provide its members opportunities to learn, network, and be inspired.

name: Students for Justice in Palestine - All-University
description: Students for Justice in Palestine (SJP) at New York University is a diverse group of students, faculty, staff, and community members organized on democratic principles to promote justice, human rights, liberation, and self-determination for the Palestinian people. SJP believes that while the Palestinian people must ultimately be able to decide their future in Palestine, certain key principles, grounded in international law, human rights, and basic standards of justice, will be fundamental to a just resolution of the plight of the Palestinians. These include the full decolonization of all illegally held Palestinian lands; the end of the Israeli occupation of the Gaza Strip and the West Bank; including East Jerusalem; the implementation of the right of return and repatriation for all Palestinian refugees to their original homes and properties; and an end to the Israeli system of Apartheid and discrimination against the indigenous Palestinian population. Just as SJP condemns the racism and discrimination underlying the policies and laws of the state of Israel, SJP rejects any form of hatred or discrimination against any religious, racial, or ethnic group. SJP’s strength is in the great diversity of its membership. SJP welcomes individuals of all ethnic and religious backgrounds to join in solidarity with the struggle for justice in Palestine.
summary: Students for Justice in Palestine (SJP) at New York University is a diverse group of students, faculty, staff, and community members organized on democratic principles to promote justice, human rights, liberation, and self-determination for the Palestine

name: American Red Cross at NYU - All-University
description:
Welcome to the American Red Cross Chapter of New York University.

We are the founding collegiate unit of American Red Cross, formed on the 16th of November in 1938. Decades later we are a group of dedicated humanitarians supporting the Red Cross mission in and around our New York Campus. 

Our work is hand-in-hand with the greater ARC organization, the greater New York Chapter, and the Red Cross National Collegiate Assembly. Our aim is to foster Red Cross engagement and provide Red Cross resources to all those passionate about getting involved in its service, such as training students, staff, and faculty to become disaster relief volunteers, organizing on-campus blood drives, installing free fire and carbon monoxide detectors in homes, certifying members in First Aid, CPR and AED, raising awareness, and fundraising for various causes.

More than just a club, the NYU ARC is an established chapter. It a representative branch of the Red Cross. We embody, emulate, and connect like-minded individuals to make up a dedicated and committed team of thousands.
summary: Welcome to the American Red Cross of NYU, the founding unit of collegiate Red Cross in the United States.

name: Korean Student Dental Association

name: Rhythmic Impulse at NYU - All-University
description:
About Us: Rhythmic Impulse at NYU (NYURI) is a student organization committed to the promotion of Korean arts and culture through the education and performance of Korean percussion music. NYURI centers on a modern genre of Korean music called Samulnori, which is an ensemble of four percussion instruments designed for stage performance. The four instruments are Kkwaenggwari, Jing, Janggu, and Buk; and their ensemble features a variety of rhythms originating from the central and southern regions of Korea.

 

Membership:

All members of NYU are welcome to join. Prior experience is not required.

 

Meeting Schedule for Spring 2026:

Fridays at 8:30-10:00 PM in Kimmel

 

What to Expect: 

New members will learn to play one of the Samulnori instruments called the Janggu and read the Korean musical composition called Jeongganbo. By the end of the semester, new members will be able to play a basic version of a Samulnori piece called Utdari.

 

Returning members will be trained in more advanced techniques for the Janggu and learn the other three instruments of Samulnori.

Check out one of our performances!
summary: NYURI is NYU's Korean percussion group founded in 1987. We perform throughout the school year all over NYC. No experience or instruments needed, we provide everything you need. Go from learning to performing while making lots of music and new friends!

It’s a physical and virtual gathering space for creatives, storytellers, and writers looking to share their narratives. We use media to build and bridge communities within the Asian-American sphere. We primarily support Korean students in New York University to understand better about the school as well as the New York City and its culture. We also have a network for each Korean students. KGSA at NYU also fulfills its role to help other national students to understand about Korea culture. There are three ways to fulfill our mission. First, KGSA aids newly admitted students acclimate themselves to life in Manhattan and in the City of New York. Second, NYU KGSA provides a forum for Koreans from other institutions to meet, network, and establish friendships that continue after life in Hyde Park comes to an end. Third, the KGSA coordinates several events for Korean corporations and firms that recruit the talented members of the NYU KGSA.

name: Ethiopian Eritrean Student Association - All-University
description: The Ethiopian and Eritrean Student Association at NYU is a vibrant and inclusive community that serves as a cultural hub for students hailing from Ethiopia and Eritrea, as well as those interested in exploring the rich heritage of these East African nations. Committed to fostering a sense of belonging and celebrating the diverse traditions, languages, and histories of Ethiopia and Eritrea, this student organization hosts a wide array of engaging events and activities throughout the academic year. From cultural showcases, traditional music, and dance performances to educational seminars and social gatherings, the association offers a unique platform for students to connect, learn, and share their experiences. Whether you're a member of the Ethiopian and Eritrean communities or simply curious about these cultures, this dynamic student association welcomes everyone to join in the exploration and celebration of these vibrant heritages.
summary: The Eritrean Ethiopian Student Association is a club created by the Ethiopian and Eritrean students of NYU to provide a space for discussion and celebration of our diverse cultures.

description: The mission of the Korean Students Association of New York University shall be to promote Korean culture through cultural, social, political, and community service events. Our activities would range from providing informal social functions to promoting our educational and cultural foundation, which are intrinsically non-political and non-discriminating in nature. It is our belief that being away from our homeland should not compel us to lose our identity. On the contrary, it should be the principle factor in solidifying our relationship and facilitating or achieving these set objectives. It is our convicted intent through this association to not only enrich each other’s lives, but also to introduce our culture and extend our goodwill to other fellow students.

name: SLAM! at NYU - All-University
description:
SLAM! at NYU -- the exclamation point says it all.

This is the official slam poetry club and team. We clap back weekly (as in weekly meetings). It's a completely open space, so even if you just wanna chill and watch good stuff without performing, we got you. 

We watch poetry, read poetry, write poetry, and give amazing feedback (or just snap and "mhm" if that's all you want)! You can also compete to be on the team that we send to an intercollegiate slam every year.

We are proud to have won first place at CUPSI in 2012, 2013, and 2015, 2017, and 2018. Come check us out!
summary:
SLAM! at NYU -- the exclamation point says it all. 

We're the official slam poetry club *and* team. Yes, we're that awesome. Click to read more!

name: International Business Association
description: The purpose of International Business Association is to promote global thinking amongst the student body. We do so by either aligning our activities with the international study components of NYU's undergraduate curriculum and helping students find careers at international organizations either in or outside of the US. We also work to increase awareness of global affairs and to foster discussion and exchange of ideas amongst students. Our professional program promotes the value of global businesses in the US as well as international careers and tries to create a better understanding of business through events with experts from diverse fields.
summary: The International Business Association (IBA) is a pre-professional student-run organization at New York University. Our mission is to promote global thinking amongst the student body and prepare our members for the fast-paced business world.

name: Kappa Kappa Gamma Sorority - All-University
description:
Kappa Kappa Gamma (ΚΚΓ), also known simply as Kappa or KKG, is an internationally-renowned Panhellenic sorority founded in 1870 with values of knowledge, trailblazing, truth, connection, optimism, and respect. 

 

ABOUT

With members from a variety of diverse backgrounds, our sisters are bonded by our shared values and the ritual of Kappa Kappa Gamma. Members’ commitment to friendship, leadership, scholarship, and service is lived out through our sisterhood, social, educational, philanthropic, and academic programming throughout the year. We are an ambitious, intelligent, and compassionate group of women who balance the rigorous academics of New York University with the thrilling and fun opportunities that New York City has to offer. Serving as a vital support system and community for each other, Kappa is our foundation for endless success.

 

HISTORY

Kappa Psi Delta was a local chapter founded in 1998 that spent 13 years at NYU. In April 2011, the women of KPsiD decided to affiliate with Kappa Kappa Gamma. On November 5th, 2011, our chapter was installed as KKG’s 138th chapter and 20 active members were initiated to become the Eta Pi charter class. In the nine years since our installation, we have grown to be an organization of over 130 women.

 

ACADEMICS

Joining a sorority in college is a life-changing experience, but garnering an education is the top priority for Kappa. This is why our chapter is expected to maintain - and consistently achieves - academic standards higher than the NYU all-sorority GPA. Each member is expected to contribute to the overall GPA of the chapter. If a Kappa struggles academically, we help her through it. The Academic Excellence Committee meets with her to craft an improvement plan using study tactics and time management. We want each sister to leave college with what they came for: a degree. Library study rooms, which we reserve for Sunday nights after chapter and the weeks surrounding finals, give girls a quiet place to study. Our chapter also uses an Owl Pal program, pairing a new member up with an older member in a similar major/field of study. This allows girls to study together and get advice related to their major. We host an annual alumnae networking event, Keyed-In, which allows collegiate sisters to meet and connect with sisters who are five, ten, or twenty years into their careers! Many sisters find internships, jobs, and mentors through this event.

 

OUR HOUSE

Our chapter resides on the penthouse floor of the Lafayette Residence Hall. For the last two years, we have been selected among the Panhellenic and Inter-fraternity organizations on a basis of academic achievement, leadership, and extracurricular activities to live in the largest penthouse! Seven sisters are selected to reside in the suite that features high ceilings and beautiful views.

 

PHILANTHROPIES

Reading Is Fundamental (RIF)

The Kappa Kappa Gamma Foundation

New York Dance Marathon (NYDM)

 

SISTERHOOD

Ask anyone in Kappa and they'll tell you how much they love their sisters. We'd do anything for each other - the girls in our chapter are our future bridesmaids and forever best friends! Fun sisterhood events include Sunday brunches in the penthouse, supporting NYU at sporting events, visiting other Kappa chapters at nearby schools, photoshoots with stores around NYC, and movie nights. We travel the world together and love spending time with each other!

 

SOCIAL

Mixers, Date Parties, Semi Formals, and our Sapphire Ball Formal are some of our favorite events as Kappas. Dancing the night away at venues across New York City, social events are a great way to dress up and have fun with sisters and our dates. Kappa participates in a number of mixers each year, where we pair up with a fraternity, dress up in costumes, and enjoy a night of socializing and dancing together at various amazing venues in Manhattan. Mixers are some of KKG’s favorite social events and a great way to get to know different Greek organizations! 

 

PANHELLENIC

We love being part of the greater Panhellenic community - no matter what the letter we're all Greek together! On the 2020 Panhellenic Executive Board, our sister served as VP of Recruitment Counselors.

 

AWARDS

2020: Risk Management Award (HQ), Honorable Mention for Standards and Academic Excellence (HQ) 

2018: Chapter of the Year, Chapter President of the Year 

2017: Outstanding Community Involvement 

2016: Most Outstanding Chapter (HQ), Most Improved Chapter 

2015: Outstanding Academic Achievement, Housing Representative of the Year

2014: Housing Representative of the Year

2013: Outstanding Recruitment, Outstanding Alumna Advisor, Sorority Woman of the Year

Of Kappa's 140 chapters, Eta Pi was incredibly honored to receive the designation of Most Outstanding Chapter of the Biennium (2014-2016) at the Kappa Kappa Gamma General Convention in June 2016. We are so proud and humbled to have been given this award! We were also recognized with Honorable Mentions for the following awards: Chapter/Advisory Board Relations Award, Academic Excellence Award, Sally Moore Nitschke Lifelong Education Award, Public Relations Award, Standards Award and Excellence in Chapter Management Award.

 

JOIN KAPPA

Being a sister of Kappa Kappa Gamma means having endless opportunities in leadership, academics, philanthropy, and true friendship. With a network of over 300,000 initiated members, the sisterhood of Kappa Kappa Gamma lasts far beyond the four years of undergraduate life. We hope that you will join us during Recruitment, and see first hand how amazing Kappa can be! 

Kappa Kappa Gamma is a single-gender organization comprised of individuals who identify as women whose governing documents do not discriminate in membership selection except by requiring good scholarship and ethical character.
summary: Kappa Kappa Gamma (ΚΚΓ), also known simply as Kappa or KKG, is an internationally-renowned Panhellenic sorority founded in 1870 that values knowledge, trailblazing, truth, connection, optimism, and respect. Learn more about Kappa at NYU at nyu.kappa.org.

name: Student Development Initiative
description:
The Student Development Initiative (SDI) is a student driven task force that attempts to facilitate and support the student body's development of the AAMC core professional competencies. SDI hosts numerous events per year that may address one or more of these core competencies: enhance cultural awareness, cultural humility, empathy and compassion, and ethical responsibility to self and others. SDI is a collective group of students who have united in an effort to strengthen the institutional mission and commitment to training students to become compassionate medical professionals. Each member of SDI is passionate about learning, developing and promoting these values. 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Student Development Initiative (SDI) is a student driven task force that looks at issues of diversity, inclusion, and cultural competency for undergraduate medical education at NYU School of Medicine.

We hope to bring together many disciplines – including pediatrics, oral and maxillofacial surgery, orthodontics, and general dentistry – to provide complete comprehensive care for those in need. Our organization aims to spread awareness, raise funds to support cleft lip surgeries, pool resources in one location, and organize outreaches. Our mission is to give back to the community both through education as well as with hands-on work.

This garden is an exploratory, experimental green space aimed at putting our interest into action. In the off-season, we do seed starting, natural flavor-infused vinegars/oils, learn about indigenous foodways, and field trips to botanic gardens!

The Orthodontics Society at New York University is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering the development and awareness of orthodontics for NYU dental students. The society aims to enrich the educational and personal growth of students interested in specializing in orthodontics. To achieve this, the society provides opportunities for research, organizes Q&A sessions with orthodontic residents and prominent orthodontists, and offers workshops to guide students through the application process.

name: Arab Students Association - All-University
description: Arab Students Association is a platform through which Arab students from different socio-cultural and political contexts can come together, promoting a better understanding of Arab culture, diversity, history and politics. Regardless of nationality and upbringing, this organization is place for all Arabs who are interested in open discussion amongst a group of culturally-similar, yet intellectually diverse students. It seeks to create and sustain an environment in which Arab students and interested members of the community can become increasingly aware of the richness of Arab heritage and to correct false negative perceptions of Arab societies and culture. We aim to conduct activities that are both on-campus and involve the larger community.

name: Pi Beta Phi - All-University
description:
Welcome to the NY Eta Chapter of Pi Beta Phi at New York University!

 

ABOUT

Pi Beta Phi (ΠΒΦ), founded at Monmouth College in Monmouth, Illinois in 1867, has 135 active chapters and more than 330 alumnae groups in the United States and Canada. Pi Beta Phi has worked to support and empower our sisters since 1867. In the century and a half since, more than 300,000 women have proudly worn our golden arrow. Through a lifelong membership experience rooted in timeless values, we promote friendship, cultivate leadership potential and enrich lives through community service—ensuring each member has the support of sincere friends and the inspiration to achieve their goals.

 

HISTORY

The NY Eta chapter of Pi Beta Phi was founded on January 31st, 2009. It is one of seven sororities that belong to the NYU Panhellenic Council. In the 14 years since installation, our chapter has grown even more in membership and diversity. Our sisterhood in Pi Beta Phi is formed by a diverse group of individuals who share common values and engage in meaningful ritual. Throughout the year, we actively demonstrate our dedication to sincere friendship, servant leadership, scholarship, and philanthropic service through various sisterhood and philanthropic activities and events. As a collective of determined, clever, and caring people, we skillfully manage the demanding academics at New York University while embracing the exciting and enjoyable experiences that New York City presents. Pi Phi serves as an indispensable source of support and a tight-knit community, laying the groundwork for its members' future achievements.

 

PHILANTHROPIC EFFORTS

Read > Lead > Achieve

Champions Are Readers (CAR)

The Literacy Fund at Pi Beta Phi Foundation 

New York Dance Marathon (NYDM)

 

JOIN PI PHI

Since 1867, Pi Beta Phi has ensured women have the support of sincere friends, opportunities to lead, and the inspiration to achieve their goals. Women coming together to support each other is as relevant today as when our 12 courageous founders formed the first women’s fraternity. Friendship has been at the core of Pi Phi's sisterhood for more than 150 years. Now more than 300,000 members strong, we cultivate relationships through shared experiences and values. We prioritize philanthropic work and maintain a century-long commitment to bettering society through literacy service. Our premier programming and member events promote personal, intellectual, and leadership growth and provide Pi Phis a place to develop and practice their skills. Our members know Pi Phi's sisterhood lasts a lifetime. 

Pi Beta Phi Fraternity does not discriminate in its operations or membership selection practices on the basis of race, religious affiliation, national origin, physical ability, or sexual orientation. Pi Beta Phi is a women’s organization for individuals who live and self-identify as women.

 

We invite you to join us for Recruitment and witness the incredible opportunities and experiences that Pi Phi has to offer!
summary: Welcome to the NY Eta Chapter of Pi Beta Phi at New York University! Founded at Monmouth College in Monmouth, Illinois in 1867, Pi Beta Phi has 135 active chapters and more than 330 alumnae groups in the United States and Canada.

name: Seed (Christian Publication) - All-University
description: Hi, we’re Seed — the only on-campus Christian publication at NYU. Named to reference the Gospel as a seed to be planted, Seed is intended to spread Christian thought across campus. Please contact us if you have any questions about our organization. We would love to hear your thoughts and speak with you. The purpose of NYU Seed is to provide spiritual and theological answers, spark new questions, address issues relevant to students, to offer an alternative view of life on a local and global scale, and to reach as many students as possible. The tone of this publication ranges from thought-provoking, controversial and informational — to frivolous, humorous and light-hearted. If you would like a question answered or if you are interested in current Christian topics, Seed wants to hear from you. Check out the past issues section to download your own PDF issue of Seed today. We are currently accepting submissions, so contact us to submit your articles, poems, concert reviews and cartoons! We are always looking for writers, artists and photographers — your talents are graciously appreciated! The views and opinions published in NYU Seed magazine and this site, NYUSeed.com, are not necessarily those of the editors, writers or members of NYU Seed, New York University or any other group affiliated with these parties.

name: Periodontology Society
description:
�� Passionate about strong smiles

🩺 Committed to education, research, & service

�� Fostering a supportive environment for future periodontists

 

Mission

To champion the success and professional growth of pre-doctoral students in periodontics through education, collaboration, and community engagement, aiming to enhance patient health and quality of life.

 

Vision

To foster a supportive environment for future periodontists to develop professionally, embracing diversity, ethical responsibility, and advocacy while striving for excellence in education and leadership.

 

Core Values

  • Support: Providing resources & opportunities for professional development and effective patient care.

  • Evidence-Based Practice: Promoting evidence-based practices and ethical research in periodontics.

  • Advocacy: Advocating for the field within dental education, organized dentistry and public policy.

  • Public Education: Educating the public on periodontal disease and periodontist expertise.

  • Collaboration: Fostering collaboration among students, residents, faculty, and healthcare partners.

  • Continuous Education: Committing to continuous education and expansion of treatment options to improve public health and safety

  • Inclusion, Respect & Dignity: Embracing diversity and cultivating inclusion, respect, and dignity.
summary:
🌟 Passionate about strong smiles
🩺 Committed to education, research, & service
🌍 Fostering a supportive environment for future periodontists

description: Dangerbox is NYU's longest-running, premiere longform improv comedy team! Founded in 2002, we now perform on and off campus for the NYU student body and NYC at large utilizing longform structures like montages, spokanes, La Rondes, and more. We are fans, students, and performers at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater, the People's Improv Theater, and Magnet Theater. We collaborate with other comedy teams on campus and universities on the east coast - we have been selected for annual improv festivals at Skidmore University and Georgetown University. The members in Dangerbox specialize in making each other laugh and sometimes there’s an audience too. Our alumni include Bowen Yang, Donald Glover, Chloe Troast, and Martin Herlihy! Many of our alumni have gone on to perform and write for SNL, HBO, Derrick Comedy, Pop Roulette, and more! Follow us on Instagram @dangerboxnyu to stay up to date, and join us for auditions on September 9th and 11th!

name: Zeta Psi Fraternity of North America - All-University
description: Follow our news and updates at www.facebook.com/nyuzete and www.nyuzetapsi.com Becoming a Zete (pronounced "Zate") will be a decision you will never regret: four great years of fun, excitement, challenge, leadership, and lifelong brotherhood through thick and thin. For our brothers, we are the source of all knowledge NOT taught in a classroom, and we provide the edge, opportunities, and resources you need to succeed in school, in business, and most importantly - in life. Zeta Psi is one of the oldest social fraternities in the United States, founded by Freemasons in 1847 here at NYU - 165 years of heritage, tradition, and enduring success. Through our extensive alumni network, our active brothers have developed connections with such companies as: Goldman Sachs, J.P. Morgan, Morgan Stanley, Credit Suisse, Barclays, Columbia Pictures, Sony Pictures, Vanguard Management Group, countless law firms and hospitals throughout New York and Washington D.C. We work hard to help our new brothers find their ideal internships early on in their career at NYU. From winning intramural sports championships to maintaining one of the highest aggregate chapter GPAs on campus (3.5), to participating in many philanthropic efforts and having frequent social events throughout the year, Zeta Psi prides itself on being one of the most diverse groups at NYU. Come out to rush week to meet some of our brothers and see what we're all about, beginning in September.
summary: Follow our news and updates at www.facebook.com/nyuzete and www.nyuzetapsi.comBecoming a Zete (pronounced "Zate") will be a decision you will never regret: four great years of fun, excitement, challenge, leadership, and lifelong brotherhood through thi...

name: LUCHA (Latinos Unidos Con Honor y Amistad) - All-University
description: OUR HISTORY L.U.C.H.A was founded in 1971 after the Higher Education Opportunity Program (H.E.O.P) began to diversify New York University. The influx of Latinos and African American students created a need for an outlet in which their voices could be heard. Politically minded students came together and formed the League of Unified Cooperative Hispanic Americans. In 1994, the E-board of the already long established club decided to change the name of their organization. L.U.C.H.A became "Latinos Unidos Con Honor Y Amistad," representing the struggle that breeds accomplishments and success for our people. Que Siga La L.U.C.H.A!!! MANIFESTO L.U.C.H.A, an organization for social, politcal, and cultural education, must struggle to bring about the necessary changes within NYU as well as our respective communities for the betterment of our society. Any further procrastination on our part will only leave our voices fixed, caught in a yoke of oppression. Our primary goal as an organization is to engage our thoughts, challenge conceptions and provoke new ideas for a better tomorrow. GETTING INVOLVED L.U.C.H.A is a student-run organization and relies heavily on the support of incoming, as well as returning, students to keep its legacy alive. While we focus primarily on issues concerning Latinos, we encourage people of all cultural backgrounds to attend and participate in all of our meetings and major events. We encourage you to keep an eye out for our upcoming community service initatives and cosponsorships with other clubs on campus. If you have any concerns, questions, suggestions, or are interested in L.U.C.H.A, do not hesitate to contact us.
summary: LUCHA, "Latinos Unidos Con Honor Y Amistad, is an org for social, political, and cultural education. We encourage people of all cultural backgrounds to attend and participate.....

name: Tech at NYU - All-University
description:
Tech@NYU is growing the technology, design and entrepreneurship culture at NYU and surrounding schools. To get there, we need students continually building, to learn and internalize the nuances of great product and business. Tech@NYU is the sandbox that students need: a place to experiment, a forum for recognition, a community for discussion and sharing, and a stage for inspiration. Tech@NYU was created in Fall 2009 to connect technical and non-technical NYU students interested in building technology startups. Incubated with the help of the NYU Stern Berkley Center for Entrepreneurship & Innovation, Tech@NYU held its first event in October 2009 to help students form teams for the Stern Business Plan Competition. Now, our events are routinely attended by over 2000 members of the NYU undergrad and graduate community as well as technologists residing in New York City. We are an officially-recognized NYU All-Square club.

Join our Discord to connect, list opportunities, and interact with the org as a whole!

Follow us on Instagram @techatnyu for updates!

name: I Recognize You
description: I Recognize You is a student group geared towards providing peer support to those who identify as Women of Color. IRY recognizes the need for socioemotional support for women of color on campus. IRY is a student group that seeks to bring Women of Color together to share their individual experiences both inside and outside of the classroom around topics such as identity, socioeconomic status, leadership, discrimination, and many others. IRY will run as both a support group where members can seek advice and assistance from one another, and also a self-care group where members can practice mindfulness and wellness techniques. IRY believes in and upholds the core values of integrity, respect, competence and social justice. IRY is a space that promotes inclusivity and bares no judgement.  Members are encouraged to come as they are, as they will be able to seek sisterhood within the other members. IRY is committed to the professional development of its future social workers through the practice of self-preservation and community.

description:
PHI CHI THETA: NEW YORK BETA CHAPTER

At New York University's New York Beta chapter of Phi Chi Theta, you will find a diverse group of ambitious, talented, friendly, and fun students who embrace the meaning of fraternity. While we all have a variety of common interests in the fields of Business and Economics, we are not just a professional organization. We are a family, and we welcome you to join us.

OUR MISSION The mission of Phi Chi Theta is to provide a national and local network to share resources, ideas, and concepts. To accomplish this, we organize an extensive lecture series each semester, which provides opportunities to interact and network with established professionals from various backgrounds. In addition, we present annual alumni panels as well as resume and interviewing workshops. As important as it is to prepare for entering the business world, Phi Chi Theta strongly believes that we must never forgo our humanity for the sake of professional achievement.

name: Quantitative Finance Society
description:
QFS is a team focused on educating and training our members for Sales & Trading roles and Hedge Fund roles. We host weekly member meetings. On top of that, we have a portfolio team which meets weekly and runs a hedge-fund style investment strategy. Members of the portfolio team consistently get into not just the most prestigious investment banks (In roles including Sales & Trading and Investment Banking) but the top hedge funds and private equity funds.

We educate our members on various forms of investing, including credit investments, macro trading, stock pitching, algorithm trading, bankruptcy investing, special situations investing, capital structure arbitrage, managed futures trading, and derivatives.

On March 17, 1917, five women at New York University School of Law took a pledge of loyalty and founded the Alpha Chapter of Delta Phi Epsilon, the first non- sectarian, social sorority and the only one founded at a professional school. Five years later, on March 17, 1922, Delta Phi Epsilon was formally incorporated under the laws of the State of New York. Philanthropies The Cystic Fibrosis Foundation (CFF) Anorexia Nervosa and Associated Disorders (ANAD) The Delta Phi Epsilon Education Foundation. Delta Phi Epsilon strives to organize activities following the principles of the Five Pearls: Sisterhood, Service, Scholarship, Self, and Social.

We act as support for anyone in the ASL classes and for those interested in American Sign Language but have not yet been immersed. During our meetings we talk about important deaf individuals, play games involving sign language, discuss controversial issues within Deaf Culture, and organize events that expose our members to an in-depth signing/cultural experience. ASL Club at NYU is a place where anyone from the novice to the expert can come to improve their signing skills. We play games, have discussions, and learn from each other all in a non-pressure environment. Our meetings are voices-off!

name: Society for the Advancement of Materials and Process Engineering
description:
As an international professional society, SAMPE provides information on new materials and processing technology via technical forums, journal publications, and books in which professionals in the field can exchange ideas and their views. As the only technical society encompassing all fields of endeavor in materials and processes, SAMPE provides a unique and valuable forum for scientists, engineers, and academicians. SAMPE at NYU is the first SAMPE chapter in New York City, founded in 2014.

By joining SAMPE at NYU, students have opportunities to learn more about the field of composites and material science, partake in student competitions, and meet local industry professionals. For more information about our regional chapter sponsor go to http://www.njsampe.org/
summary: As an international professional society, SAMPE provides information on new materials and processing technology via technical forums, journal publications, and books in which professionals in the field can exchange ideas and their views.

The Upsilon Upsilon Chapter of Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. was chartered May 24, 2016 on the campus of New York University by Chevaun Samuels, Nathaniel Simmons, Orville Edwards, Kwame Assoku, Ta'riq Fisher, Romel Williams, Tamir Griffin and Udensi Kalu.

summary: APUG Club is a student-run organization dedicated to providing opportunities for students to enhance their understanding and appreciation of psychology through a variety of social, professional development, and community service activities.

name: The Black Muslim Initiative at NYU - All-University
description: BMI is a student-led group that was established in the fall 2017 because the duality of being Black and Muslim often puts us in a position where we feel that we must choose between our identities. Our main goal is to shed light on the experiences of Black Muslims within the broader Muslim community while acknowledging and accepting our history and ancestors’ legacies. We want folks to feel a sense of acknowledgment, respect, and empathy being at the intersection of multiple identities and communities. In addition, we seek to integrate and create an atmosphere that is inclusive of the larger Muslim community. Ultimately, we hope we can bridge the divisions, and the misunderstandings and dismantle the stereotypes alongside the that persists about Black Muslims.
summary: At BMI, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment where we learn from and celebrate the rich history of Black Muslims, while striving to build solidarity within and beyond the Muslim community. Join us in celebrating our common humanity.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America at NYU - All-University
description:
NYU YDSA meets weekly where we plan strategic campaigns, political education meetings, and socials in which we discuss topics relating to socialism and a fair and just world. Young Democratic Socialists of America at NYU is NYU's chapter of the Democratic Socialists of America, America's largest socialist organization. We believe that a group to discuss, learn, and advocate for a different kind of society is necessary in the face of the historical moments we are living through. Our beliefs align with an economy and society being run democratically to meet human needs, instead of making profits for a few. We are a political and activist organization, not a party; we welcome all sorts of political beliefs, as you do not need to be a socialist to want to better the world. We use a variety of tactics, from legislative to direct action, to fight for reforms that empower working people. This is an urgent time, and we want an open and inclusive space for students at NYU to hear new political ideas, to learn about activism and how different struggles can move forward together, and to support each other and all those who lose out from the current order.

 

New members should fill out our sign up form to get involved!
summary: NYU Young Democratic Socialists of America is a leftist political club at NYU fighting for a world that works for all of us. We know that a better world is possible and organize for healthcare as a right, worker power, and more at NYU and across NYC.

TAMID students are paired with challenging, yet exciting projects within consulting, tech, and fund tracks to help build skills through real-world experiences to prepare them for their future careers. Our apolitical, areligious organization exists on 60+ college campuses, with over 3000+ members in the US, China, Canada, and Israel. All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.

We bring speakers in from the best venture capital firms and technology companies to speak about their experiences. We also host workshops for all of our members where we walk through VC deals, understand why certain startups are successful, and how we can value private companies with a hands-on, interactive approach. name: Bahá'í Club - All-University
description: The Bahá'í Faith is an independent world religion whose purpose is to unite humanity. Bahá'í's believe in Bahá'u'lláh, whose teachings explore the existence of God, how all religions come from the same Source to educate the people of the world, and how all of humanity is one. Bahá'u'lláh also writes that all of us have "been created to carry forward an ever advancing civilization". This is why Bahá'í's all over the world are engaging with friends from all backgrounds and ideologies to contribute to building a better world. Come join us to deepen together and to serve our local communities! We invite everyone from any or no background to explore meaningful topics.
summary:
"The religion of God is for love and unity; make it not the cause of enmity or dissension." - Bahá'u'lláh
We invite everyone from all walks of life to explore meaningful and spiritual topics.

name: Composers' Collective
description: NYU Composer's Collective (CC) exists, first and foremost, to promote the music of NYU Steinhardt composers. The title of composer is not exclusively referring to those pursuing a major in Music Theory and Composition. With each event, we strive to spotlight and showcase the wide range of talents found at our school: cutting-edge concert composers, film composers, songwriters, and electronic music composers are all to be given opportunities for promotion and exposure. The wide variety of events CC puts on—concerts, social events, forums, guest speakers, workshops, etc.— to build a community for music-makers of all types and genres, expanding opportunities already provided in school, and creating a plethora of connections to those who study or are interested in music at NYU.
summary: NYU Composer's Collective (CC) is a club that provides opportunities to promote the music of Steinhardt composers and music makers across NYU. Hosting five concerts each year, filled with student works, as well as various community-building events.

name: SPS Hospitality Business Society
description:
The Hospitality Business Society is a student-run club under NYU’s Jonathan M. Tisch Center. The organization is primarily for hotel and tourism management undergraduate majors but is open to students of all majors.

 

HBS serves as a resource to connect our student members with New York’s dominant hospitality industry through various events and initiatives, which span the realms of education, community service, career advancement, industry relations, and social networking. We organize biannual Internship Fairs, announce professional and volunteer opportunities, and establish both student and alumni relationships through our mentorship program. General meetings typically occur on a monthly basis, and a variety of our events are organized for both professional and social purposes.

 

These efforts will allow students to build meaningful professional relationships with NYU faculty, industry professionals, and local community members. HBS was once known as the Pineapple Society, which honored the pineapple as a symbol of the hospitality industry.
summary: The Hospitality Business Society is a student-run club under NYU’s Jonathan M. Tisch Center. The organization is primarily for hotel and tourism management undergraduate majors but is open to students of all majors.

name: Medical Student Doulas
description:
The Medical Student Doulas and Prenatal Partners Program will provide medical students with the opportunity to learn about social determinants of health and the patient perspective as they support families prenatally and in the delivery room. Further, we will be working with a Reproductive Choice Clinic to provide support individuals while they receive medical care. 

 

The primary aims of the Medical Student Doula/Prenatal Partners Program are three-fold:

 

Medical students as doulas: We will provide full spectrum doula training for medical students interested in volunteering with us. These students would then have the opportunity to use their skills by working as a volunteer doula on Labor and Delivery, in a Family Planning Clinic, and through a phoneline service.

 

Reflection and education: Monthly meetings would provide an educational and safe space for student participants to reflect on their experiences and learn more about factors impacting prenatal, postnatal, and neonatal health through group exercises and guest speakers. 

Prenatal support: We hope to expand the program to provide prenatal services to families as patient navigators/partners. A specific plan for this aim will depend on further exploration of how medical students can provide the most effective support to patients. 


This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Medical Student Doulas and Prenatal Partners Program will provide medical students with the opportunity to learn about social determinants of health and the patient perspective as they support families prenatally and in the delivery room.

name: Effective Altruism at NYU - All-University
description: Effective altruism is a philosophy and social movement that applies evidence and reason to determine the most effective ways to improve the world. Effective altruists aim to consider all causes and actions, and then act in the way that brings about the greatest positive impact. It is this broad evidence-based approach that distinguishes effective altruism from traditional altruism or charity. While a substantial proportion of effective altruists have focused on the nonprofit sector, the philosophy of effective altruism applies much more broadly, e.g., to prioritizing the scientific projects, companies, and policy initiatives which can be estimated to save and improve the most lives. We at EA NYU are excited to be a part of this movement and hope you’ll join us.
summary: Effective altruism is a philosophy and social movement that applies evidence and reason to determine the most effective ways to improve the world. Effective altruists aim to act in ways that bring about the greatest positive impact.
