name: Kappa Phi Lambda - All-University
description:
The Epsilon Associate Chapter of Kappa Phi Lambda was established at New York University on March 7, 1998 and rechartered on May 1, 2016 on the pillars of sisterhood, service, and cultural diversity. 

 

Although it is difficult to describe, the bond of sisterhood is unique relationship based on friendship, respect, and a shared vision of empowering one another. Wherever we go, and no matter how long ago we were initiated, each sister is connected and important to the sorority. With a network of sisters spanning across the globe, we can find comfort in knowing that Kappa Phi Lambda extends so much further beyond our own universities!

 

Contributing to our local community through service is also an important shared value for our sisters. We uphold strong ideals of philanthropy and are proud to support CARE, a leading humanitarian organization fighting global poverty as our national philanthropy. In addition, we work closely with our local philanthropy, Sanctuary for Families, a non-profit organization dedicated to the safety, healing and self-determination of victims of domestic violence and related forms of gender violence.

 

Finally, we believe in educating ourselves and the community about not only the beauty and complexity of Pan-Asian cultural heritages, but of all identities, including other BIPOC communities and the LGBTQ+ community. Although we are an Asian / Asian American interest sorority, by no means are we Asian exclusive. Across the nation, sisters represent over 50 different ethnicities and over 30 different native languages, with many intersectional identities that we believe in creating an inclusive and empowering environment for.

 

Twenty-five years since our establishment, the Epsilon Associate Chapter is now 182 sisters strong and growing!
summary: Kappa Phi Lambda is an Asian-interest sorority that aims to provide a community that is culturally sound and educationally inspiring for both Asian-American women, and any women interested in Asian culture.

name: Music Technology Student Ambassadors
description: Our mission is to foster a welcoming and inclusive community for music technology students by raising awareness of the vital role technology plays in the music industry. We connect students with professionals, provide educational opportunities, and ensure that the contributions of music technologists are recognized and celebrated. Through mentorship and events, we empower students to succeed academically and professionally, while building a supportive network for the future.
summary: The Music Technology Student Ambassadors aim to act as a bridge between those of all skill levels interested in Music Technology and other related musical and technical fields.

name: Society for Human Resource Management
description: TechSHRM (formerly PolySHRM) is the Society for Human Resource Management student chapter at NYU Tandon School of Engineering - Polytechnic Institute. TechSHRM has been awarded the Superior Merit Award by SHRM for its innovative activities almost every year since its founding in 1987. In 2019-20, TechSHRM was selected as one of the 10 most outstanding chapters in the U.S. (for the third time). In addition, many of its leaders have received the coveted Sherman Award from NYC SHRM. TechSHRM is committed to providing its members and the NYU Tandon student community with professional and leadership development opportunities to prepare them for future careers. Most TechSHRM members are students in the Department of Technology Management and Innovation which offers a concentration in People Analytics in the Management of Technology MS degree program. The TechSHRM chapter reinforces this unique program by focusing on activities that integrate the management of people, organizations, analytics and technology. The testament to our zeal and achievements is also highlighted by our reception of "Student Organization of the Year 2025" award as well, which is indeed a pinnacle of honor.
summary: TechSHRM (formerly PolySHRM) is the Society for Human Resource Management student chapter at NYU Tandon School of Engineering - Polytechnic Institute. TechSHRM has been awarded the Superior Merit Award by SHRM for its innovative activities.

name: Gallatin Film Alliance
description:
Welcome to Gallatin's hub for all things film! �� ��️ ✨

 

Our goal at GFA is to provide all students at NYU with access to everything needed to get involved with film: community, material resources, a strong student/alumni network, education, & much more! We're so excited to meet you! Everyone, regardless of NYU college, is welcome here at GFA. 🫶 <3

 

 

As an organization, we...

 

- Run club meetings throughout the semester to foster community and provide education (Equipment training sessions,  "How to Pitch Like a Pro," "Post-Production 101," Jeopardy night, cozy evening film screenings, etc…) 

 

- Host large-scale Gallatin events, welcome to the public upon RSVP to celebrate student work and provide our students with networking opportunities— especially involving our astounding alumni community (Especially notable: the annual GFA Film Festival every April!)

 

- Provide our amazingly creative and hard-working students at Gallatin (aka: YOU!) with material resources to help you produce your own films and get you working on set! (Our student resource programs include: Equipment Rental program, Cast & Crew Database outreach system, student/alumni promotions, and more!) 

 

 

🤩 SUBSCRIBE TO OUR NEWSLETTER AND CHECK OUT OUR STUDENT RESOURCE PROGRAMS ON OUR LINKTREE! 

 

📨 Email us: gallatinfilm@gmail.com

 

📱 See our events and DM us: @gallatinfilm on Instagram

 

 

We hope to hear from you and meet you soon! 

 

All our love,

GFA 🎬❤️😸
summary:
Welcome to Gallatin's hub for all things film! 🎥 🎞️ ✨

Here to provide NYU students with access to everything needed to get involved with film: community, material resources, a strong student/alumni network, education, & much more!

name: The Ballet Collaborative - All-University
description: As NYU's only ballet performance group, we seek to showcase NYU ballet dancers and choreographers while fostering an inclusive, creative, and collaborative environment for classically trained students who want to continue their art form in college.
summary: As NYU's only ballet performance group, we seek to showcase NYU ballet dancers and choreographers while fostering an inclusive, creative, and collaborative environment for classically trained students who want to continue their art form in college.

name: Economics Review at NYU - All-University
description:
The Economics Review At NYU is an undergraduate organization that publishes student articles, essays and research papers in an online and print publication. Our content focuses on economic theory, policy and associated fields such as law, business, and politics. Our goal is to provide all interested NYU students with the opportunities to conduct research, get published and advance future career prospects. The student-led editorial staff works with in-house and freelance writers to improve research and writing skills and to produce content that will encourage debate and awareness among undergraduates. We encourage not only the ambitious students of Economics to contribute to the Review, but also all students, regardless of their school, field or degree level. We recruit writers and editors for our online publication at the beginning of each semester.

Applications to join our Online Publication team are closed for the Spring 2026 semester, but will re-open in Fall 2027.
summary: The Economics Review At NYU is an undergraduate organization that publishes student articles, essays and research papers in an online and print publication. Our content focuses on economic theory, policy and associated fields such as law, business, and...

name: Teaching and Learning Club
description:
We are a club for Teaching and Learning students who wish to discuss issues surrounding education, and participate in service projects. We are a club for Steinhardt students who are passionate about education both in the classroom and out. Throughout the year, we host community service and community building events, such as volunteering in schools through our annual book drives, card-making for local hospitals, and more social events like crafting and trivia nights.

Our mission is to connect with the students not only in Teaching and Learning, but anyone in Steinhardt who is interested in education or teaching. We tend to host one to two events per month. Our events will range on date and time each meeting, to try and meet the schedules of everyone. Attendance is not required, but it is encouraged!
summary: We are a club for Teaching and Learning students who wish to discuss issues surrounding education, and participate in service projects.

name: New York Masti - All-University
description: New York Masti, a female South Asian a capella group, aims to bring and integrate the diversity of Indian culture into American culture, among other cultures around the world through music. It aims to grow the community by increasing exposure and awareness of the Indian community at NYU through performances, events, and practices. NY Masti's goal is to bring different genres, methods, and languages of music together around which to build a strong community of musicians and appreciators of music.
summary: New York Masti, a female South Asian a capella group, aims to bring and integrate the diversity of Indian culture into American culture, among other cultures around the world through music. It aims to grow the community by increasing exposure and aware..

name: Undergraduate Nursing Organization
description:
The Undergraduate Nursing Student Organization (UNSO) works to connect students with faculty and staff in order to strengthen the community at NYU Rory Meyers College of Nursing. We seek to give voices to the students so that we can collaborate to identify opportunities for improvement and work with faculty to generate initiatives for positive growth. UNSO members provide social and educational programs that benefit student learning and educational experiences, as well as peer support, and opportunities to enhance nursing education and professional development/leadership experiences.

If you would like to join, please email nursing.studentaffairs@nyu.edu.
summary: The Undergraduate Nursing Student Organization (UNSO) works to connect students with faculty and staff in order to strengthen the community at NYU Rory Meyers College of Nursing. We seek to give voices to the students so that we can collaborate to iden...

name: Uproar Theatre Corps
description:
Uproar is a student theatre club devoted to producing new and existing theatrical works, allowing students to get involved at all levels of theatre production. We aim to create an environment where any and all students can involve themselves in theatre in some capacity. Take a look at our gallery for photos from past productions and check out our events page to see what's coming up for Uproar!

For more information, head to our social media:

https://www.instagram.com/uproartheatre/

https://www.facebook.com/uproartheatre/
summary: Uproar is a student theatre club devoted to producing new and existing theatrical works, allowing students to get involved at all levels of theatre production. Any and all are welcome!

name: Global Spiritual Life at NYU - Department
description: Global Spiritual Life offers religious, spiritual, and ethical communities empowering contemplative tools for self- and community-care, bridge-building, and resilience. We provide opportunities for members of the NYU community to strengthen their sense of belonging, purpose, transcendence, and capacity for storytelling in order to cultivate a Beloved Community.
summary: Global Spiritual Life offers religious, spiritual, and ethical communities empowering contemplative tools for self- and community-care, bridge-building, and resilience.

name: Education Studies Student Committee
description:
The mission of the Education Studies Student Committee (ESSC) is to create a unified community across our student body. We aim to provide academic, social, and professional opportunities that will enrich the undergraduate experience in the Education Studies Program. Through this commitment, we hope to foster the exchange of knowledge and cross-cultural understanding. We aim to build lasting relationships between faculty, alumni, and current students.

Email: steinhardt-essc@nyu.edu
summary: The mission of the Education Studies Student Committee (ESSC) is to create a unified community across our student body and build lasting relationships between faculty, alumni, and current students. We hope to see y'all at one of our events soon!

name: First-Generation Low-Income Partnership - All-University
description: At FLIP, we will address the needs of first-generation, low-income students. Our club will work towards raising awareness of the specific needs of FLIP students ranging from access to educational resources on campus to food insecurity through community building and advocacy. As an effect of our work, we hope to create solutions for these issues and establish a strong voice for our group at NYU.
summary: At FLIP, we will address the needs of first-generation, low-income students. Our club will work towards raising awareness on the specific needs of FLIP students ranging from access to educational resources on campus to food insecurity through community...

name: Supporting Queers and Allies in Dentistry
description: SQuAD is the LGBTQ+ club at NYU Dentistry. Our mission is to foster an inclusive, supportive, and an empowering community for LGBTQ+ students and allies at NYUCD. We strive to create a safe space and promote awareness  for queer rights and issues through educational programs, activism, outreach programs, and social events. We aim to build a campus culture that embraces diversity, challenges discrimination, and advocates equality for all.
summary: SQuAD is NYU Dentistry's LGBTQ+ club. Our mission is to foster an inclusive and empowering community for LGBTQ+ students and allies, creating a safe space and raising awareness through educational programs, activism, outreach, and social events.

name: Asylum Clinic
description: The NYU Grossman School of Medicine Asylum Clinic is a student-led organization that is focused on supporting asylum seekers' applications for asylum by working with physicians to provide medical affidavits. We are also working on establishing continuity with our clients and help them assimilate by connecting them with the longterm care and social support they need.
summary: Student-led organization centered around producing medical affadavits for asylum seekers in support of their application for asylum.

name: Student Alumni Council - All-University
description: The NYU Student Alumni Council (SAC) helps students connect with alumni, provides leadership opportunities, participates in efforts that support the NYU Alumni Association and the NYU community, and promotes NYU pride amongst students. This select group of NYU students has special access to resources and guidance with staff and alumni through serving the NYU alumni community. Students in SAC will take pride in being the keepers of NYU traditions and legacy.
summary: The NYU Student Alumni Council (SAC) helps students connect with alumni, provides leadership opportunities, participates in efforts that support the NYU Alumni Association and the NYU community, and promotes NYU pride amongst students. This select grou...

name: International Association of Emergency Managers
description:
The mission of the International Association of Emergency Managers (IAEM) - USA at NYU is to develop the next generation of emergency management leaders to save lives, protect communities, and foster global resilience. Guided by our core values of innovation, collaboration, leadership, integrity, diversity, and service, we equip students with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities to drive effective disaster response and preparedness through professional development.

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: The mission of the International Association of Emergency Managers (IAEM) - USA at NYU is to develop the next generation of emergency management leaders to save lives, protect communities, and foster global resilience.

name: Department of Social and Cultural Analysis
description: The Department of Social and Cultural Analysis (SCA) hosts seven dynamic programs in Africana Studies, American Studies, Asian/Pacific/American STudies, Gender and Sexuality, Latino Studies, Metropolitan Studies, and Social and Cultural Analysis. SCA combines topics from humanities and methodologies from the social sciences and offers the most cutting-edge programs that are dedicated to advancing progressive interdisciplinary scholarship.
summary: The Department of Social and Cultural Analysis (SCA) hosts seven dynamic programs in Africana Studies, American Studies, Asian/Pacific/American STudies, Gender and Sexuality, Latino Studies, Metropolitan Studies, and Social and Cultural Analysis. SCA c...

name: NYU Tandon Career Services
description:
Resources and events to assist with the job and internship search:
- your online/social media profile 
- sample co-op and internship postings for each major
- networking efficiently 
- job search tips 
- preparing for the interview
- workshops, events and more!
summary: Resources and events to assist with the job and internship search:- your online/social media profile - sample co-op and internship postings for each major- networking efficiently - job search tips - preparing for the interview- workshops, events and more!

name: Steinhardt Undergraduate Student Government
description: The students of the School of Culture, Education, and Human Development elect members to USG’s Executive Board to serve as their governing body. We are the primary representatives, advocates, and liaisons to our school’s community, its administration, faculty, alumni, and students, as well as to the rest of NYU. USG aims to represent and advocate in the interests of students and to foster an environment that enables every matriculated student within the School of Culture, Education, and Human Development to excel academically, professionally, and socially. We also work to create and continue social traditions and programs designed to build community within the entire undergraduate population.
summary: Welcome to the Steinhardt Undergraduate Student Government's online headquarters. This is your resource for all things related to our student government, our mission, events, clubs, and ways to get involved.

name: Macro Social Work Student Network
description:
First, MSWSN will function as the New York University chapter of the nationally-recognized professional organization of the same title. This organization was started by Hunter’s MSW program and connects macro social work students from around the country for professional development, career growth, and networking. Through the diligent efforts of our academic advisor, Dr. Linda Lausell-Bryant, NYU has generously covered membership fees for this organization.

The group will also function as a student group that works closely with the Macro FLO and the Office of Global and Lifelong Learning, among other offices, to continue to expand the macro student voice through student/faculty engagement, as well as through less curriculum-based endeavors such as the creation of additional macro opportunities such as joining Columbia, Fordham, and Hunter by bringing NYU Silver to the United Nations Social Work Day. 

Additionally, we will be working to tackle the circular nature of progressive actions that exists as a result of the relatively brief nature of our program, attempting to instill a process of continuity to counter the natural regression after our cohort's departure into the professional world.
summary: First, MSWSN will function as the New York University chapter of the nationally-recognized professional organization of the same title. This organization was started by Hunter’s MSW program and connects macro social work students from around the countr...

name: Literacy Matters at NYU - All-University
description: Literacy Matters consists of a group of NYU students dedicated to addressing New York City's illiteracy epidemic through various advocacy efforts including facilitating free English Classes in low-income immigrant communities. If you're interested in becoming a fellow literacy advocate, reach out to us to learn about how you can help!
summary: Literacy Matters consists of a group of NYU students dedicated to addressing New York City's illiteracy epidemic through various advocacy efforts including facilitating free English Classes in low-income immigrant communities. If you're interested in b...

name: Gallatin Law Society
description: The Gallatin Law Society aims to provide a social and professional networking space for Gallatin (and interested non-Gallatin) students curious about law, thinking about pursuing a legal career in the future, or on the ‘pre-law track’ at NYU. We will help you through your concentration to best prepare you for law school and internship applications. We will also frequently share resources to support and guide those applying to law school.
summary: To get students excited about law, let them explore how their interdisciplinary concentrations can help them approach legal questions or problems from a unique perspective, and provide a strong support network for students applying to law school.

name: Outdoors Club
description:
The Outdoors Club is a group of medical school students who are looking to foster friendship and fun in the outdoors. The outdoors community is known for being laid-back and inclusive, and it is our goal to welcome everyone into these spaces, whether they may be seasoned wilderness experts or completely new to the outdoors. We seek to foster this camaraderie by hosting trips to local climbing gyms, outdoors activities in and beyond the city, film screenings, and many others. If you're ever feeling like its time for a break from the demands of medical school, feel free to stop by our next event. We would love to have you!

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Our aim is to provide students with opportunities to engage with the outdoors and outdoors recreation! We believe this is an important way to maintain balance while living in NYC, and a great way to make new friends and see old ones.

name: Food Studies Graduate Society
description: The purpose of The Food Studies Graduate Society is to provide support to students of the Food Studies Master’s program at New York University with the intent of creating a greater sense of community within the degree program. This club holds regular meetings and events to keep students engaged and informed of academic, research, and professional opportunities.
summary: The purpose of The Food Studies Graduate Society is to provide support to students of the Food Studies Master’s program at New York University with the intent of creating a greater sense of community within the degree program. This club holds regular m...

name: MarketPlace Collective
description:
The NYU School of Global Public Health (GPH) Marketplace is dedicated to promoting health equity and student well-being by expanding access to nutritious food within the GPH and broader NYU community. Through education, advocacy, and action, we empower students to lead efforts that enhance food access and create a supportive environment. Together, we strive to build a healthier, more resilient NYU community. 

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: We create opportunities for students to build unique communication and storytelling skills that will help them engage their target audiences, amplify their work, and advocate for public health causes.

name: NYU StudentLink Center
description: The StudentLink Center is NYU's improved student services. Our counselors are professionals who are crossed-trained in Registrar, Financial Aid, Bursar, and international student services rather than in just one area. Follow us on Instagram, Facebook, Twitter @NYUStudentlink
summary: The StudentLink Center is NYU's improved student services. Our counselors are professionals who are crossed-trained in Registrar, Financial Aid, Bursar, and international student services rather than in just one area.

name: Black Women's Health Collective
description:
The Black Woman Health Collective (BWHC) aims to support and encourage the leadership of Black women in public health. We do this by highlighting experiences and opportunities related to Black women’s health outcomes. Our events and activities include professional development, discussion panels, volunteering, and many more. We welcome participation from individuals of all backgrounds, understanding that the subject matter of this group centers on the advancement of Black women in the public health industry. 

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: The Black Woman Health Collective (BWHC) aims to support and encourage the leadership of Black women in public health.

name: Dental Mindfulness & Fitness Society
description:
The Dental Mindfulness & Fitness Society at NYU College of Dentistry is dedicated to fostering holistic well-being within the dental community. Our mission is to promote both mental and physical health, empowering members to cultivate a resilient mind and a strong body. By prioritizing mindfulness and fitness, we aim to equip our peers with the tools to manage stress, enhance performance, and thrive in their academic and professional commitments.

To contact us, please email our president at eli2010@nyu.edu

Follow us on instagram @ nyucd.dmfs
summary: The Dental Mindfulness & Fitness Society aims to promote the health awareness in the dental community. The club helps the community build up a strong mind as well as a strong body to fulfill other commitments. If you wish to contact us, please email us...

name: GPH Student Governing Council
description: The Student Governing Council (SGC) is the umbrella student organization for all students and student groups at the School of Global Public Health. The SGC is a group of GPH students (undergraduate, MA, MPH, and Ph.D.) elected to serve on behalf of the general GPH student body. The SGC works to foster a sense of community, promote leadership/professional development opportunities, and oversee the creation of new GPH student groups.
summary: The Student Governing Council (SGC) is the umbrella student organization for all students and student groups at the College of Global Public Health. The SGC is a group of GPH students (undergraduate, MA, MPH, and Ph.D.) elected to serve on behalf of th...

name: Queer Health Collective
description:
The Queer Health Collective (QHC) aims to build a safe community for GPH students, including queer students, by creating an inclusive space that consists of students, staff, and faculty. We will create awareness, representation, and impact at GPH. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds and identities.

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: The NYU Queer Health Collective is a club dedicated to the promotion of queer health at NYU and in NYC. We strive to help our community through organizing, distributing resources, and educating! If you're interested in queer health, join us!

name: Governance Council of Minority and Marginalized Students
description: GCOMMS is a constituency council first conceptualized during the 2015-16 School Year, birthed from the Demands List by the Black & Brown Coalition. It is a space for student leaders representing clubs across all backgrounds to come together and create policy and change.
summary: GCOMMS is a constituency council first conceptualized during the 2015-16 School Year, birthed from the Demands List by the Black & Brown Coalition. It is a space for student leaders representing clubs across all backgrounds to come together and create ...

name: Women in Dentistry
description: Women in Dentistry aims to provide networking, mentorship, outreach, and social opportunities for women in dentistry. We believe this is a necessary and important organization not just for women, but for others as well, so the proper perspective and education can be provided to all of us here at NYUCD as we go out into our professional careers.
summary: Women in Dentistry aims to provide enormous opportunities for women dentists to network, as well as provide mentorship throughout dental school in preparation for our future careers. We believe this is a necessary and important organization not just fo...

name: Club Student Life - Test Club
description:
This is NYU Club Life's test organization to test the Center for Student Life (CSL) and Student Activities Board (SAB) initiatives and for teaching and training purposes. We are not an active student club. If you have any questions, please feel free to email us at clublife@nyu.edu.

Keywords: Test, Club Life,
summary: This organization account is run by NYU Club Life and is not a real club. It exists for teaching and training purposes. If you have SAB club related questions, please email us at clublife@nyu.edu.

name: Steinhardt Graduate Student Organization
description: The Graduate Student Organization (GSO) is the student government for all Steinhardt graduate students. GSO seeks to provide activities and services which assist students in their graduate and post-graduate work. All graduate students enrolled in the Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development are automatically members of the GSO. Our Goals Are: To promote better communication among graduate students, faculty and the administration. To develop programs of cultural, recreational, professional, and intellectual enrichment for graduate students. To provide activities and services which will assist students in their graduate and post-graduate work. To strengthen the Steinhardt community and bridge the relationships between academic programs through diverse programs.
summary: The Graduate Student Organization (GSO) is the student government for all Steinhardt graduate students. GSO seeks to provide activities and services which assist students in their graduate and post-graduate work.

name: Peruvian Student Association - All-University
description: The NYU Peruvian Student Association is an organization that celebrates Peruvian and Andean culture by fostering the rich history and traditions of the country, allowing Peruvians to connect with their community and providing a welcoming space that is available for all members of the NYU community.
summary: NYU Peruvian Student Association. We are an organization that celebrates Peruvian and Andean culture.

name: International Student Council
description:
ISC is the largest club on campus that governs international student affairs, and we represent international students on all levels (undergrad, graduate, PhD). We work directly with student government and the NYU administration to make changes that improve the international student experience. We also host both intimate and large scale events to celebrate the diverse cultures and identities of the international student body and foster a sense of belonging away from home. 

Join us for the 2024-2025 academic year!

See the events and meetings we plan to host for this school year. 

Promote your events and initiatives on our social media

Collaborate with us on events and initiatives
summary: ISC is the largest club on campus that governs international student affairs, and we represent international students on all levels (undergrad, graduate, PhD). We work directly with student government and the NYU administration to make changes that impro

name: Views from NYU
description: Our club produces the NYU College Variety Hour, a Speaker Series and Variety Comedy Television Interview Show at N.Y.U. taped weekly in front of a live studio audience! Our show features interviews with a great range of exciting and prominent guest speakers, and terrific comedy segments!
summary: A SPEAKER SERIES and VARIETY COMEDY TELEVISION INTERVIEW SHOW taped WEEKLY in front of a LIVE STUDIO AUDIENCE at N.Y.U.!

name: Economic Development Group
description: EDG is the primary NYU student-run organization for understanding and engaging with the broad field of international development. Our mission is to connect students with the necessary resources for applying their business skills to economic development opportunities in developing countries.
summary: EDG is the only NYU student-run organization for understanding and engaging with the broad field of international development. Our mission is to connect students with the necessary resources for applying their business skills to economic development op...

name: Future Fashion Group - All-University
description:
Fashion is commonly referred to as one of the world's most polluting industries, with human rights and environmental violations going unpunished. We are dedicated to raising awareness of the social and environmental consequences of textile manufacturing by producing pop-ups, panels, documentary screenings, gatherings, and in house publication FFZINE . Our programming is focused on industry solutions and collaboration throughout various sectors such as apparel, technology, business, and environmentalism. 

FFG looks to broaden the discussion of fashion systems through unique opportunities and professional partnerships to identify key opportunities for future development of critical components for a successful and sustainable fashion industry.

To stay updated, SIGN UP FOR OUR NEWSLETTER HERE !
summary: Fashion is commonly referred to as one of the world's most polluting industries, with human rights and environmental violations going unpunished. We are dedicated to raising awareness of the social and environmental consequences of textile.

name: Health and Human Rights Association
description:
The Health & Human Rights Association (HHRA) aims to foster a community around issues of social justice within the New York University (NYU), New York City, and the global community. We intend to raise awareness among NYU students, faculty, staff, and other professionals regarding the complete interdependence of health and human rights to bridge the gap between scholarship, research, policy, and practice.

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: The Health & Human Rights Association aims to foster a community around issues of social justice within New York University (NYU), New York City, and the global community. We intend to raise awareness among NYU students, faculty, and staff.

name: Urban Design & Architecture Society - All-University
description: We are a group of architecture & urban design enthusiasts passionate about cities, design, and our built environment at large. At UDAS, we welcome students from all schools, backgrounds, and interests, hosting events in and out of the city. Between lectures, sketching sessions, walking tours, professional workshops and social events, there's surely something for everyone regardless of if you intend to pursue the field professionally. And if you are, look no further: our past and future continue to explore the best New York City has to offer and has introduced members to worlds of all types. Students are encouraged to build their networks here with speaker panels, career fairs, and alumni networking - come find your studiomates!
summary: We are a group of architecture & urban design enthusiasts passionate about the built environment. Join us if you're interested in exploring the true heart of the urban core we call New York City, whether hobby or profession! All schools welcome!

name: Black Women's Social Work Coalition
description: The Black Women's Social Work Coalition seeks to cultivate a space for holistic support within NYU Silver’s School of Social Work. Additionally, we aim to implement a lively network that facilitates connection between current students and alumni to share resources and further our development within the Social Work profession. The coalition strives for sustainability and continuity as members transition from the academic program to other impactful spaces in academia, the community, the workforce and the profession as a whole.
summary: The Black Women's Social Work Coalition seeks to cultivate a space for holistic support within NYU Silver’s School of Social Work.

name: Tri-Service Military Dental Club
description: A student organization structured to support military­ sponsored students and veterans in dental school, provide information to current and potential dental officer candidates about serving in the military, to participate in promoting oral health in the community and to support and give back to military veterans.
summary: A student organization structured to support military­ sponsored students in dental school, provide information to current and potential dental officer candidates about serving in the military, to participate in promoting oral health in the community a...

name: Sprout Up - All-University
description: Sprout Up provides free, youth-led environmental education programs to 1st and 2nd grade classrooms in public schools throughout California and New York City. Our college student-instructors teach children vital concepts in environmental science and sustainability during the earliest stages of their educational development, cultivating the next generation of passionate caretakers of the earth. Through activities, experiments, and discussions, we get kids thinking in new ways about the world that surrounds them, inspiring them to bring the message of environmental stewardship home to their families and friends. In this way, we strive to promote sustainability throughout our many communities, from the youngest members of society up.
summary: Sprout Up provides free, youth-led environmental education programs to 1st and 2nd grade classrooms in public schools throughout California and New York City. Our college student-instructors teach children vital concepts in environmental science and su...

name: HRSA Grant - Student Programs at NYU Dentistry
Medical Students for a Sustainable Future
description:
The impact of the environment on health cuts across all specialties. We explore this multidisciplinary topic through lectures, workshops, and advocacy projects related to community and environmental health.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The impact of the environment on health cuts across all specialties. We explore this multidisciplinary topic through lectures, workshops, and advocacy projects related to community and environmental health.

name: Nutrition Without Borders
description:
Nutrition without Borders (NwB) is a student-led organization dedicated to domestic and global food, nutrition, and health equity issues. Our work intends to highlight how race, gender, class, age, ability status, sexual orientation, gender identity, and immigration status can impact access to adequate nutrition and the broader role nutrition plays in public health. The mission of NwB is to increase student involvement in these issues through advocacy, education, and outreach, starting with the New York University community. 

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: Our mission is to increase student involvement in domestic and global nutrition issues through advocacy, research, volunteering, and outreach, starting with New York University’s School of Global Public Health (GPH) community.

name: Cardiothoracic Surgery Interest Group
description:
The Cardiothoracic Surgery Club, organized with the NYU Department of Cardiothoracic Surgery, is an enthusiastic community dedicated to nurturing student interest in cardiothoracic surgery. Students are exposed to a broad range of topics, diseases, and interventions in CT surgery through a series of talks, workshops, and panels. Mentorship, shadowing, and research opportunities with CT faculty and fellows are encouraged and facilitated. Ultimately, the CT Surgery club hopes to foster an appreciation for what it means (and what it takes) to become a cardiothoracic surgeon.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Cardiothoracic Surgery Club, organized with the NYU Department of Cardiothoracic Surgery, is an enthusiastic community dedicated to nurturing student interest in cardiothoracic surgery. Students are exposed to a broad range of topics, diseases, and...

name: Tisch School of the Arts Film and Television Techs
Urology Interest Group
description:
The Urology Interest Group functions to expose students to a fascinating and important field that receives very little coverage in the medical school curriculum. Past events include faculty meet and greets and dinner lectures on reconstructive urology, bladder cancer, and other urologic subspecialties. Once a semester, we organize a surgical skills lab with urology residents so that students can learn about laparoscopic techniques.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Interest group for students who are interested in the surgical subspecialty of Urology.

name: Violet Diabolo - All-University
description: Violet Diabolo is dedicated to spreading the performance art of diabolo (Chinese yo-yo) to the NYU community. We want to provide the opportunity for students and locals to experience the growing recreational sport of diabolo in an active and cultural manner through weekly practices and performances. We are an inclusive community for all those who want to play the diabolo.
summary: Violet Diabolo is dedicated to spreading the performance art of diabolo (Chinese yo-yo) to the NYU community. We want to provide the opportunity for students and locals to experience the growing recreational sport of diabolo in an active and cultural way

name: Step Team at NYU - All-University
description: Stepping is a form of rhythmic and percussive dance where your body is the instrument. This is done through forms of clapping and stomping! Step Team is open to anyone and no experience is required. We are a student run organization dedicated to fostering an inclusive and safe community at NYU.
summary: Stepping is a form of percussive dance in which your body is the instrument. We use a variety of footsteps, hand clapping, knee slapping and spoken word to make rhythms and sounds. Step Team is open to anyone and no experience is required. Stepping is ...

name: Student Research Group
description: The Student Research Group (SRG) of NYU Dentistry is a local chapter of the National Student Research Group (NSRG). NSRG is an affiliate of the American Association of Dental Research (AADR). We aim to help connect students to the various research opportunities, awards, and national/international presentations available. Our goal is to streamline research participation for students making it more accessible and allowing our community to become evidence-based future practitioners. SRG holds events including: journal clubs, research symposiums, annual research Q & As, and mock poster presentations.
summary: The Student Research Group (SRG) of NYU Dentistry is a local chapter of the National Student Research Group (NSRG). NSRG is an affiliate of the American Association of Dental Research (AADR). We aim to help connect students to the various research oppo...

name: Left Handed Student Association
description: The LHSA will provide invaluable support and guidance to the left-handed dental students of NYUCD in several ways. First, by organizing a community of (mostly) left-handed students, we will be able to provide members with a list of faculty, TAs, and other students who are left-handed and able to assist them as questions emerge, whether it be in bench-lab, or clinic. Additionally, we hope to work with the D1 GDS and D2 CRI and CRII course coordinators to try to ensure that there will be at least one left-handed TA in each lab section. Further, we will hold tutorials aimed at helping students learn about and become comfortable with hand positions used for different procedures/teeth. As these tutorials will mostly serve the D1 and D2 students, we also hope to conduct lunch and learns that will be useful to all years. Topics may include facilitating work with associates and especially assistants whose dominant hand is opposite of those whom they work with, and improving dexterity in one’s non-dominant hand. Additionally, we will provide demonstrations on converting the operatories from the default right-handed dentist set-up to a left-handed dentist set-up. Consequently, the LHSA will make advice, guidance, resources, and hands-on instruction more accessible to left-handed dental students.
summary: The LHSA will provide invaluable support and guidance to the left-handed dental students of NYUCD in several ways. First, by organizing a community of (mostly) left-handed students, we will be able to provide members with a list of faculty, TAs, and ot...

name: ART Collective
description: Our mission is to support and nurture the strong community of creators of art, music, and film at NYU Dentistry, and provide a beautiful art experience for the NYU Dentistry student community.
summary: Our mission is to provide all art, music and theatre related entertainment within the school. Our scope has no boundaries and always welcome new ideas

name: T-Party - All-University
description: The NYU LGBTQ+ Center-affiliated club for transgender, gender nonconforming, and questioning NYU students (and their allies) to meet each other and chat!
summary: The NYU LGBTQ+ Center-affiliated club for transgender, gender nonconforming, and questioning NYU students (and their allies) to meet each other and chat!

name: Academy of General Dentistry Student Chapter at NYU Dentistry
description:
Academy of General Dentistry (AGD), as the second-largest dental organization nationwide, offers an exciting platform for dental students at NYU Dentistry to forge connections with like-minded general dentists in the vibrant Greater New York area right from their school years.

             AGD's student chapter at NYU Dentistry serves as a valuable resource for students seeking to accrue continuing education (CE) credits while still in school. These credits can be meticulously recorded within the official AGD transcript, paving the way for pursuing a prestigious Fellowship Award with AGD—an accomplishment that can set a dentist apart in their career journey.

             The AGD student chapter at NYU Dentistry is all about collaboration and learning. They organize multiple guest lecture events covering a wide range of topics proposed by members each year. So, don't miss out! Join us and become a part of the AGD family, where you can immerse yourself in the world of general dentistry together with a community of passionate professionals.
summary: Academy of General Dentistry (AGD), as the second-largest dental organization nationwide, offers an exciting platform for dental students at NYU Dentistry to forge connections with like-minded general dentists in the vibrant Greater New York area.

name: Horticulture Club
description:
We aim to support medical student well-being and support a love of horticulture through plant-related events and workshops.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: We aim to support medical student well-being and support a love of horticulture through plant-related events and workshops.

name: Peer Tutoring
description:
PLEASE EMAIL THE TUTOR TO CONFIRM ATTENDANCE PRIOR TO THE SESSION! Emails are at the bottom of this page.

NYU College of Dentistry Peer Tutoring is a service provided through the Office of Student Engagement. Peer Tutors were carefully selected to tutor you, and participate in an honors course on teaching and learning.

  • To help Peer Tutors prepare, please RSVP at least 48 hours before a session
  • If you are unable to attend a session you signed up for, please change your RSVP and email the Peer Tutor so they know not to expect you
summary: NYU College of Dentistry Peer Tutoring is a service provided through the Office of Student Affairs and Academic Support Services.

name: Middle East Business Association
description: MEBA explores enterprise and capital markets in the emerging Middle East while promoting a better understanding of the region and fostering a community of like-minded individuals within Stern and broader NYU. We aim to bring together students and faculty members to better understand: key business sectors in the emerging Middle East, their growing roles within the global financial system and their adaptation to unique sets of geopolitical circumstances. Through club meetings, and networking and speaker events, this club would play an important role in connecting students with increasingly lucrative job opportunities in the Middle East, and expand Stern’s representation in companies operating in the region.
summary: The Global Islamic Financial industry is one of the fastest growing financial sectors worldwide, valued at $2 trillion and growing at 15-20% each year. With a $580 billion halal food market, a $280 billion Islamic fashion market, and over 50,000 new jo...

summary: Digital Learning at NYU Tandon

name: WoMentorship Program
description: The WoMentorship Program matches sophomore and junior undergraduate women with upper-class women, and first-year graduate women with second-year graduate women. Mentor and mentees meet at least eight times each semester and engage in social activities, academic opportunities, and career and professional development. Mentors and their mentees independently plan one-on-one meetings and activities, and attend campus events and programs together.
summary: The WoMentorship Program matches sophomore and junior undergraduate women with upper-class women, and first-year graduate women with second-year graduate women. Mentor and mentees meet at least eight times each semester and engage in social activities,...

name: Student Health Center
description:
NYU's Student Health Center (SHC) is a campus resource and service center for all matriculated students. We offer universal, hassle-free appointment-based and walk-in medical, counseling, and disability services at either no cost or very reduced cost to all NYU students, regardless of insurance coverage. Our goal is to add great value to the NYU experience for students and great comfort in knowing that a safety net for basic healthcare exists at NYU.

The SHC is accredited by the Accreditation Association for Ambulatory Health Care, placing it among the top university health centers in the country.
summary: NYU's Student Health Center (SHC) is a campus resource and service center for all matriculated students. We offer universal, hassle-free appointment-based and walk-in medical, counseling, and disability services at either no cost or very reduced cost t...

description: The Student Professionalism and Ethics Association in Dentistry is a national, student-driven association that was established to promote and support a student’s lifelong commitment to ethical behavior in order to benefit the patients they serve and further the dental profession.

description:
We used to go to museums and have fun. 

But given the circumstances, we are organizing arts workshops virtually and having speakers talk about digital art and art during pandemic. Let art help us through this difficult but interesting time.
summary: We go to museums and have fun!

The STCs provide access to a variety of technology resources including specialized software, printing, video conferencing, video production suites, group study spaces, and more.
summary: NYU has three Student Technology Centers (STCs) in New York that are accessible to degree-seeking students, as well as faculty. The STCs provide access to a variety of technology resources including specialized software, printing, video conferencing, v...

To expose students to applied programming skills and topics that are relevant to computation and data in healthcare.

name: Falun Dafa at NYU - All-University
description:
Falun Dafa at NYU (The Club) creates opportunities for the NYU community to learn and practice the exercises and belief system of Falun Dafa, a traditional meditation style from China with a moral philosophy centered on the principles of Truthfulness, Compassion, and Forbearance, or in Chinese, 真 (zhen), 善 (shan), and 忍 (ren).

summary: After School Improv is Tisch's premier improv troupe. We perform monthly shows at NYU and are featured in festivals/tournaments at Columbia, Skidmore, Harvard, CIT, and more. We're also a group of really close friends that just love to laugh :).

name: Space Exploration Society at NYU
description: The central mission of the Space Exploration Society is to bring together students who are interested in space, rocketry, space law, space technology, and anything else that has to do with exploring the final frontier. Our goal is to not only stimulate existing interest, but to also inspire newcomers to join in mankind’s drive for discovery in the universe.
summary: The central mission of SES is to bring together students who are interested in space, rocketry, space law, space technology, and anything else that has to do with exploring the final frontier.

name: New Major Records
description:
New Major Records is NYU Gallatin’s music community, dedicated to creating a space for Gallatin and NYU musician to be heard! Our goal is to provide Gallatin students as well as the rest of the university with a space to create, collaborate, and connect with others interested in music.

Whether you make music, produce it, write about it, market it, or simply love it, join the club to get to know the NMR community!

More info at https://linktr.ee/newmajorrecords

summary: The Private Equity Group's mission is to better inform undergraduate students about the private equity industry, learn about recruiting into the private equity industry, and provide students the tools and resources to be successful.

description:
Welcome to the Recreation Committee – your gateway to shared adventures! Unite with like-minded peers as we dive into spin classes, epic sporting spectacles, harmonious jam sessions, exhilarating amusement parks and so much more!

We're the driving force behind NYU intramural sports, rallying SPS team members for the exhilarating ALL-University games. Join us for unforgettable moments of unity and excitement!

Spring 2025 Intramural Registration Available

 

Please fill in our new member interest form here.

name: Association for Computing Machinery for Women
description: The Association for Computing Machinery for Women's (ACM-W) is an association that celebrates, supports and informs women in the STEM field. We host tech workshops, bring in guest speakers from the industry, and host community-building activities.
summary: The Association for Computing Machinery for Women's (ACM-W) is an association that celebrates, supports and informs women in the STEM field. We host tech workshops, bring in guest speakers from the industry, and host community-building activities.

description: Through showcasing student research, Compass strives to review, publish and archive the best academic and creative work produced by NYU students. It is our hope that Compass will inspire and support a new generation of innovative, interdisciplinary researchers.
summary: The Gallatin research journal.

name: NYU Tandon MakerSpace
description: The NYU Tandon MakerSpace is a cutting edge lab created to foster collaborative design projects. The space is open to all NYU students, staff, and faculty. It features rapid prototyping and PCB production equipment, as well as advanced machining and testing capabilities. The MakerSpace was designed to support and encourage project-based learning at all levels and provides training and workshops on machine usage, prototyping skills, and design. Usage of the MakerSpace equipment has been integrated into the curriculum of several departments throughout the Tandon School of Engineering. It serves as a tech hub connecting NYU students to the broader NYC tech community, the NYU Future Labs, and local incubators and startups. The space also supports the local community by hosting many courses and design projects from the Center for K12 STEM Education. Diversity of use and thought are encouraged, with over 3000 users from 18 different schools across the university using the space in its inaugural year.
summary: The NYU Tandon MakerSpace is a cutting edge lab created to foster collaborative design projects. The space is open to all NYU students, staff, and faculty. It features rapid prototyping and PCB production equipment, as well as advanced machining and te...

name: Undergraduate Law Review at NYU - All-University
description: The Undergraduate Law Review at New York University, founded in 2017, provides a platform for the publication of exceptional legal scholarship for students at NYU. The journal publishes online articles throughout the academic year as well as one printed volume publication each semester. To find out more information about seasonal recruitment and the editorial review process, visit ulrnyu.org.
summary: The Undergraduate Law Review at New York University, founded in 2017, provides a platform for the publication of exceptional legal scholarship for students at NYU. The journal publishes online articles throughout the academic year as well as one printe...

name: All Asian Arts Alliance
description:
We are the All Asian Arts Alliance, a coalition of artists building community, collaborating, and celebrating art informed by the Asian Experience. All of our programming is open to all students.

We currently have branches in Drama, Dramatic Writing, and Film. We've held events with AAPI artist pioneers including Qui Nguyen, Nimesh Patel, David Henry Hwang, and more! There is no formal joining process, just join our roster, mailing list, follow our Instagram, and hop into our meetings and events. 

We're currently operating through our mailing list where we send out links to events, meetings, and collaboration opportunities. Fill out the form^ to join up! 

We meet biweekly on Thursdays at 7:00PM EST. 

Last updated: 10/11/2023

*feel free to contact us at allasianartsalliance@gmail.com with any inquiries
summary: We build community, collaborate, and celebrate art informed by the Asian Experience. We host several events a month featuring workshops, panels, and other events, often in collaboration with professional Asian-Identifying artists.

name: Project Crescendo - All-University
description: Project Crescendo is a community of NYU students that love music! Our goal is to provide NYU students with meaningful musical opportunities for teaching, performing, and collaborating with peers. Some of the activities we do include volunteering to teach instruments to elementary school students and NYU peers for free, performing at hospitals, and playing together in chamber groups/bands.
summary: Project Crescendo empowers students at NYU to grow and connect with their communities through musical performance, collaboration, and service — while bringing the joy of music to every corner of the city.

name: Artists in Action
description:
Artists in Action is a student-led organization at the NYU Tisch School of the Arts committed to connecting student-artists with opportunities to tangibly impact their local communities through art, advocacy, and service.

 

To get involved, email us at artistsinaction.nyu@gmail.com or join our mailing list to receive updates. 

Linktree
summary: Our mission is to connect, inspire, and create -- empowering Tisch students to turn their art into community impact and social change.

name: Economics Society - All-University
description: The Economics Society intends to provide a platform for -ALL- New York University students, irrespective of school, to learn about economics and business through a variety of activities, as well as to strengthen communication between NYU students and faculty in the Economics Department. We partner with premier corporate partners (Deloitte Consulting, EY Parthenon, Houlihan Lokey), public sector leaders (Federal Reserve), as well as faculty and other student organizations to deliver information, and networking opportunities to students across the entire university. We've held debates and discussed research and contemporary economic affairs with students and faculty alike. Other events include: alumni panels, movie nights, and social gatherings.
summary: The Economics Society intends to provide a platform for all New York University students, irrespective of school, to learn about economics through a variety of activities, as well as to strengthen communication between NYU students and faculty

name: New York Dance Marathon
description: NYDM is NYU's biggest student-run philanthropy. It is a year-long effort to raise awareness and funds to help fight childhood cancer. Every Spring, this effort culminates in a 6-hour dance marathon. The funds support The Andrew McDonough B+ Foundation, which provides financial and emotional assistance to affected families, and funds research initiated with the primary goal of alleviating the secondary pains of childhood cancer.
summary: New York Dance Marathon, NYU's biggest student-run philanthropy benefitting the B+ Foundation and the Fight Against Childhood Cancer

name: Puerto Rican Student Association - All-University
description: PRÁ provides a space for Puerto Rican students and all other students interested in Puerto Rican culture to network, connect and learn about Puerto Rico. We unite the diaspora and the island to create an open and conscious community. We discuss our current and historical issues and identity as well as celebrate our culture. We create a community in and of the city and in and of Puerto Rico.
summary: PRÁ provides the space for Puerto Rican students and all other students interested in Puerto Rican culture to network, connect and learn about Puerto Rico. We unite the diaspora and the island to create an open and conscious community. We discuss our c...

name: Broke People Play Festival
description:
The Broke People Play Festival is NYU's largest student-run play festival. Every semester, we produce a One Act festival featuring 5-9 fully produced One Acts plays acted, directed, and written by students all across the NYU community. Additionally, we present 2-4 Full Length and Developmental plays. Through our process, we bring together writers, actors, and directors to create new and exciting creative works. 

 

Follow us on Instagram @brokepplplayfest for updates!
summary: The Broke People Play Festival is NYU's largest student-run play festival. Every semester, we produce student-written, acted, and directed works as part of our One-Act Festival or Full Length Series. Follow us on Instagram @brokepplplayfest for updates!

name: Friends With Dads
summary: Friends with Dads is NYU’s only variety comedy group. Open to ALL students, we do sketch, stand up, musical, political, really anything you can think of. We write and create our own shows every month at NYU and perform at festivals around the city.

name: CAS Student Council
description: The students of the College of Arts and Science elect the CAS Student Council to serve as their governing body. We are the primary representatives, advocates, and liaisons to the CAS Community- its administration, faculty, alumni, and students- as well as to the rest of NYU. We have a two-fold mission: Student Advocacy and Programming. We strive to gather and express student opinions, actively represent student views, appropriately address student concerns, and provide a forum for the student voice. We also work to create and continue social traditions and programs designed to foster cohesiveness within the entire undergraduate population.
summary: CAS Student Council members are the primary representatives, advocates, and liaisons to the CAS Community--its administration, faculty, alumni, and students--as well as to the rest of NYU.

name: Italian Club at NYU
description:
Benvenuti, studenti. Welcome!

 

We are the NYU Italian Club and we welcome you with open arms, just like your nonna. 

 

But on a serious note, whatever your background, whatever your major, whatever your italo-americana family has to say about your new tattoo or piercing, come hang with us! We will be going on field-trips throughout the city to Italian-themed locations... think museums, restaurants, cooking classes, etc.. Readings and interviews at NYU's Casa Italiana will be part of the club too!

 

No knowledge of the Italian Language needed whatsoever... that's what the Italian Conversation Club is for. We also have an Instagram @italianclubnyu. A presto!
summary: Benvenuti a tutti! Learn, reflect, and engage in Italian Culture! Take it all in and experience Italy from the big city!

name: Academic Achievement Program
description:
The Academic Achievement Program (AAP) promotes, facilitates, and advocates for a welcoming campus community by centering the experiences of Black, Latine, and Indigenous students. AAP provides a family atmosphere where students can make lasting friendships, seek refuge from classes and daily stresses, and push their limits to achieve their potential in all aspects of their academic journey at NYU.

Additionally, AAP serves as a platform for personal growth and support. Our curriculum is designed to cultivate leadership skills through engaging workshops, experiential learning, and community service activities. We offer numerous opportunities for students to broaden their horizons and learn not only about leadership but also about culture, interpersonal connections, and self-discovery.
summary: The Academic Achievement Program (AAP) promotes, facilitates, and advocates for a welcoming campus community by centering the experiences of Black, Latine, and Indigenous students. AAP provides a family atmosphere where students make lasting friendships.

name: Phi Alpha Delta
description:
Hello and welcome to Phi Alpha Delta, NYU's Pre-Law Fraternity! 

We are an international, pre-professional, and co-ed fraternity that aims to form a bond between undergraduate students that are interested in pursuing law school. Through various academic, professional, and social events, we wish to promote the welfare of our members and prepare them as they embark on the next step in their journey toward a career in the legal field. Our members are interested in a range of specialties and we program with those specific areas of interest in mind. We are an all-inclusive club and invite students of all backgrounds and experiences to join. 

Follow us on instagram @nyu_pad to stay in the loop!

7 Sage x PAD Partnership 

Members of PAD will receive half off of their first month of any 7Sage subscription! You can make a free 7Sage account using their school email address, then email studentservice@7sage.com and ask to apply 50% off their first month through the discount for Student Group Partnerships. Make sure to mention NYU and PAD in the email.
 
Please note: To use a Live subscription, students must have a LawHub Advantage account through LSAC ($120/year). This is a licensing fee required to access official LSAT materials and is required to use any prep platform on the market.
summary: Phi Alpha Delta, The Pre-Law Fraternity of NYU. Our organization assists undergraduate students in becoming well-rounded young professionals who feel supported in their journey to law school.

name: NYU Mathematics Society
description: We’re crazy about math! Regardless of whether you’re new to NYU or simply just curious about mathematics, you’re more than welcome to drop by one of our events. We strive to learn more about the subject and its applications outside of the classroom through social events as well as lectures and sharing of research, often by NYU Courant’s own established professors. We’re not strictly an academic club so math majors and non-majors alike are all welcome. Drop in to chill, mingle and make friends, all in the spirit of learning! Check our emails below for contact information or to join the club.
summary: The official Mathematics Society for undergraduates at NYU. Open to all majors and years, with opportunities to socialize and attend special seminars, as well as run the annual math-outreach event, cSplash!

name: Pre-Dental Society
description: If you’re trying to become a dentist, or you’re pre-med and interested in exploring your options, join Pre-Dental Society! Come meet fellow pre-dental students and attend informative events about applying to dental school, the DAT, the life of a dentist/dental student, and have questions about your path as an undergraduate answered. There are typically 4 events a semester, ranging from demonstrations to guest speakers to dental student panels, so you don't want to miss out! We have a new mentorship program with NYU dental students, and a DAT Bootcamp discount code to offer, so be sure to become a member on NYU Engage to receive news on events and opportunities.
summary: The Pre-Dental Club's mission is to provide an outlet where students can explore the dental profession.

name: Phi Delta Epsilon
description: Phi Delta Epsilon is an international medical fraternity that fosters a community for those who are on the journey to medical school. PhiDE NY Theta chapter volunteers with Children's Miracle Network hospitals, fundraises for CMN, hosts discussions led by doctors and researchers, supports a peer-tutoring network, offers guidance, holds field days, organizes social events, and much more. We enable you to make lifelong friendships with fellow pre-med students who will one day become physicians of integrity like yourself.
summary: PhiDE NY Theta Chapter is NYU's premier medical fraternity. Phi Delta Epsilon is an international co-ed medical fraternity that fosters a community for those who are on the journey to medical school.

name: Guitar Fingerstyle Club
description: NYU GFC is an official CAS student club focusing on popularizing guitar fingerstyle, a special artistic form of guitar playing, in NYU community. We hope to connect students from different cultural environment together through holding musical concerts, where all students, professors, and faculty members are welcome to go to. In our minds, music is not constrained by languages or certain kinds of shared cultural identities, but serves as the key to unlock the accesses to different cultural backgrounds. We are looking forward to seeing you all in our upcoming concerts!
summary: NYU GFC is an official CAS student club focusing on popularizing guitar fingerstyle, a special artistic form of guitar playing, in NYU community. We hope to connect students from different cultural environment together through holding musical concerts,...

name: American Medical Students Association
description:
Est. 1950, the American Medical Student Association (AMSA) is the oldest and largest independent association of physicians-in-training in the United States with attention and broader participation from students internationally. 

 

The AMSA New York University Chapter is a dynamic group that embodies this progress and ambition in the realm of healthcare. As the largest premedical organization at NYU, we curate events and opportunities in every aspect of career development for our members throughout the semester, open to all students passionate about medicine, propelling them toward a future of excellence. Through captivating engagements with distinguished healthcare professionals, hands-on experiences, and unparalleled guidance, we empower aspiring medical practitioners to conquer the challenging pre-medical track.

 

Embracing the mission of the American Medical Student Association, we strive to build a group of ambitious and equally supportive community leaders to push the boundaries of medical education, and address the social, moral, and ethical obligations of the medical profession. With a global perspective, we tackle pressing health challenges, fostering deeper understanding, and propelling the welfare and growth of medical students, premedical students, interns, residents, and post-MD/DO trainees.

 

Together, we cultivate a vibrant sense of belonging and empowerment that goes beyond the confines of academic pursuit. Beyond the confines of our events, we create opportunities for lasting friendships, social connections, mentorship, and personal development, fostering a network that extends far beyond graduation. We are here with you, every step of the way, empowering you to unleash your full potential and create meaningful change.

 

Let's get started.

 

LEADERSHIP DIRECTORY

Serena Kher

Vice President

Aparna Saha

Director of Finance

summary: The AMSA NYU Chapter is the local chapter of a prestigious international network of ambitious premedical student. As the largest premed organization at NYU, we curate opportunities in every aspect of health career development for our members throughout..

name: Anthropology Undergraduate Student Association
description:
We are the Anthropology Undergraduate Student Association at NYU (AUSA)! Our goal is to facilitate and encourage interest in anthropology, and to showcase the multi-faceted nature of the discipline. We hold weekly events and meetings, at which we have discussions, panels with professors and graduate students, outings to NYC museums and cultural activities, and more. If you are interested in Sociocultural Anthropology, Archaeology, Biological Anthropology, Linguistic Anthropology, or some combination of the four, this is the club for you! 

 

If you have any questions about AUSA, feel free to email Alfie: tc3595@nyu.edu

 

To join the email list, email tc3595@nyu.edu 

 

P.S.: Follow our Instagram for weekly updates @nyuausa!
summary: We are the Anthropology Undergraduate Student Association at NYU!

name: Pre-SOMA
description: Pre-SOMA is NYU's only osteopathic medical association. Our goal is to provide an on campus organization to represent and bring awareness to osteopathic medicine. We offer volunteer events, shadowing opportunities, osteopathic medical school tours, admission panels, and much more!

name: Fine Arts Society
description: The Fine Arts Society is a student-organized College of Arts and Science club with the goal of creating a community of students interested in art history and all visual culture. The society extends membership to all NYU students. Through visits to New York City museums and galleries, as well as walking tours, lectures, symposia, and film screenings, the Fine Arts Society brings art history majors and non-majors together, through lively discussions and a wide variety of events. In addition, the group publishes a newsletter at the beginning of every month during the academic year, with information on mostly free arts-related events in and around New York City happening that month. The group's annual highlights include a Study Party, held every December on Reading Day before final examinations, and a Career Symposium, convened every spring. The Fine Arts Society also encourages students to participate in other events and public programs hosted by NYU, especially those at the Grey Art Gallery located on the main floor of the Silver Center (entrance at 100 Washington Square East).
summary: The Fine Arts Society is a student-organized College of Arts and Science club with the goal of creating a community of students interested in art history and all visual culture.  The society extends membership to all NYU students. Through visits to New...

name: History Society
description: NYU History Society serves as a resourceful organization for those undergraduates pursuing advanced degrees in Law, History, Policy, and other fields in the humanities. Society members are connected with professors and educational opportunities in order to promote leadership and growth within the undergraduate history community at NYU.
summary: NYU History Society serves as an organization for those undergraduates pursuing careers in History, Law, and other humanities fields. Members are connected with professors and opportunities in order to promotes growth in the undergrad community at NYU.

name: Society for Undergraduate Neural Science
description:
Through NYU SUNS, you can further your interest in neuroscience outside of the classroom. Join a community of NYU students with common interests, regardless of your major/area of study!

We serve as the main neuroscience interest group primarily for Neural Science majors, but all students interested in neuroscience are welcome. We host a variety of events including faculty talks, research Q&A panels, career-building workshops, review sessions, community outreach opportunities, and much more.

 

Please contact the NYU SUNS E-board if you have questions!

Irene Chen, President
