name: International Relations Society
description:
The International Relations Society is an undergraduate club at New York University’s College of Arts and Science.

The IR Society partners with NYU’s Politics Department to bring together students who are passionate about international affairs, economics, and global news events. Through panels and discussions with some of the most prominent voices in politics and international relations, we aim to encourage interest in – and knowledge of – international affairs beyond the classroom. Our past events have explored women’s rights, education and immigration in Afghanistan, human trafficking, landmines, and more. IR Society alumni have gone on to intern and work for Morgan UN, Morgan Stanley, the Council on Foreign Relations, Human Rights Watch, CNN, MSNBC, and more! 

IR INSIDER is New York University’s student-run international relations publication, powered by the IR Society. 

At the INSIDER, we produce up-to-date news articles on the day’s most important global issues and topics. With nine regional sections, two “ideas'' sections, and event reporting, the INSIDER is a journalistic hub and go-to source for the international affairs you need to know. Bringing together writers and editors from a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, majors, and regional interests, the INSIDER tells today’s top stories— from Baghdad to Berlin — through an innovative and always reliable lens.
summary: The International Relations (IR) Society at NYU is an undergraduate club in CAS partnered with the Politics Department. It seeks to bring together International Relations, Politics, Economics, and Area Studies majors/minors as well as anyone else inter...

name: Dean's Service Honor Corps
description: The Dean's Service Honor Corps (DSHC) is the CAS Scholar Based Community Service Scholar Group, which is dedicated to service and service learning. As a student led group, we plan opportunities for students to connect with local community service organizations, as well as easy access community service opportunities. We also plan scholar lectures which allow us to think critically about service, and other intellectual pursuits.
summary: The Dean's Service Honor Corps (DSHC) is the CAS Scholars Community Service Group, which is dedicated to service and service learning. As a student led group, we plan opportunities for students to connect  with local community service organizations.

name: Ink & Image
description: Ink & Image is the NYU Art History Department's student-run, student-published academic journal for original undergraduate research in art history, architecture, and urban design. Submissions for the spring 2025 issue of Ink & Image are now closed. Applications for the journal's E-Board are also now closed. To submit to the 2026 issue of Ink & Image, please check back in fall 2025.
summary: Ink & Image is the NYU Art History Department's student-run, student-published academic journal for original undergraduate research in art history, architecture, and urban design.

name: French Club at NYU
description: The goal of the NYU French Club is to foster community, develop passion, and increase interest for the French language and culture within the NYU community. The club will offer an environment in which individuals with a passion for the French language and/or culture can befriend other French speakers and help one another grow. The club will also offer numerous events monthly that will allow students to learn more about the French language and culture and will enable them to practice speaking French in a stress-free environment. The club initiatives will help create an inclusive community because its acceptance of all levels of French speakers, from beginner to fluent, and with varying levels of understanding of French culture will create a sense of inclusion and companionship for people who wish to learn and improve their French capabilities. This will build interest within the community and establish a foundation for the club, as we want students to know that this club is a safe space where they can reignite their interests in French, regarding both the language and culture, in a non-classroom setting.
summary: The goal of the NYU French Club is to foster community, develop passion, and increase interest for the French language and culture within the NYU community. The club will offer an environment in which individuals with a passion for the French language ...

name: Delta Epsilon Mu
description: Delta Epsilon Mu (DEM) is the nation's premiere co-ed pre-health fraternity dedicated to uniting students interested in various health professions, including but not limited to pre-medical students, pre-dental students, nursing students, psychology majors, and pre-veterinary students. DEM was founded on the pillars of loyalty, dedication, friendship, and support in May of 1996 at Binghamton University in Binghamton, New York. In late November of 2019, ten motivated and tenacious students successfully completed DEM's Colony phase and were granted a charter for the newly established Alpha-Tau Chapter. Joining DEM will allow you to further develop leadership skills, as well as interact with students interested in the various health fields because at the end of the day, healthcare providers work as a team to ensure the best care for their patients.
summary: Delta Epsilon Mu (DEM) is a the nation's premiere co-ed pre-health fraternity dedicated to uniting students interested in various health professions including but not limited to pre-medical students, pre-dental students, nursing students, psychology ma...

name: Hemmes Engineering Student Society
description: The Hemmes Engineering Student Society is the official student organization of the Dual-Degree Program in Science/Engineering. Students in the program, as well as all students in scientific fields, are encouraged to come to both our social and career-themed events.
summary: The Hemmes Engineering Student Society (HESS) is the official student organization of the Dual-Degree Program in Science/Engineering

name: Classics Club
description:
The NYU Classics Club is a place for anyone and everyone interested in classical antiquity and ancient civilizations!

Our meetings will be held in the Classics department, which is located on the fifth floor of the Silver Center for the Arts, in Room 503K (the Classics Lounge) every other Tuesday at 5:30pm. In addition to holding biweekly general meetings, we plan to take weekend excursions to museums and plays, host lectures with graduate students, watch informative videos, play games, and organize language study sessions.
summary: The NYU Classics Club is a place for anyone and everyone interested in classical antiquity to find their community! Our meetings are located in Room 503K of the Silver Center (The Classics Dept. Lounge).

name: Society of Physics Students
description: The Society of Physics Students is for everyone with any level of interest in physics. Our weekly meeting is at 7pm on Thursdays in 726 Broadway (Rm 1067). We have activities, discussions, and student talks. For the physics-oriented, we also organize external talks by professors and other networking opportunities. We are a chapter of the national Society of Physics Students organization that works closely with the CAS Physics Department. All in all, this club is for having fun and meeting new people.
summary: The New York University chapter of the Society of Physics Students is a nationally-recognized chapter of the American Institute of Physics. NYUSPS retains a very loyal following. The SPS board cultivates mentorship among NYU undergraduate physics major...

name: American Chemical Society
description: The American Chemical Society at NYU is a undergraduate and graduate chapter of the national organization. We are dedicated to supporting aspiring Chemists and Biochemists as well as Chemistry enthusiasts through a host of networking, research, and professional development events. In addition, we engage in public outreach projects as a form of community service.
summary: The American Chemical Society at NYU is a undergraduate and graduate chapter of the national organization. We are dedicated to supporting aspiring Chemists and Biochemists as well as Chemistry enthusiasts through a host of networking, research, and pro...

summary: IN2 M2 is a Korean-Christian club at NYU. We host weekly bible study sessions for those who are interested in learning more about bible. All are welcome!

name: BUGS Open Source at NYU
description: We promote and practice the ideals of Open Source through first hand experience with project participation and speakers using open source in different industries. Stay up to date by visiting our website at bugsnyu.com, following our Instagram @bugsnyu, joining our Discord, and subscribing to our mailing list at bit.ly/bugsnewsletter.
summary: We promote and practice the ideals of Open Source through first hand experience with project participation and speakers using open source in different industries.

name: Peer Undergraduate Mentorship Program (PUMP)
description: PUMP (Peer Undergraduate Mentorship Program) is a CAS club that pairs first- and second-year women currently pursuing or interested in STEM majors or tracks with an upper-level woman in a similar concentration. Mentor-mentee pairs are expected to virtually meet once a month to discuss academic life, career options, and research opportunities, among other topics. There are also larger events throughout the year, including panels, webinars, and networking events.
summary: PUMP aims to create a sense of community and support for NYU women in STEM. PUMP provides an opportunity for women to share knowledge and experiences as well as network with fellow STEM peers.

name: Public Policy Student Board
description: The goal of the Public Policy Student Board (PPSB) is to foster a community of public policy-minded students who engage with current events, advocate for causes they care about, and learn effective ways to make and implement policy in the United States. We also aim to promote opportunities for NYU students to access professional development in the policy sphere.
summary: The goal of the Public Policy Student Board (PPSB) is to foster a community of public policy-minded students who engage with current events, advocate for causes they care about, and learn effective ways to make and implement policy in the United States...

name: Proud to Be First Program
description: Proud to Be First seeks to engage and support first-generation college students. The definition of a first-generation college student is a student in the first-generation of their family to go to college. (Any student who identifies as first-generation is welcome.) The program assists first-generation students in making a successful transition to NYU while cultivating investment and engagement in the College and University Community. This is accomplished through creating supportive networks, establishing personal and academic goals, getting connected to campus resources and services, and meeting other first-generation students, faculty, and staff.
summary: CAS Proud to Be First (P2B1) is program designed to engage and support first gen students at NYU

name: CAS College Leaders
description:
CAS College Leaders is the engage group for the College of Arts and Science College Leaders. Members of this group are first year students for the 2025-2026 academic year at CAS and upperclassman students holding the college leader position!

Events for first years at CAS will be promoted by College Leaders on this engage group. Information and events about applying to be a future CAS College Leader will also be promoted on this engage group!
summary: CAS College Leaders is the engage group for the College of Arts and Science College Leaders. Members of this group are first year students for the 2025-2026 academic year at CAS and upperclassman students holding the college leader position!

name: International Students Club
description:
As a member of CAS ISC, you will have the opportunity to participate in a variety of events and programs that showcase the diversity of our student body and promote cross-cultural exchange. From cultural celebrations and food festivals to educational seminars and language exchanges, there is something for everyone at CAS ISC.

We also strive to support our international students in their academic and personal pursuits and provide resources and information to help them navigate the challenges of living and studying in a new country. Whether you seek to improve your language skills, make new friends, or simply explore the city, CAS ISC is the place for you.
summary: The International Students Club is an inclusive community for students in the College of Arts and Science and beyond to connect, celebrate diverse cultures, and engage in enriching activities to foster cross-cultural understanding and friendships.

name: CAMS on Campus
description: CAMS on Campus is a student-run organization that serves as a liaison between the students and the professors of the Child and Adolescent Mental Health Studies program at NYU. We plan a variety of events every semester, including stress busters, volunteer opportunities within the community, career panels, theatre events, and host a biannual mixer! Overall, our goal is to promote the mission of CAMS: spreading awareness of child & adolescent mental health and public health issues.
summary: CAMS on Campus is a student-run organization that serves as a liaison between the students and the professors of the Child and Adolescent Mental Health Studies program at NYU. We plan a variety of events every semester, including stress busters, volunt...

summary: NYU Graduate School of Arts & Science

name: National Student Speech Language Hearing Association NYU Chapter
description: NYU NSSLHA is an academically based student-run organization designed to meet the needs and interests of students studying communicative sciences and disorders.  In addition to addressing academic concerns, the group performs community service and organizes social events for its members.Traditional events include the Walk4Hearing and the Winter Banquet. NSSLHA also runs a mentorship program to connect incoming students and upperclassmen. This Chapter strives to: encourage professional interest among college and university students in the study of human communication sciences and disorders, provide continuity to the dissemination of professional information, and to provide a vehicle for student representation in matters of professional concern.
summary: NYU NSSLHA is an academically based student-run organization designed to meet the needs and interests of students studying communicative sciences and disorders.  In addition to addressing academic concerns, the group performs community service and organi

name: ASH Doctoral Student Forum
description: The ASH Doctoral Student Forum supports doctoral students in the Department of Applied Statistics, Social Sciences, and the Humanities, professionally, academically, and socially. In doing so, we provide support for teaching/assistant teaching, professional development workshops to meet the needs of students, and social events that foster a sense of community amongst doctoral students.
summary: ASH Doctoral Forum is to support doctoral students in NYU Steinhardt’s Department of Applied Statistics, Social Sciences, and Humanities and related studies academically, professionally, and socially.

name: Association of Student Affairs Professionals
description: The Association of Student Affairs Professionals (ASAP) at New York University is an organization for primarily Higher Education and Student Affairs graduate students and alumni in the Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development. The Association serves as the official graduate assembly and as such is committed to enhancing the experiences of current students, faculty, and alumni of the Higher Education and Student Affairs program. The Association provides educational, professional, and social opportunities encouraging and enhancing professional growth and development. The Association strives to facilitate the assimilation of new members to the New York University community, as well as New York City. The Association works as an advocate on behalf of its members and the Higher Education and Student Affairs program. Furthermore, the Association seeks to create a cohesive and lasting experience that will positively impact its members and alumni for years to come.
summary: The Association of Student Affairs Professionals (ASAP) is an organization primarily for current graduate students and alumni of the Higher Education and Student Affairs program at NYU's Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development.

name: Bilingual Language and Literacy Investigative and Networking Group
description: BLLING is dedicated to conducting and discussing current research topics; offering education to teachers, speech-language pathologists, and other related professionals on bilingual language and literacy issues; and supporting the community through hosting family literacy workshops and other events aimed at increasing awareness of bilingual and biliteracy issues.
summary: Bilingual Language and Literacy Investigative and Networking Group is dedicated to conducting and discussing current speech-language pathology research topics.

name: Diversity and Inclusion Student Committee
description: DISC seeks to address various community needs within Steinhardt, including fostering cultural competency skills in academic and professional settings, building a more inclusive community, and integrating more practices within the program that serve students of all backgrounds.
summary: DISC is to both give space to students to speak about diversity and inclusion, as well as create events for students to get involved and actively support it.

name: Food and Racial Equity Colllective
description: The mission of F.A.R.E. Collective is to create a community for self-identified graduate students of color in the NYU Food Studies Program; support and acknowledge each other in the context of our cultural identities, and within the respective communities to which we belong; discuss social and political issues that directly impact communities of color; collaborate through student-initiated programming, and share knowledge and information both inside and beyond the Food Studies program.
summary: Food and Racial Equity (F.A.R.E.) Collective seeks to create a space for social and political dialogue for self-identified graduate students of color and other students concerned with the empowerment of students of color in the Food Studies program.

name: Human Development and Social Intervention
summary: The HDSI Grad Society's purpose is to provide support to students enrolled in the HDSI Master's program at NYU.

name: International Education Student Board
summary: The International Education Student Board (IESB) is a board of International Education graduate students who are committed to creating both social and professional events for the International Education student body.

name: Steinhardt LatinX Graduate Student Association
description: Steinhardt LatinX Graduate Student Association Declaracion de Nuestro Mision To build a familia within Steinhardt to expand the graduate student of color network at large. To bring students of LatinX backgrounds together and to bring awareness to injustices faced by the LatinX community, including the complexities of race and class issues within the LatinX diaspora. We seek to encourage dialogue and engagement through socials, community service, professional development, speakers series, and a variety of other events. Through this mission we hope to strengthen our community ties and to build together- porque “en la union esta la fuerza”.
summary:
Steinhardt LatinX Graduate Student Association 
Declaracion de Nuestro Mision: To build a familia within Steinhardt to expand the graduate student of color network at large.

name: The Torch Journal of Higher Education & Student Affairs
description: The Torch Journal of Higher Education & Student Affairs is an annual peer reviewed journal that explores contemporary issues and current trends in the field of higher education with a particular focus on topics of interest to student affairs professionals. The journal offers graduate students the opportunity to participate in manuscript revision processes used in notable research journals. Research and academic manuscripts are submitted to a rigorous peer editing process including a host of graduate student peers at NYU and professionals in the field of higher education both inside and outside of NYU. Student research undergoes three cycles of peer review editing before being considered for the final publication at the end of the academic year. The Torch is excited to release its first issue this year. Please contact torchjournal@nyu.edu with questions.
summary: The Torch Journal of Higher Education & Student Affairs is an annual peer-reviewed journal that explores contemporary issues and trends in higher education and student affairs.

name: Student Association for the Master's in Media
description: The Student Association for the Master's in Media (SAMM) is a student organization that represents the graduate student community at the department of Media, Culture, and Communication. It acts as a liaison between graduate students and the department and greater Steinhardt administration, and also creates opportunities for professional and academic development.
summary: The MCC MA Student Association is a student organization that represents the graduate student community at the department of Media, Culture, and Communication.

name: National Student Speech Language and Hearing Association NYU Graduate Chapter
description: The mission of Graduate NSSLHA at New York University is to promote awareness of Speech and Language Pathology and Audiology as a profession and enrich the knowledge of graduate students. NSSLHA encourages professional interest among college and university students in the study of Communicative Sciences and Disorders, spreads professional information and awareness, and provides a means for student representation in matters of professional concern. NSSLHA hosts monthly meetings, social activities, service opportunities, professional panels, and participates in legislative action. The NYU Chapter is a recognized local chapter of NSSLHA and serves as a liaison with the national organization of ASHA.
summary: The mission of Graduate NSSLHA at New York University is to promote awareness of speech and language pathology and audiology as a profession and enrich the knowledge of graduate students.

name: Nevermind the Noise Composer Collective
summary: Nevermind the Noise Composer Collective serves to promote and advocate for performance and recording of new works by NYU Steinhardt Graduate Composers, and to put on professional productions of original concert music to engage the wider NYU community.

name: Steinhardt Black Society
description: Steinhardt Black Society (SBS) creates a safe space and comfortable environment for Black Undergraduate and Graduate students, their community and their allies to build community within Steinhardt. Furthermore, we wish to affect change within our community by working with other members of the greater NYU community. This association will not only brings us together, but also bring awareness to the accomplishments and injustices affecting the Black community. Overall, we ensure and encourage this dialogue through socials, community service, professional development opportunities, speakers series, and a variety of other events.
summary: Steinhardt Black Society (SBS) creates a safe space and comfortable environment for Black Undergraduate and Graduate students, their community and their allies to build community within Steinhardt.

name: Society for Statistics Club
description: Society for Statistics Students aims to foster community amongst NYU students interested in statistics and data science, cultivate networks with New York University and New York City’s broader STEM sector, provide professional development opportunities for students pursuing quantitative oriented careers, and serve as an official student chapter of the American Statistical Association at New York University.
summary: Society for Statistics Students aims to foster community amongst NYU students interested in statistics and data science.

name: Steinhardt Dance Education Association
description:
The SDEA provides students enrolled in the NYU Steinhardt Dance Education graduate program the chance to
meet mentors in the field of dance education, develop leadership skills, coordinate guest teaching artists, professional development, and other enrichment opportunities for its members, make connections with community organizations to offer free dance workshops or classes, prepare and submit proposals to present at the annual NDEO National Conference, and volunteer and advocate for dance education within the local, state, and national level.

The organization also affords students the unique opportunity to enter into a continuous dialogue about the current state of dance arts education and other pressing issues pertinent to the field of dance. The SDEA will strengthen the growing web among graduate students interested in dance education, providing a forum for intellectual and creative exchange.
summary: The SDEA provides students enrolled in the NYU Steinhardt Dance Education graduate program the chance to meet mentors in the field of dance education, attend national conferences, and develop leadership and professional development skills.

name: NYU Center for Data Science
description: The Center for Data Science (CDS) is the focal point for New York University’s university-wide efforts in Data Science. The Center was established in 2013 to advance NYU’s goal of creating a world-leading Data Science training and research facility, and arming researchers and professionals with the tools to harness the power of Big Data. Today, CDS counts 20 jointly appointed interdisciplinary faculty housed on three floors of our magnificent 60 5th Avenue building, one of New York City’s historic properties. It is home to a top-ranked MS in Data Science program, one of the first PhD programs in Data Science, and a new undergraduate program in Data Science, as well as a lively Fellow and Postdoctoral program. It has over 60 associate and affiliate faculty from 25 departments in 9 schools and units. With cross-disciplinary research and innovative educational programs, CDS is shaping the new field of Data Science.
summary: The Center for Data Science (CDS) is the focal point for New York University’s university-wide efforts in Data Science.

name: Bechdel Test Comedy
description: Bechdel Test is NYU's comedy sketch/improv group dedicated to providing space for the female/non-binary community. All Tisch clubs are open to all students.
summary: Bechdel Test is NYU's comedy sketch/improv group dedicated to providing space for the female/non-binary community. All Tisch clubs are open to all students.

name: Applied Urban Science Association
description: The Applied Urban Science Association (AUSA) is an association mainly consisting of (not limited to) graduate students at NYU Tandon's Center for Urban Science and Progress who are committed to advancing the fields of urban science, data-driven city management and urban analytics.
summary: The Applied Urban Science Association (AUSA) is an association of graduate students at NYU’s Center for Urban Science and Progress who are committed to advancing the fields of urban science, data-driven city management and urban analytics.

name: Prosthodontics Society
description: A creative mix of engineering and dentistry, come with us to explore the possibilities of Prosthodontics, from crowns and bridges to oral maxillofacial reconstructions! 🦷 📐
summary: TBD

name: NYU Entrepreneurial Institute
summary: Tracing its roots to the launch of the NYU Innovation Venture Fund in 2010, the NYU Entrepreneurial Institute leads a university-wide initiative to accelerate the pace of technology commercialization and the launch of successful startups founded by NYU's

name: CSL Leadership Development
summary: CSL Leadership offerings including the Leadership Academy.

name: Test Club
description: This is a testing club site for the Office of Student Affairs
summary: This is a testing organization for the Office of Student Affairs.

name: Polygeia
description:
Polygeia NYU GPH is part of Polygeia, a UK-based global health research group. As a student-led think tank, we aim to empower students to explore and write comprehensive reviews on global health challenges. Our work involves a variety of commissioned projects, in collaboration with NGOs, to investigate pressing public health topics, with our findings presented at an annual conference. In the past, we've partnered with organizations like Nomi Network, focusing on Southeast Asia, and CorpsAfrica, among others. Our club seeks to combine a passion for public health, research writing, and teamwork to make a meaningful impact both within the NYU GPH community and globally.

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: Polygeia NYU GPH is part of Polygeia, a UK-based global health research group. As a student-led think tank, we aim to empower students to explore and write comprehensive reviews on global health challenges.

name: GPH International Student Society
description:
The GPH International Students Society (GISS) is a student organization that represents the GPH international student body. GISS focuses on helping international students to be part of the GPH global community and supports their transition into campus life and adjustment to the American culture, enabling them to build professional relationships and share the cultural richness of public health experiences. 

GISS cultivates a diverse, inclusive, and dynamic community for students from every corner of the world, providing a forum through which members can come together to celebrate differences and achievements alike, share experiences and stories,  raise concerns, and most importantly, build a strong community of future public health advocates. GPH ISS is a place where we grow and help others grow with us. 

GISS hosts social and cultural events, academic lectures, and professional and networking events to celebrate the diversity of GPH’s student body and give international students the unique experience of living and studying in New York City.

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: The GPH International Students Society(GISS) is a student organization that represents the GPH International student body. We are devoted to support cultural diversity and promote professional Public Health practices.

name: Astronomy Club
description:
The NYU Astronomy Club is a community for anyone curious about astronomy the cosmos in general, ranging from casual stargazers to aspiring researchers. Relaunching in the Spring Semester of 2026, we are excited to connect members with the NYC astronomy community for telescope viewing, museum outings and more. We also have various astronomy-related special events planned, such as launch watch parties, guest lectures, movie nights and astronomy trivia.

Join us now and stay informed about our upcoming exciting club activities. Drop us an email at nyuastronomyclub@gmail.com for more information on getting involved!
summary: The NYU Astronomy Club was established in 2010 with the goal of exploring astronomy as a subject, and as an area of bringing people together through online and in-person events across NYC and the broader American community.

name: NYU Admissions Ambassadors
description:
The Admissions Ambassador Application is now open for Fall 2025 recruitment! Please visit https://linktr.ee/NYUAmbassadors for more information and to apply!

 

Ambassadors play a crucial role in the recruitment efforts of New York University's Office of Undergraduate Admissions. Through campus tours, special events, conversations at the Admissions Center and on the telephone, Ambassadors have unique contact with prospective students and their families. As one of the most trusted sources in the admissions process, Ambassadors provide prospective students with authentic representations of the undergraduate experience at NYU. Ambassadors is a paid student job and also a social opportunity!

If you have any additional questions please email ambassadorsmr@gmail.com.

What we do:

  • Work at university recruitment & special events 
  • Give campus tours every week
  • Answer phones at the admissions office
  • Greet guests at the Welcome Center & Wunsch Building

Visit our Linktree for more!
summary: Ambassadors play a crucial role in the recruitment efforts of New York University's Office of Undergraduate Admissions.

name: 1831 Fund
description: The 1831 Fund is a student-run organization that raises unrestricted use scholarship support for the next generation of NYU students. 100% of all donations go directly to financial aid for students at undergraduate degree-granting schools.
summary: The 1831 Fund is a student-run organization that raises money for student-created scholarships. We host events and campaigns to help more students be able to come to NYU each year! Every dollar raised goes directly back to an incoming student next Fall.

name: Society of Women in Technology
description: The Society of Women in TeCHnology (SWiTCH) is a student-run organization for minority graduate and undergraduate students in STEAM-based areas of study at NYU. SWiTCH empowers our members, most of whom identify as underrepresented genders, to succeed academically and professionally in technology-related fields, with an emphasis on music technology professions. SWiTCH is made up of engineers, scientists, artists, musicians, educators, and music industry professionals who strive to inspire and learn from one another. Through SWiTCH, marginalized communities are given an opportunity to network, collaborate, and share ideas.
summary: SWiTCH is a student-run club specifically for graduate and undergraduate technology students of underrepresented genders at NYU.

name: Student Occupational Therapy Association
description: The New York University Student Occupational Therapy Association (NYU SOTA) will provide a community specifically for NYU graduate OT students outside of the classroom. The Association will promote diversity and equality among students to encourage strong professional relationships, through study groups, social events, and philanthropic activities, the Association will facilitate individual and communal growth.
summary: The New York University Student Occupational Therapy Association (NYU SOTA) will provide a community specifically for NYU graduate OT students outside of the classroom.

name: Pan Asian Graduate Student  Association
description: The Steinhardt Pan Asian Graduate Student Association (PAGSA) is committed to raising visibility and awareness surrounding Asian/Pacific Islander/Desi American (APIDA) social and policy issues both domestically and internationally within the public service sector and the wider community. PAGSA strives to provide a network and support system among Steinhardt APIDA and other students of color by enhancing the educational experience through various forums and resources geared towards professional development, social events, and dialogue addressing relevant issues concerning the APIDA community.
summary: The Steinhardt Pan Asian Graduate Student Association (PAGSA) is committed to raising visibility and awareness surrounding Asian/Pacific Islander/Desi American (APIDA) students.

name: Gallatin STE(A)M Zine
description: The goal of this zine is to build STEAM awareness and community within Gallatin by showcasing creative student work at the intersection of science, technology, engineering, mathematics, medicine and/or the humanities.
summary: Gallatin's STE(A)M Zine is a student-led publication centering on Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Math.

To cultivate, promote, and sustain the art and science of dental hygiene, to represent and safeguard the
common interest of the members of the dental hygiene profession, and to contribute toward the improvement of the oral health of the public.

name: Sigma Psi Zeta - All-University
description: Sigma Psi Zeta is an Asian-interested, multicultural Greek Organization. We are a cultural, social, educational, and community service-oriented sorority. We are the first Greek-lettered sorority of its kind to be established on the East Coast, and today, we are one of the largest and most distinguished multicultural sororities in the nation.
summary: Sigma Psi Zeta is a multicultural, Asian-interest (but not Asian-exclusive) Greek organization. We are a cultural, social, educational, and community service-oriented sorority.

name: NYU Health Promotion Office
description:
Health Promotion supports the health and well-being of NYU students whether they come to the Student Health Center in-person or not.

The office creates programs on topics including stress, alcohol and other drugs, mental and sexual health, healthy eating, sexual assault prevention, and bystander intervention.

Health Promotion provides interactive, engaging health information to help students make informed and healthy choices while at NYU, and works with students to promote activities that create a healthier environment for the NYU community.
summary:
Health happens in the everyday- We promote flourishing, inspire positive decision-making & foster a healthier, happier NYU.

Find more at nyu.edu/health/promotion or on Instagram at hello_hpo.

name: Gender Equality in Music - All-University
description: The Gender Equality in Music (GEM) club is a safe space for those with a gender identity that is not heavily represented in the music industry. Our mission is to create a community of students from all aspects of the music industry to learn and support each other. I'm sure many of you know that many still face gender inequality in this industry today and that's why we want to create this network of support and resources for students. Through showcases, panels, industry events, workshops, and collaborations, members of the club will gain an opportunity to meet like-minded people and make connections with other students at NYU and within the music industry.
summary: Gender Equality in Music (GEM) is a club open to all students at NYU. Our mission is to create a community of people to learn about from all aspects of the music industry and support each other through showcases, panels, workshops, and events.

name: One Love at NYU - All-University
description: As a chapter of the One Love Foundation at NYU, we aim to educate fellow NYU students about the difference between healthy and unhealthy relationships. By educating our peers, we hope to empower them with the knowledge and framework they can use to talk about healthy and unhealthy relationship behaviors in their own lives. Our core guiding values are inclusion and intersectionality, respect, continuous learning, teamwork, and accountability.
summary: One Love at NYU is a chapter of The One Love Foundation, a national organization that teaches young people to love better in their romantic and interpersonal relationships.

name: Black Student Health Association - All-University
description:
The Black Student Health Association's (BSHA) mission is to:

  • To provide guidance to Black students interested in pursuing health careers, which includes medical education, allied health fields, and health sciences.
  • To provide opportunities for students at NYU to support and aid the Black community in a health-related fashion.
  • To provide a setting for the dissemination of information to educate and help Black students be successful in the pursuit of these health-related fields.
  • To provide counseling, mentorship, and advising to Black students interested in pursuing health-related careers.
  • To serve as a liaison between this organization and other organizations with alike missions.
  • All other appropriate actions that the organization deems necessary in order to implement its mission and objectives.
summary: BSHA provides guidance to Black students interested in pursuing health careers, which includes medical education, allied health fields, and health sciences.

name: New Masculinities - All-University
description: The mission of NYU New Masculinities is to serve as an inclusive and educational community that examines and explores the abstract concepts of masculinity, gender identity, and expression from an LGBTQ+ affirming lens. We aim to provide a space for club members to socialize and share experiences to broaden their understandings of queer identity, create university-wide events exploring social identity and queerness, and be a network of support for LGBTQ+ students at NYU.
summary: New Masculinities is an NYU LGBTQ+ Center affiliated club that is dedicated to the exploration of masculinity through a queer, critical lens and an inclusive, educational community.

name: Medical Intelligence and Innovation - All-University
description:
Medical Intelligence and Innovation (MI2) introduces students to the interdisciplinary future of medicine, business, and computer science. As the first chapter of a nationwide network, we coordinate speaker events with industry professionals ranging from physicians to biotech startup founders. We also host team workshops for students to further understand the latest developments in healthcare and biotechnology, and enhance the critical thinking and research skills shared by all healthcare fields. And through our partnership with the NYU Entrepreneurial Institute, we have the resources to build a biotech idea into something greater and impactful.

Keywords: Medicine, Medical, Innovation, Artificial Intelligence, AI, Technology, Entrepreneurship
summary: MI2 at NYU introduces students to the interdisciplinary future of medicine, business, and computer science, through speaker panels with industry professionals, workshops and challenges for students, and opportunities to take an idea to the next level.

name: Aces & Aros
summary: For people who identify across the asexual or aromantic spectrum and their allies

name: QFaith
description: QFaith is a multifaith club for LGBTQ+ NYU students & their allies to discuss religion, faith, and spiritual beliefs. You do not have to be religious in order to join! We welcome individuals from all religious and spiritual backgrounds.
summary: QFaith is a multifaith club for LGBTQ+ NYU students & their allies to discuss religion, faith, and spiritual beliefs. You do not have to be religious in order to join! We welcome individuals from all religious and spiritual backgrounds.

name: OutBreath
summary: Mindful Meditation for the LGBTQ+ community

name: Philosophy Club
description: The Philosophy Forum is NYU's largest undergraduate philosophy organization and is open to all. We host biweekly meetings and some of our activities include talks by academic philosophers, fun social events, and philosophical film screenings. We also organize the Annual Undergraduate Philosophy Conference and NEO, NYU's forthcoming undergraduate philosophy journal. Visit our Instagram page or our website using the icons below to see our past events and to learn more about us. All undergraduate students interested in philosophy are encouraged to join.
summary: Also known as the NYU Philosophy Forum, the Philosophy Club is the largest undergraduate philosophy organization on campus. Regular meetings consist of talks by academic philosophers, social events, panels, film screenings, and more.

name: Digital Accessibility Team
description: Digital Accessibility is an important part of being inclusive online by ensuring digital documents, the web, and mobile apps work for everyone, including people with disabilities. Learn more about Digital Accessibility at NYU.
summary: We are the team who provide awareness training and technical support for digital accessibility. This Engage site is for testing purposes only. If you have questions about digital accessibility at NYU, please contact us at: digital-accessibility@nyu.edu

name: Liberal Studies Tea and Philosophy Club
description:
Tea and Philosophy is a collaborative reading and discussion group. We have meetings every Friday (although our meeting day is subject to change) in which we discuss a weekly short reading. Our readings range from short stories to ancient texts to contemporary philosophy, all suggested by the members of our group. If you’d like to join or find out more about us and the logistics of our meetings, you can do so by searching for our Facebook page (Tea and Philosophy NYU) or our Instagram (@tea_and_philosophy_nyu)

P.S. Anyone can join regardless of major or school.
summary: T&P is a collaborative reading and discussion group. Readings range from short stories to ancient texts to contemporary philosophy, all suggested by members of our group.

name: Translation Nation
description:
How many translated texts have you read? 
Ask yourself, what kind of role translation has played in your life and academic career? 

Translation Nation is a chance to meet others interested in linguistics, literature, language, accessibility, and art. Join us in engaging with the art of translation: its practice, its theory, its politics, its community, its impact and its many applications. Maybe even do some translating yourself! We look forward to hosting virtual translation workshops, discussions, and even some panel events with translators, interpreters, subtitlers and other specialists. 

No formal translation experience or even second language proficiency is required! 

Join our mailing list by submitting a request on Engage! Meetings Tuesdays at 5pm:)
summary:
Translation Nation is a chance to meet others interested in linguistics, literature, language, accessibility, and art.

No formal translation experience or even second language proficiency is required!

name: Liberal Studies Law Cooperative
description:
The LS Law Co-op provides its members the opportunity to discuss the role of law in society, including various topics, from immigration to corporate law and from racial justice to the death penalty, as well as the practice of law, including criminal and civil trials and international courts. Each year we sponsor special meetings on how to get into law school and what to do when you get there as well as visits to federal court in NYC to experience law in a real-life setting. Note, however, that you don't have to plan to become a lawyer to become a member of the Law Co-op. The Law Co-op plans to hold a law conference in the spring on a subject chosen by its members. Past conferences addressed such themes as immigration and “Trump's America.”

We would love to answer any questions you may have.

Join our Email list by filling out the form: https://forms.gle/YM4t8yfFiP9EEcXEA

Follow us on Instagram @lslawcooperative

Email us @ liberalstudieslawcooperative@gmail.com
summary: The LS Law Co-op provides its members the opportunity to discuss the role of law in society, including various topics, from immigration to corporate law and from racial justice to the death penalty, as well as the practice of law.

name: Liberal Studies Economics Club
description: The LS Economics Club focuses on creating a community of Economics enthusiasts (primarily but not exclusively those who study in Liberal Studies) to have fruitful discussions and grow together in our knowledge and interest in the field of Economics. We will also connect students with networking opportunities and prepare them to manifest their economic interests into career opportunities. Activities include learning new topics in the theoretical field of economics through readings, 24/7 discussions on group chat, debates on economic schools of thought, guest speakers for advice on careers, etc.
summary: The LS Economics Club is designed for students who want to keep themselves up to date in terms of the economic climate we are currently facing.

name: Femmes in Film
description: Femmes in Film is an NYU Tisch Club dedicated to providing resources and advocating for women & non-binary students interested in the entertainment industry. All are welcome.
summary: A club dedicated to advocating for and providing resources to women and non-binary people in the film industry.

description: NYU Engage is a brand new platform provided by NYU for clubs and events. Stern has adopted NYU Engage for the MSQM program.

name: NYU Changemaker Center
description:
The NYU Changemaker Center prepares students to be active changemakers in their careers and communities. Through immersive programs, global awards advising, experiential learning, and public events, we help students build the skills, knowledge, and networks needed to realize the world they want.

 

Learn more at nyu.edu/changemaker.
summary: Changemaker Center

name: SPS Student Support Center
description:
NYU School of Professional Studies offers free academic support for currently enrolled students including tutoring services and workshops.  

 

Tutoring Services are tailored to meet the needs of both our undergraduate and graduate student populations.  Students can book their own tutoring sessions online; graduate students can do so via NYU Connect, while undergraduate students can do so via the WCONLINE system.  

 

Academic Enhancement Workshops are two-hour, deep dives into specific areas, such as public speaking and presentation skills, academic writing, English pronunciation, and more.  You can view upcoming workshops HERE.

 

If you have any questions or need assistance, please email us at sps.academicsupport@nyu.edu.  We are here for you!
summary: NYU School of Professional Studies offers academic support to our students in the form of free tutoring services and academic enhancement workshops.

name: Office of Student Success
summary: Office of Student Success

name: NYU Paris
description: At NYU Paris, explore French language and culture, engage with art and theatre, study media and philosophy, or deepen your musical and performance skills. And, through the academic internship program, you can also gain both course credit and career experience in your chosen field.
summary: At NYU Paris, explore French language and culture, engage with art and theatre, study media and philosophy, or deepen your musical and performance skills...

name: Tisch Tap Collective
description: Tisch Tap Collective is NYU's Premiere Tap Dance Company. As a student-run organization, we aim to educate, inspire, and celebrate the art of tap dance through workshops, panel discussions, performances, and productions. The Tisch Tap Collective is dedicated to preserving the African American legacy of tap dance and honoring its deep connection to jazz music in all that we do.
summary: Tisch Tap Collective

name: SPS Center for Global Affairs (CGA)
summary: The SPS Center for Global Affairs (CGA)

name: Alliance for Environmental Justice
description:
Welcome to the Alliance for Environmental Justice (AEJ) student club at NYU, which focuses on climate change, ecological health, biodiversity collapse, and environmental injustice. AEJ honors the movement started in the United States in response to environmental racism, and explores the concept as interpreted around the world. The Alliance takes an ecocentric approach that considers the context of vulnerable human and non-human communities and ecosystems. The club aims to help community members connect and share information, including news, events, recommendations, and more to advance environmental justice. 

Follow us on Instagram (@nyualliance), visit our website (https://sites.google.com/nyu.edu/nyuaesj/home), and come to our events and meetings.
summary: The Alliance for Environmental Justice (AEJ) student club at NYU focuses on climate change, ecological health degradation, biodiversity collapse, and environmental injustice.

name: NYU Tandon Department of Finance and Risk Engineering
description: If you’ve ever been drawn to a field that some people are calling “the rocket science of Wall Street,” Finance and Risk Engineering might be the course of study for you.
summary: If you’ve ever been drawn to a field that some people are calling “the rocket science of Wall Street,” Finance and Risk Engineering might be the course of study for you.

name: Silver IESL
description: The Office of Inclusive Engagement and Student Life provides support and advising to NYU Silver student governments and groups. We are wholly committed to creating an environment where students are empowered to create social, academic, advocacy, and support groups. These groups are instrumental in creating a strong community at NYU Silver and, as such, they are encouraged to use the myriad resources available at NYU and NYU Silver to meet their goals.
summary: Office of Inclusive Engagement and Student Life provides support and advising to NYU Silver student governments and groups.
