description: TEST
summary: Org for testing Engage feature by SPS staff

name: Digital Dentistry Club
description: Through collaboration with dental technology providers and innovative clinicians, the mission of the Digital Dentistry Club is clear: to engage, educate, and spark the interest amongst students of NYU Dentistry. We aim to continue the ongoing exploration and edification of the digital processes that govern and constantly innovate the field of dentistry.
summary: Through collaboration with dental technology providers and innovative clinicians, the mission of the Digital Dentistry Club is clear: to engage, educate, and spark the interest amongst students of NYU Dentistry. We aim to continue the ongoing exploration

name: Gallatin Disability Collective
description:
Disability Collective welcomes students, guest speakers, and collaboration with Gallatin and All-University clubs to engage in conversations about the past, present, and future topics that relate to the triumphs and obstacles that people with disabilities encounter. [You do not need to have a in- or visible disability to join us, we accept anyone who wants to learn and be part of a supportive community!] We are dedicated to providing an inclusive and safe space for conversation, media, and writing that can be shared within our meeting space and beyond. There will be service and field trips, game nights, lectures, and more!  If you are interested in becoming a member do it through engage*, keep up with us through the social media links on our website! Our contact info is there as well. Join our discord if you can and/or cannot come to the meetings, share it with your friends and classmates because we want to create meaningful connections!

General meetings tbd!

We will use Mailchimp to communicate with people, therefore please become a member on engage to receive our updates about the club, outside resources, etc.*
summary: we are dedicated in facilitating equitable, intersectional, and intentional conversations between people interested in disability justice. We will help each other expand upon disability studies across disciplines & collaborate w/ advocates

name: The Islamic Center at New York University
description:
The Islamic Center at NYU provides a safe place for individuals with multiple points of entry to learn, pray, socialize and utilize a space situated at intellectual and spiritual crossroads. 

Inspired by the belief that communities develop in a supportive environment, people from all backgrounds are welcomed to engage in cross-cultural experiences, social justice endeavours, civic engagement initiatives, multifaith programming, spiritual practices and religious education.
summary: The Islamic Center at NYU provides a safe place for individuals with multiple points of entry to learn, pray, socialize and utilize a space situated at intellectual and spiritual crossroads.

name: Gallatin Research Incubator
description:
The Gallatin Research Incubator (GRI) is a network of undergraduates interested in conducting interdisciplinary inquiry, helping students refine their research and actively engage with resources at Gallatin.

Our mission is to build a collaborative network of passionate researchers and guide them in campus research, grant applications, and skill development for industry and academia. The GRI helps demystify the research process early-on for underclassmen while also promoting ongoing projects, aiming to foster a community of academic exploration and innovation.

We are Notion Ambassadors and recruiting partners with the Wasserman Center. For contact/questions, email us at gallatinresearchincubator-group@nyu.edu!
summary: The Gallatin Research Incubator (GRI) is a resource hub and pre-professional network of undergraduates at NYU interested in conducting systematic inquiries at the intersection of various disciplines

name: Epidemiology, Prevention, Intervention, and Control
description:
The EPIC club's main goal is to create bonds between the students of NYU GPH while learning and giving back to the public health community, with a focus on engaging with current epidemiological advances both in the US and globally. We strive to create an environment where students feel heard and set them up for success on their journeys regarding coursework and post-grad life.

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: EPIC (Epidemiology, Prevention, Intervention, and Control) aims to work closely with GPH students, alumni, and faculty to enhance the educational experience of all the individuals in and beyond the Department of Epidemiology.

name: NYU Student National Association of Teachers of Singing
summary: NYU SNATS is a student chapter of the National Association of Teachers of Singing. Our chapter encourages high standards of singing and pedagogy. Members have the opportunity to participate in national, state, and regional competitions, and: the chapter

name: Dental Anesthesiology Club
description: We, the students of NYUCD, in order to foster a community enhancing the knowledge and awareness of the field of dental anesthesiology, build a foundation that will support evidence-based principles of anesthesiology in the treatment of adult, pediatric, geriatric and special needs populations, as well as propel the clinical significance of dental anesthesiologists, do hereby establish the "NYUCD Dental Anesthesia Club" enacting this constitution for its government.
summary: Enhancing the knowledge and awareness of the field of dental anesthesiology, build a foundation that will support evidence-based principles of anesthesiology in the treatment of adult, pediatric, geriatric and special needs populations.

name: West Fourth Stage Company
description: West 4th Stage Company provides all NYU students with a platform to express themselves through theater and music. We fill curriculum gaps and provide professional enrichment opportunities to aspiring directors, producers, performers, and creators.
summary: West 4th Stage Company provides all NYU students with a platform to express themselves through theater and music. We fill curriculum gaps and provide professional enrichment opportunities to aspiring directors, producers, performers, and creators.

name: Rita and Burton Goldberg Department of Dramatic Writing
description:
The Rita and Burton Goldberg Department of Dramatic Writing cross-trains students in all areas of dramatic writing across the mediums of Playwriting, Screenwriting and Episodic writing.

Both our BFA and MFA programs are small, intimate communities with the unique advantage of being part of a major university in the world's most vibrant city.
summary: The Rita and Burton Goldberg Department of Dramatic Writing cross-trains students in all areas of dramatic writing across the mediums of Playwriting, Screenwriting and Episodic writing.

name: Office of Global Inclusion, Diversity, and Strategic Innovation
description: As part of the Office of the President, NYU’s Office of Global Inclusion, Diversity, and Strategic Innovation is dedicated to developing and implementing strategies that advance inclusion, diversity, belonging, equity, and innovation across the entire NYU community—including at our campuses in Abu Dhabi and Shanghai and our 11 global academic centers.
summary: OGI provides expert consultation, resources, and innovative strategies to help guide the University—and its uniquely global and diverse student, faculty, staff, administration, and alumni—toward a more inclusive future

name: NYU SACNAS (The Society for the Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science)
description:
SACNAS stands for The Society for the Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science. NYU SACNAS is one of 118 official and nationally recognized chapters. In fact, we were awarded 2021 National Chapter of the Year!

The organization is geared toward empowering people of Latino(a/x) and Native American descent in the sciences; however, it also encourages college students, graduates, and professionals of ALL backgrounds to become involved in the organization as well as in their communities.

As such, the New York University SACNAS Chapter is devoted to further diversifying the next generation of STEM scholars through leadership, community outreach, mentorship, and educational advancement at all levels. In addition, we provide students a platform to network, collaborate, and serve their community in impactful ways.
summary: SACNAS is an inclusive organization dedicated to fostering the success of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans, from college students to professionals, in attaining advanced degrees, careers, and positions of leadership in STEM.

name: Wasserman Center for Career Development
description: Whether you have no idea what you want to do after graduation or you know exactly what you want to do, we are here to support you in your journey.
summary: Whether you have no idea what you want to do after graduation or you know exactly what you want to do, we are here to support you in your journey.

name: Girls Who Code
description:
Girls Who Code (GWC) is a nonprofit organization that is dedicated to closing the gender gap in technology and computer science. Their mission is to inspire more girls to become computer scientists and engineers. The purpose of our club specifically targets college aged women, and helps them assimilate into the tech field later in life. We want to do this by holding programming that supports their endeavors academically and professionally.

Website 

LinkTree

Instagram

Mailing List
summary: The mission of Girls Who Code College Loop is to support women entering the tech field, academically and professionally. Our club strives to build belonging and community through meetings during the school year.

name: Department of Art History & Urban Design
description: “The best undergraduate department of art history in the country”: This is how New York Times art critic John Russell once described the Art History program at NYU. The Department was established to provide a rigorous and wide-ranging education in the many facets of the history and theory of art, a mission that its faculty continues to enthusiastically embrace. Students become familiar with global art from antiquity to the present. The Department offers courses in Ancient, Medieval, Renaissance, Baroque, Modern, Contemporary, East Asian, South Asian, Islamic, Latin American, African, Oceanic, Pre-Columbian, and Native American art, treating not only painting, sculpture, architecture, and photography but also graphic media, manuscript illumination, the decorative arts, and aspects of urban design. The Department offers two undergraduate majors and minors, one in Art History and another in Urban Design and Architecture Studies (URDS).
summary:
"Ars longa, vita brevis." Art is long life is short. 

We offer two undergrad majors and minors in Art History & in Urban Design and Architecture, as well as courses in many of NYU's global sites. We have a robust lecture event series.

name: Blueprint
description:
The mission of Blueprint is to bring together perspectives across the real estate ecosystem to share ideas, create new connections and explore opportunities at the intersection of real estate innovation and sustainability.

At our core, we’re a community of passionate people invested in understanding how to make our built world better.

About

Founded at the NYU School of Professional Studies Schack Institute of Real Estate, Blueprint is a student-led publication sponsored by REISA and distributed to the Schack community bi-monthly via email. If you are outside of Schack and want to be added to our distribution list, please subscribe on our website.
summary: Blueprint is a student-run publication that covers emerging trends reshaping the real estate industry. The mission of Blueprint is to showcase the diversity of real estate perspectives at NYU Schack Institute of Real Estate, publish original writing by s

name: Sunrise Movement NYU - All-University
description: Taking a semester off! Reach out to sns9982@nyu.edu if you're interested in starting this club back up. Sunrise NYU focuses on connecting NYU students to NYC-wide social and climate justice actions and fighting for environmental justice within NYU. After our success with the Divest NYU campaign we are expanding to other projects, both within NYU and out in the community. Our hub has approximately 60 active members and a growing leadership team.
summary: The NYU hub of the Sunrise Movement is building an army of young people to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs in the process. We attend protests, phone bank, canvass, hold teach-in events, and more on campus and around NYC.

name: Department of Photography & Imaging
description: The Department of Photography and Imaging (DPI) in Tisch School of the Arts at New York University is a four-year B.F.A. program situated in New York City. Centered on the making and understanding of images, DPI offers students both the intensive focus of an arts curriculum while demanding a broad grounding in the liberal arts. Our department embraces multiple perspectives and approaches which encourages critical engagement both in and outside of the classroom. Our majors explore photo-based imagery as personal and cultural expression while working in virtually all modes of analog and digital photo-based image making, multimedia, new media, immersive, and post-photographic 3D simulation technologies.
summary: The Department of Photography and Imaging (DPI) in Tisch School of the Arts at New York University.

name: Silver Silvers
description: The Silver Silvers provides a space of connection and support to students who are 40+. In addition we work with the school to create programming geared towards issues of interest to older students. We welcome people of all backgrounds, races, gender expression and abilities in our effort to create a vibrant and diverse age-affirming community.
summary: The Silver Silvers provides a space of connection and support to students who are 40+.

name: NYU Production Lab
description: The Production Lab made its first film investments in fall 2014 and then opened its doors to NYU’s creative community in fall 2015.  As a creative content incubator, the Lab serves as an experiential-learning bridge for students navigating the transition from an academic classroom to the professional creative industry. To date, the Production Lab has signed twenty projects onto the Lab’s Slate which have premiered at film festivals such as Sundance, Tribeca, and Cannes, and reached audiences on Netflix, Amazon, iTunes; pledged over $400,000 in film financing to the NYU creative community; hosted over 42,000 visits and over 2,100 auditions; supported 19 Writing Fellows developing feature-length scripts from 200-word ideas; held over 60 career-oriented events and panels; produced 21 podcast episodes with the support of NYU Abu Dhabi’s Music Technology & Sound Recording Group, and so much more.  With innovative creative program opportunities — including the Creative Career Design Fellowship and the VICE Episodic Storytelling Workshop — the Production Lab is at the forefront of cultural entrepreneurship, preparing today’s emerging artists for the jobs of tomorrow.
summary: The NYU Production Lab is a storytelling incubator whose mission is to help the next generation design and launch thriving careers in creative industries.

name: Opportunity Programs
summary: NYU's Opportunity Programs (HEOP and STEM/CSTEP) provides academic, financial, and development support for historically excluded students at NYU, many of whom identify as first-gen and students of color.

name: African Graduate Students Association - All-University
description:
Our mission is to foster a vibrant and supportive community for graduate students at NYU who identify and/or affiliate with the African continent and diaspora. We cater to the diverse needs of our members, addressing social, cultural, academic, and professional aspects, while also striving to enhance awareness and engagement within the broader NYU community on matters related to Africa. 

If you would like to join our vibrant WhatsApp group, please follow this link to submit your request: AGSA on WhatsAPP
summary: African students in graduate programs at NYU seeking to connect and build community.

name: The Pow(h)er Collective - All-University
summary: The Pow(h)er Collective raises awareness on intersectional health and wellness needs of marginalized womxn through volunteering, fundraising, community advocacy, sustainable practices, and creating access to healthcare education.

name: Book Buddies - All-University
summary: Book Buddies is for anyone who loves to read and talk about the books they are reading without having to be in a regular club when everyone reads the same book. The goal is to make a judgement free space for book lovers to read whatever book they want.

name: The Social Justice Art Project - All-University
description: The goal of the Social Justice Art Project is to empower NYU students to create art that addresses political and/or social issues as a form of activism. SJA was founded based on the notion that mainstream forms of verbal advocacy such as protesting are not exhaustively accessible due to barriers including but not limited to cultural/familial stigma, physical disability, general shyness, and language discordance. Thus, our hope is to create safe spaces for NYU students of all backgrounds to exploit the creative powers of the arts and fight for their causes.
summary: SJA aims to empower students to utilize artistic expression as a tool for social justice activism. We believe in promoting non-verbal advocacy as an alternative to mainstream actions. No artistic experience required!

name: Hong Kong Student Advocacy Group at NYU - All-University
description: HKSAG-NYU's mission is to advocate for democracy, human rights and any other political issues affecting HK. We're interested in global solidarity efforts with struggling Hongkongers at home and everywhere. From rallies, forums, screenings, handing out flyers, Lennon Walls and more, we raise awareness overseas and send our support. Hong Kong Student Advocacy Group stands for democracy in Hong Kong, and welcomes the support of any students, staff, faculty, and community members.
summary: Hong Kong Student Advocacy Group at NYU is for students interested in Hong Kong politics and advocacy.

name: Rubik's Cube Club at NYU - All-University
description: Welcome to the new 2025-2026 school year! Rubik's Cube Club is open for everyone, regardless of skill level. Come to our meetings to learn how to solve a cube.
summary: The mission of the Rubik’s Cube Club at NYU shall be to foster a community of students who enjoy solving puzzles, especially the Rubik’s cube. We are dedicated to supporting students who want to explore this hobby, either casually or competitively.

name: The Women's Network at NYU - All-University
description: The Women’s Network (TWN) is committed to changing the way women network. In creating a supportive, non-competitive community, we lift women up, creating a more proactive way of communicating and networking while embracing ambition. The network is open to all majors, departments, and industries. We strive to create a community of like-minded ambitious women.
summary: The Women’s Network (TWN) is committed to changing the way women network. In creating a supportive, non-competitive community, we lift women up, creating a more proactive way of communicating and networking while embracing ambition.

name: NYU Athletics - Intramurals
description: Intramurals are available to ALL undergraduate and graduate students at New York University who are in good standing. Alumni and other members of the NYU community are eligible for selected programs and offerings as designated. All skill levels and abilities are welcome to register (e.g. league divisions based on experience). Prizes, t-shirts, and more are awarded to participants. With an extremely diverse and global campus that spans many boundaries and borders, it is our goal to offer a unique, inclusive, and engaging program that meets the interest of all and helps in the education and personal development of the NYU student.
summary: Standing by our motto of "All Kinds. All Sizes. All Abilities. Everyone", NYU Intramurals aims to serve to serve, promote, and contribute to each participant's learning and developmental experience by fostering a positive, social, and competitive sports

name: SPS Digital Production Committee
description:
The SPS Digital Production Committee produces audio-visual content such as podcasts and event videos/photos. We also conduct workshops to teach students digital production skills like video editing, audio engineering and photography. 

We're looking for creative team members who are enthusiastic about working collaboratively on interesting projects. Any member of the SPS student body can be a part of the DPC (no experience required!) Just bring a can-do attitude and a desire to learn! Keen students may fill this form.
summary: The SPS Digital Production Committee produces audio-visual creatives such as podcasts, videos, and the official Student Council website.

name: Turkish Culture Association - All-University
summary: The Turkish Culture Association aims to connect students with an interest in Turkish culture by organizing events that allow people to experience Turkish culture.

name: Family Medicine Interest Group
description:
The mission of the Family Medicine Interest Group is to expose students to family medicine, foster interest in the specialty, and help students connect with family medicine physicians to learn more. Additionally, we intend to bring awareness to the numerous opportunities to get involved with family medicine professional societies on both state and nationwide levels.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The mission of the Family Medicine Interest Group is to expose students to family medicine, foster interest in the specialty, and help students connect with family medicine physicians to learn more.

name: High Value Care Club
description:
The High Value Care Club is committed to providing a foundation in high value care principles to all students. Despite having the costliest healthcare system in the world, the United States fails to perform well on numerous health outcomes metrics. Therefore, we seek to teach medical students how to deliver efficient and more equitable care while eliminating unnecessary waste. We partner with Choosing Wisely STARS, a national organization that seeks to foster student-led initiatives to advance health care value in medical education. The leaders of NYUGSOM High Value Care will attend a yearly summit with hundreds of medical students from across the country and gain exposure to the Choosing Wisely guidelines, as well as learn strategies to increase curricular content focused on the practical aspects of medicine. The club will host speaker and networking events to connect students to high value care focused physicians who are driving quality improvement initiatives, digital health projects, and policy initiatives at NYU Langone Health. These events will facilitate a pipeline of research opportunities for students interested in contributing to high value care during the academic year and summer term. Our hope is that all graduates of NYUGSOM will recognize high value care as the only type of care that should be delivered.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The High Value Care Club is committed to providing a foundation in high value care principles to all students. Despite having the costliest healthcare system in the world, the United States fails to perform well on numerous health outcomes metrics.

name: First-Generation & Low-Income Society
description:
First Generation & Low-Income Society (FGLIS )'s mission is to foster a close-knit community of first-generation and/or low-income students affiliated with NYU’s School of GPH and to encourage these students to engage in public health resources and events both on and off campus. Through the promotion of volunteering, professional development, and research opportunities, students will develop the leadership skills necessary to emerge as future trailblazers in various domains of public health. Our club first started with the idea that we wanted to fill in a gap at GPH because we realized that there was no existing club exclusively meant for first-gen and/or low-income identifying students.  

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: The mission of the NYU GPH FGLIS shall be to foster a close-knit community of first-generation and/or low-income students affiliated with NYU’s School of GPH.

name: Scholars of Finance
description: SOF seeks to solve the world’s largest problems by investing in undergraduate students through leadership development, mentorship, and the active support of hundreds of purpose-driven finance professionals. Our members join top firms, funds, and a lifelong support community that empowers them to spend their careers serving the greater good using the biggest lever in the world: finance.
summary: SOF seeks to solve the world’s largest problems by investing in undergraduate students through leadership development, mentorship, and the active support of hundreds of purpose-driven finance professionals.

name: Grey Matters Journal
summary: Grey Matters Journal focuses on beauty stemming from the exploration of the human brain, creating a network of scientific and interdisciplinary inquiry writing through the United States higher education system using art, publication, and research.

name: Psychology Student Society
summary: Psychology Student Society was founded in Spring 2022 with two main goals: creating a tight-knit community of psychology students and providing resources for navigating student life, networking and pursuing career paths.

name: Fubon Center for Technology, Business and Innovation
description: The Fubon Center serves as the School’s hub to support, facilitate and enhance cross-disciplinary collaboration among its existing areas of excellence, including: Fintech, Data Analytics and AI, and Tech Innovation. As a new nexus for continuous innovation at Stern, the Fubon Center strengthens and builds industry ties, focusing on cutting-edge research that creates impact for business and society. The Fubon Center will also help shape future coursework to align with a rapidly evolving business landscape.
summary: The Fubon Center serves as the School’s hub to support, facilitate and enhance cross-disciplinary collaboration among its existing areas of excellence, including: Fintech, Data Analytics and AI, and Tech Innovation.

name: Clive Davis Institute of Recorded Music
description: The Clive Davis Institute is the first and only program of its kind to provide professional business and artistic training toward a BFA in Recorded Music. We aim to provide students with the necessary skills — business, creative, and intellectual — so that they might emerge as visionary creative entrepreneurs in the evolving global music industry.
summary: The Clive Davis Institute is the first and only program of its kind to provide professional business and artistic training toward a BFA in Recorded Music.

name: Mu Delta Alpha - All-University
description:
Iman is our Identity, Ihsaan is our Legacy.

 

Mu Delta Alpha Sorority, Inc. is a Muslim-founded, multicultural, professional sorority committed to empowering women through faith, sisterhood, leadership, and service. We are a values-driven organization where women grow spiritually, academically, and professionally — while uplifting their communities locally and globally.

Founded to provide a safe and dignified space for Muslim and multicultural women, Mu Delta Alpha has become a national sisterhood where women pursue excellence with purpose, integrity, and heart. We believe leadership is not defined by titles but by service, character, and contribution to the world.

 

Across every chapter, our mission is lived through genuine sisterhood, academic support, professional development, and impactful service initiatives through our nonprofit, the M4C Foundation.

 

We prepare women to lead, on campus, in their careers, and in their communities, guided by the principles of faith, compassion, dignity, and excellence.

 

For information regarding recruitment, please refer to our Instagram @mda.nyu.
summary: Mu Delta Alpha is a sorority for Muslim Women who are devoted to Islam, sisterhood, professionalism, and service.

name: American Association of Neurological Surgeons
description:
Connecting students with neurosurgery residents and faculty, organizing events including speakers, journal clubs, and skills labs, and broadly promoting the field of neurosurgery to all at NYU. 

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Welcome to the NYU Grossman School of Medicine AANS Student Chapter/Neurosurgery Interest Group!

name: Kimmel Operations
summary: Kimmel Operations

name: No One Dies Alone (NYUGSOM)
description:
Our mission is to be present with people who are at the end of life and would otherwise be alone. We work together as a team of volunteers to provide companionship to patients at Bellevue who are dying and do not have loved ones present. Our goal is to educate and inform future healthcare providers about palliative and end-of-life care by creating a volunteering space and hosting various events. Referrals are sent to us like a sub-palliative service, and trained volunteers sign up for 1-2 hour-long shifts to spend time with referred patients in a non-medical capacity. Volunteers are encouraged to talk, listen to music, read poetry, sit quietly, etc. to share intentional presence with patients.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Our mission is to be present with people who are at the end of life and would otherwise be alone. We work together as a team of volunteers to provide companionship to patients at Bellevue who are dying and do not have loved ones present.

name: A/P/A Institute
description:
The A/P/A Institute's programs, exhibitions, and collections highlight and make accessible A/P/A cultural and intellectual production.

Events -- Engage with thought-provoking talks, discussions, performances, and gatherings.

Exhibitions -- Experience powerful stories and gain new perspectives through the visions of emerging and established artists.

Collections -- Explore extensive archives documenting the rich histories of Asian/Pacific/American communities.

Initiatives -- Our projects highlight innovative collaborations, scholarly research, and creative endeavors that center Asian/Pacific/American narratives and address critical issues impacting our communities.

For more information, visit apa.nyu.edu
summary: The Asian/Pacific/American Institute at NYU supports scholarship, art, and community engagement about and with Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders, promoting dialogue through collaborative programming, research, exhibitions, and exchanges.

name: Stern Legacy Fund
description:
Stern Legacy is a student organization in Stern that raises money throughout the year to create scholarships for incoming students in need. 100% of all funds raised goes directly to scholarships for Stern students next year.

We hope that you will help us pay it forward!
summary: Stern Legacy is a student organization in Stern that raises money throughout the year to create scholarships for incoming students in need.

name: Performing Arts Appreciation Club
description:
NYU Grossman SOM’s Performing Arts Appreciation Club (PAAC) offers students the opportunity to explore world-class music, dance, and theater through group outings to attend professional and amateur performances in NYC. The club alerts the student community to upcoming performing arts events of note, and, based on interest and availability, obtains tickets and coordinates travel and attendance. As a venue for shared enjoyment of NYC’s exceptional performing arts offerings, the club offers med school students not only cultural enrichment but also a chance for informal conversation and relaxation off-campus.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: NYU Grossman SOM’s Performing Arts Appreciation Club (PAAC) offers students the opportunity to explore world-class music, dance, and theater through group outings to attend professional and amateur performances in NYC.

name: CSD Student Ambassadors
description: The CSD Student Ambassador Organization is a student-run group for Communicative Sciences and Disorders graduate students that strives to strengthen our community through building initiatives, sharing information, and uplifting peers. The organization works through active participation in student-led initiatives.
summary: To strengthen our community through building initiatives, sharing information, and uplifting our peers.

name: LBGTQ+ CSD Clinical Club
description: The NYU LGBTQ+ Clinical Club seeks to create a safe space for LGBTQ+ students and their allies. The purpose of the LGBTQ+ Clinical Club is to support and cater to the unique, currently sidelined needs of undergraduate and graduate students in Communicative Sciences and Disorders (CSD) that identify as part of the LGBTQ+ community or allies through the following: fostering community dialogue and engagement to increase awareness, create shared resources for LGBTQ+ clinician students, and hosting educational events related to our field.
summary: As a group, we seek to provide a safe space to discuss the unique challenges faced by LGBTQ+ students, clinicians, and clients in academia, field placements, service delivery, and beyond.

name: National Black Association for Speech-Language and Hearing
description: NBASLH Graduate Chapter at NYU is an organization for Black graduate students studying Communicative Sciences and Disorders. Our mission is to provide a space for Black students in the CSD field to connect to identify common interests and achieve common goals that align with the purpose, mission, and vision of the Association. The goal of NBASLH is to increase the number of Black speech, language, and hearing professionals and to improve the quality of service delivery to Black individuals with communication disorders and differences. This club will hold meetings, panels, and community service events to help support aspiring professionals.
summary: Our mission is to provide a space for Black graduate students in the field of Communicative Sciences and Disorders to connect to identify common interests and achieve common goals that align with the purpose, mission, and vision of the Association.

name: Nutrition Graduate Society
description: The Nutrition Graduate Society (NGS) provides unique opportunities for students within the DPD/MS in Clinical Nutrition Program at NYU to foster community and develop professionalism through diverse speakers and networking events. This club holds regular meetings to keep students informed of academic, research, and professional opportunities within (or related to) the dietetics field. Areas of focus include Clinical Nutrition, Community Nutrition, Private Practice Nutrition, Nutrition Research, and more!
summary: The Nutrition Graduate Society's (NGS) goal is to provide support to students in the MS in Clinical Nutrition Program at NYU with the intent of creating a greater sense of community through diverse speakers and networking events.

name: American Academy of Developmental Medicine and Dentistry (AADMD)
description:
The American Academy of Developmental Medicine & Dentistry Chapter (AADMD) is a non-profit, membership organization of interdisciplinary health professionals — including primary physicians, medical specialists, dentists, optometrists, nurses and other clinicians — committed to improving the quality of healthcare for people with intellectual & developmental disabilities (IDD).

●Membership is free for students

●For more information, visit aadmd.org
summary: NYU Chapter of American Academy of Developmental Medicine and Dentistry (AADMD)

name: Contemporary Music Society
description: The Contemporary Music Society Club is unique in the fact that we don't just specialize in one aspect or instrument of contemporary music. We recognize that our musical superpower and great connector is that we all come from different culture, personal and musical backgrounds. Whether you are a producer, singer, songwriter, mixer, or videographer, we celebrate and encourage each other's musical intersections. Each role is vital in the storytelling, production, and growth of contemporary music.
summary: CMS is a space for its members to make connections, be creative, express themselves through performance, celebrate each other’s cultures and intersectionality, and learn and grow as artists and people through a diverse range of contemporary music.

name: Courier: Screenwriting Club
description:
Welcome! We are Courier and so glad you are here. Named after the screenplay font, we seek to create a space that motivates screenwriters to produce imaginative stories beyond the classroom with the support of fellow writers. By collaborating on scripts, reviewing and providing constructive feedback, and organizing engaging workshops, we hope to facilitate an inspiring and productive writers’ room environment for all students.

HOW TO JOIN

We do not require any applications or fees to join. All you have to do is show up! 

EMAIL LIST:

- Join our e-mail list HERE and follow our Instagram to learn about upcoming events, meetings, assignments, and more!

PREVIOUS EVENTS

- Logline, Coverage, Dialogue, Adaptation, Pitch Workshops

- Script Swaps for feedback

- Networking Nights

- Script Showcases

- Competitions

- Guest Speakers

HAVE SUGGESTIONS?

We know a thing or two about revisions and constructive criticism! Send Edie, our president, an email with concerns, ideas, questions, or anything else you might need! Her email is ep2706@nyu.edu.
summary: The Official Screenwriting Club of NYU! We motivate writers to produce imaginative stories beyond the classroom with the support of fellow writers. Come visit us at our weekly meetings!

name: S.W.E.A.R. (Social Workers Engaging in Anti-Racism): A White Accountability Group of Student Workers
description: S.W.E.A.R. (Social Workers Engaging in Anti-Racism): A White Accountability Group of Student Workers is a space for white-identifying students at the Silver School of Social Work to dismantle patterns of racism and injustice within themselves, with the intention of transforming their broader school, work, and personal communities. Using Layla Saad’s Me and White Supremacy book and journal as a guide, students identify, examine, and challenge the ways in which they both experience privilege from and perpetuate white supremacy. Members of this group strive to create a space that fosters openness and accountability, and commit to taking an active role in unlearning, resisting, and challenging white supremacy. This is an open group and people are welcome to join at any time.
summary: S.W.E.A.R. (Social Workers Engaging in Anti-Racism): A White Accountability Group of Student Workers is a space for white-identifying students at the Silver School of Social Work to dismantle patterns of racism.

name: First Gen Students of Color
description: We First Gen Students of Color are here to raise visibility to the first generation experience of BIPOC folks in higher academia. We intend to expand accessibility and resources to first gen students of color to and within NYU Silver through student life engagement, networking opportunities with alums, as well as through campus community building. We want to offer a brave space for first generation students of color to connect and share their narratives throughout their years at NYU and we hope to be a new community of support to the current and for the future first generation students to come.
summary: We First Gen Students of Color are here to raise visibility to the first generation experience of BIPOC folks in higher academia.

name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers Club
description:
Welcome to the IISE Club at NYU Tandon!

Mission: The mission of the organization shall be to support academic and professional development and improve the educational experience of the student community in collaboration with the Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers, the world’s largest professional society through workshops, competitions, and events relevant to industrial engineering.

Who We Are: The IISE Club at NYU Tandon is a vibrant community dedicated to fostering academic excellence and professional development in Industrial and Systems Engineering. Our goal is to create an open environment where young professionals can exchange ideas on career planning, leadership opportunities, and knowledge advancement. We bring together students, faculty, and industry professionals to share knowledge, inspire innovation, and drive success.

What We Offer:

  • Exclusive Resources: Gain access to thousands of case studies, technical articles, webinars, and newsletters that keep you at the forefront of industry trends.
  • Professional Growth: Attend workshops, seminars, and resume reviews led by industry experts and alumni to boost your career prospects.
  • Networking Opportunities: Connect with professionals, alumni, and fellow students through networking events, social mixers, and collaborative projects.
  • Leadership Development: Take on leadership roles and volunteer opportunities to build your resume and develop essential soft skills.
  • Industry Insights: Stay informed about the latest advancements and best practices in Industrial and Systems Engineering through our events and publications.
  • Career Support: Utilize the IISE Career Center for job listings, internships, and career advice tailored to your field.

Get Involved: Joining the IISE Club at NYU Tandon is your gateway to a wealth of knowledge, resources, and connections that can significantly impact your academic and professional journey. Participate in our regular events, engage with our community, and start your journey towards becoming a leader in Industrial and Systems Engineering.

Connect With Us: Follow us on social media to stay updated on our latest events, news, and opportunities. Join the conversation and be a part of a community that’s as passionate about Industrial and Systems Engineering as you are.
summary: Join the IISE Club at NYU Tandon for career advancement in Industrial and Systems Engineering. Access resources, attend workshops, and network with professionals. Be part of a community dedicated to excellence.

name: NYU Stern Undergraduate Advising
description: The NYU Stern Undergraduate Office of Advising provides one-on-one assistance to help students make informed academic decisions throughout their college careers. The Academic Advising team offers guidance on various topics, including course scheduling, exploring areas of study, selecting minors, study away planning, co-curricular activities, and holds events that support students' academic success. These events include Advising on Location (AOL), Quick Visits (every M-F 3:00-4:30 pm), and opportunities to bring students together with their advisers to de-stress and connect.
summary: The Undergraduate Advising Office at NYU Stern provides personalized assistance to students to help them navigate their college careers.

name: SPS Division of Applied Undergraduate Studies (DAUS)
description:
Welcome to the SPS Division of Applied Undergraduate Studies (DAUS).

Join us for Division-wide programming that will enhance your academic journey at the NYU School of Professional Studies
summary: Welcome to the SPS Division of Applied Undergraduate Studies (DAUS).

name: South Asian Health Awareness
description:
The South Asian Health AwaReness Club (SAHAR) grew out of the South Asian Health and Research Group! Our club aims to address SA health disparities through awareness, multidisciplinary collaborations, and community and student involvement in the field of public health. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds and ethnicities.

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: The mission of SAHAR is to address and reduce South Asian health disparities and expose the next generation to develop culturally sensitive and relevant skill sets to meet the health needs of South Asians.

name: Student National Medical Association
description:
The NYU Chapter of the Student National Medical Association (SNMA) is dedicated to supporting current and future underrepresented minority medical students, promoting cultural competency in medicine, and addressing the health needs of underserved communities. Through mentorship, service, and advocacy, we strive to empower students and advance health equity both within NYU and beyond.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Student National Medical Association (SNMA) is dedicated to supporting current and future underrepresented minority medical students, promoting cultural competency in medicine, and addressing the health needs of underserved communities.

name: Health Career Collaborative
description:
The Health Career Collaborative (HCC) is a nationwide program with 25 branches that provides an exciting link between high school students and teaching partners in health and medicine to bring to life the possibilities of further studies and careers in the health professions. To do this, we have established a sustainable partnership between NYU and a local Title I high school to provide mentorship, an engaging health curriculum, and exposure to health careers to high school students from low-income, underrepresented minority communities.

 

The three-year curriculum is case-based and designed to foster active hands-on learning. In the 10th grade, students encounter cases commonly seen in the emergency room, exploring a range of questions associated with each case related to epidemiology, clinical presentation, complications, diagnosis, work-up, and multidisciplinary management. In 11th grade, the students deepen their knowledge with public health modules, such as trauma, nutrition, and sexually transmitted infections. Finally, in 12th grade, the students identify a healthcare need of their community and design a community health initiative (such as a health fair) to help address it.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The mission of the Health Career Collaborative is to provide mentorship, engaging health curriculum, and exposure to health careers to high school students from low-income, underrepresented minority communities.

summary: NYU Advance is a student-led community at the intersection of medicine and healthcare innovation. We aim to introduce and educate medical students about the process of innovation in healthcare.

name: GSAS Graduate Fellowships and Awards
description:
Welcome! GSAS offers fellowship opportunities and support for graduate students and graduate alumni. We offer comprehensive fellowship advising and application guidance on many fellowship opportunities, such as Fulbright, Boren, NYU-specific fellowships, and more.

For more information about fellowships, please visit our website here.
summary: GSAS offers fellowship opportunities and support for graduate students and graduate alumni. We offer comprehensive fellowship advising and application guidance on Fulbright, Boren, NYU-specific fellowships, and much more.

name: SPS Emerging Technologies Student Association
description:
The Emerging Technologies Student Association (ETSA) at NYU SPS explores how emerging technologies connect to real-world impact. From AI to virtual production and assistive tech, we focus on understanding how innovation is shaping the way we work, create, and build across industries.

Through curated events, hands-on sessions, and industry conversations, we create space to learn, experiment, and stay ahead. We are a multicultural community bringing together perspectives from around the world—making our approach to technology more global, inclusive, and forward-thinking.
summary: ETSA at NYU SPS bridges emerging technologies with real-world business impact, equipping students with the strategic insight, practical exposure, and network to understand, evaluate, and lead innovation responsibly.

summary: JPI is a student-led research, news, and political commentary online newspaper from the International Relations graduate program at New York University.

name: NYU Financial Education
description: We offer innovative workshops, resources, and coaching services designed to incrementally strengthen your financial knowledge during your time at NYU and beyond. By engaging with us, you will grow your financial acumen, develop a skill set that will allow you to feel confident about money management, and learn how to think critically about current and future financial choices.
summary: The Office of Financial Education is dedicated to providing support for our students related to personal finance and money management thereby developing a culture of financial empowerment and success.

description: The Master’s College at the Graduate School of Arts and Science provides access to information, advisement, and resources for prospective and current master's students as they focus on their scholarly, professional, and personal/leadership development. Led by a student-run Program Board, the Master's College helps organize a variety of social, cultural, academic, and professional programming for all GSAS Master's students.
summary: The Master’s College at the Graduate School of Arts and Science provides access to information, advisement, and resources for prospective and current master's students as they focus on their scholarly, professional, and personal/leadership development.

description: This club is for SPS Graduate students who are interested in Financial Planning.
summary: This club is for SPS Graduate students who are interested in Financial Planning.

name: Artists Anonymous at NYU - All-University
description: Artists Anonymous plans to provide students who do not have the coursework, time, or art supplies, with a creative outlet on campus. Through this club, we will allow students a collaborative space to maintain, improve, and explore their artistic skills while learning from fellow artists at NYU. We will be focused on the fine arts including painting, drawing, pen and ink, mixed media and more.
summary: Artists Anonymous allows students a collaborative space to maintain, improve, and explore their artistic skills while learning from fellow artists at NYU.

name: BIPOC Legal Society - All-University
description: BIPOC Legal Society stands as a beacon of information and inclusivity for students of color passionate about pursuing careers in the legal profession. Acknowledging the systemic barriers that disproportionately affect our communities, we dedicate ourselves to providing tailored resources and support to navigate these challenges. Our mission is to forge a dynamic community where students can collaboratively demystify the pre-law journey, share knowledge, and actively engage in enriching activities that pave the way for success in the legal profession. We commit to realizing this vision through a curated series of social gatherings, workshops, and informational sessions designed to facilitate meaningful networking opportunities with peers, distinguished alumni, and seasoned legal professionals. More information can be found at: https://www.nyubipoclegalsociety.org/ or Instagram @nyubipoclegalsociety.
summary: Our mission is to create a space where scholars of color can learn, share, and engage in activities that demystify the pre-law process, create community, and promote student engagement in the legal field.

name: World Cultures Club - All-University
description: We strive to help educate the NYU community about the different cultures present on campus by inviting students to share their voices and discuss their experiences. World Cultures Club embraces all cultures under one setting and allows for discussions to take place. This club also offers an opportunity for minority ethnic/national groups that don't have an established student group/club to use their voices to discuss their own experiences and share their culture with others. It is a truly multicultural club — no other club at NYU offers a place for all cultures to meet together and interact with each other. Here people are allowed to share their cultures with each other through activities, discussions and festivities.
summary: World Cultures Club is a multicultural club that aims to bridge the gap between cultural groups at NYU and create a safe space where different ethnic and national communities can learn from each other.

name: South Asian Women's Association - All-University
description: The mission of the South Asian Women’s Association (S.A.W.A.) at NYU is to provide female-identifying students of South Asian heritage a safe space for mental welfare and the opportunity to engage in service and participate in cultural, social, and career-building events while building solidarity amongst the NYU community. Our club will be a safe space for female-identifying students, with a focus on South Asian heritage. We encourage diversity and are looking to cater to both South Asian women as part of the diaspora, as well as those directly from South Asian countries. We are open to South Asian women of all nationalities with a focus on South Asian heritage. These heritages include but are not limited to Indian, Pakistani, Bengali, Sri Lankan, Nepalese, Bhuttanian, Burmese, Kashmiri, and Afghan.
summary: South Asian Women’s Association provides female-identifying students of South Asian heritage a safe space for mental welfare and the opportunity to engage in service and participate in cultural, social, and career-building events at NYU.

name: Saudi Students Association - All-University
description:
The Saudi Students Association (SSA) at NYU is a dynamic and inclusive community dedicated to connecting and supporting Saudi students across the university. Our mission is to create a home away from home for Saudi students and to welcome those from neighboring countries and beyond to explore our culture, showcasing Saudi Arabia's dedication to collaboration and regional unity. We are deeply committed to celebrating and sharing the rich cultural heritage of Saudi Arabia, promoting a deeper understanding and appreciation of our traditions, values, and history within the broader NYU community.

The SSA focuses on three main pillars to enrich our members' experiences:

  1. Social: We host cultural events and large gatherings where students can socialize, build friendships, and strengthen community ties. These events offer a welcoming space for meaningful conversations and connections.

  2. Recreational: Our recreational activities and games provide fun and engaging opportunities for members to relax and enjoy themselves. These events foster teamwork and camaraderie through shared experiences.

  3. Career: We support our members' professional growth through workshops, recruitment events, and guest speaker sessions. These events aim to enhance career development, provide networking opportunities, and prepare students for future success.

Through these diverse and enriching events, the SSA aims to represent Saudi Arabia authentically and vibrantly, providing a platform for cultural exchange and lifelong friendships. Join us to experience the warmth of Saudi hospitality and to be part of a thriving, culturally enriched community at NYU.
summary: The SSA at NYU fosters a vibrant community for Saudi students, celebrating and sharing Saudi cultural heritage. Our mission is to promote unity, cultural understanding, and support through authentic representation and meaningful connections.

name: The Egyptian Union - All-University
description: Egyptian or not, The Egyptian Union will serve as a hub for the NYU student body who are interested in Egyptian culture and history, and building relationships with the Egyptian community in NYC and Egypt.
summary: The Egyptian Union aims to provide a space where students can indulge in the 5,000 year-long story of Egyptian culture. It will serve as a hub for pertinent Egyptian topics, as well as a supporting organization for Egyptian & Arabic students at NYU.

name: Half Asian Peoples Association at NYU - All-University
description:
Half Asian Peoples Association aims to foster an environment for part-Asian students across NYU by creating a community for students that identify with being Hapa. We want to create a safe space where students can express their culture and individuality.

Follow us on Instagram @nyu.hapa
summary: Half Asian Peoples Association aims to foster an environment for part-Asian students across NYU by creating a community for students that identify with being Hapa. We want to create a safe space where students can express their culture and individuality.

name: Anthropozine - All-University
description: Anthropozine welcomes any student interested in environmental matters, but especially for those who wish to contribute something new and innovative to the university. Whether it be an op-ed, film, art piece, or even a study done independently, Anthropozine will curate and publish works online that align with a sustainable, forward-thinking environmental theme that can inspire the NYU community. We are committed to showcasing and exploring various environmental values and causes through these media forms in order to further educate our student community about environmentalism. Visit our Linktree to learn more!
summary: Anthropozine is a media collective that offers a platform for students passionate about the environment to create and curate art, academic resources, and other expressions of environmentalism for public display to the NYU community.

name: Product Management Club - All-University
description:
The Product Management Club is a community at NYU that connects students interested in Product and Product-adjacent roles with resources, mentorship, and workshops so they can tackle diverse problems faced by businesses and consumers. Our club encourages curiosity and teamwork as we inspire students to build exciting products and explore PM in various industries.

Website: nyupmc.com

View all our links here!
summary: The mission of the Product Management Club at NYU shall be to build a community of students interested in the interdisciplinary career of Product Management (PM) and help them develop the diverse skills needed to succeed in this role.

name: Middle School BASIS
description:
Middle School BASIS is an organization devoted to helping 8th grade students be as prepared as possible for high school and young adulthood! Every few weeks, we go to the local middle school, Salk School of Science, to teach a class on a variety of topics, such as mental health, sex education, and drugs/alcohol. Opportunities are also available to mentor middle school students.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Middle School BASIS is an organization devoted to helping 8th grade students be as prepared as possible for high school and young adulthood!

name: NYU Student Association for Psychedelic Studies
description:
The NYU Student Association for Psychedelic Studies (SAPS) offers education and networking opportunities to the NYU Graduate student body, considering the latest advancements in psychedelic research and practice and their implications in social work and psychotherapeutic settings. By bringing together students, researchers, and practitioners from diverse fields and backgrounds, SAPS creates a unique platform for sharing knowledge and sparking innovation and ethical practice. We are dedicated to equipping future clinicians and researchers with a thorough understanding of the current landscape and future prospects of psychedelic-assisted therapies. Our activities include hosting seminars, workshops, and guest lectures from leading experts in the field, facilitating rich academic dialogue and practical insights.

The field of psychotherapy is currently experiencing a renaissance of psychedelic research and treatment modalities. Though regularly practiced in indigenous communities, prohibition imposed an injunction on psychedelic research in the first half of the 20th Century. Recent changes in cultural perception and legislation has ushered in a new era of psychedelic integration in the Western clinical setting. Clinical psychedelic assisted psychotherapy has shown exceptionally positive outcomes in the treatment of trauma, depression, anxiety, addiction, grief cycles and end-of-life anxiety. Future clinicians will be well served by a fluency and familiarity with the emerging research in this exciting and rapidly growing field. Current estimates place MDMA therapies becoming legally available to licensed clinicians in the near future, with psilocybin already becoming legal for psychotherapy in a growing number of U.S. states. Ketamine therapy is currently practiced widely by mental health professionals and has well documented success when integrated with traditional talk therapy to address treatment resistant depression.

Members of the NYU Student Association for Psychedelic Studies will enter the job market uniquely prepared to participate in cutting edge research, deploy strengths based strategies for harm reduction and offer effective, safe and accessible therapy grounded in the principles which guide the field of social work and psychotherapy.

 

To join the group, please follow the link below to join our WhatsApp channel! This is the primary method through which we connect, organize, and communicate:

https://chat.whatsapp.com/BQC9cTndvMT6JPFOgmTNlU
summary: NYU's Student Association for Psychedelic Studies offers networking and educational opportunities to advance interdisciplinary and ethical psychedelic assisted psychotherapy and research.  Join our learning community for workshops, potlucks & symposiums

name: Latino Pre-Health Society
summary: .The mission of this club is to provide guidance to Latino students interested in pursuing all sorts of health careers by providing opportunities, guidance and a sense of community support from other Latino students or Latino medical professionals.

name: Transracial Adoptee Social Work Collective
description: The Transracial Adoptee Social Work Collective (TRASWC) is an adoptee student-led group that offers support and community for transracial adoptees for MSW students at Silver. The goal of TRASWC is to serve as an inclusive space for adoptee social work students to discuss the unique challenges transracial adoptees (TRAs) social workers face in the field of social work, explore how our TRA identities influence our personal and professional lives, and provide opportunities for community building, professional development, and networking. TRASWC is committed to amplifying adoptee voices at NYU Silver and the larger social work community.
summary: The Transracial Adoptee Social Work Collective (TRASWC) is an adoptee student-led group that offers support and community for transracial adoptees for MSW students at Silver.

name: Women in Computing
description: NYU Women in Computing is a student run club which provides a community for NYU students, faculty, and professionals from different companies around New York. We often hold tech talks by female engineers, recruiting events, and other events partnering with tech companies. We have worked with companies such as Facebook, Google, Spotify, Datadog, and Goldman Sachs. Come to our events to learn about scholarships, internships, and full-time jobs, and learn new skills from our club's eboard, or even join the eboard yourself! There are no specific requirements for becoming a member, just come to any of our events and you will become a part of NYU WinC!
summary: NYU Women in Computing is a student run club which provides a community for NYU students, faculty, and professionals from different companies around New York. We often hold tech talks by female engineers, recruiting events, and other events.

name: Business and Political Economy
description:
NYU Stern’s Business and Political Economy (BPE) degree merges liberal arts, business fundamentals, social impact learning, and global experiences, while still offering you the flexibility to pursue elective courses and minors across New York University.

The two required semesters of study away will give you a real-world understanding of today’s global business environment and satisfy the Global Experience requirement. 

The BPE degree will prepare you for a broad array of international business careers at multinational corporations, in government, at non-profit organizations, and more.

NYU Stern works with faculty, alumni, and leading industry partners to develop programs that help you explore careers, prepare for recruitment, and connect with employers. As a BPE student, you will have access to a wide range of professional development programs and opportunities.
summary: NYU Stern’s Business and Political Economy (BPE) degree merges liberal arts, business fundamentals, social impact learning, and global experiences, while still offering you the flexibility to pursue elective courses and minors across New York University.

name: Center for Undergraduate Research
description:
Welcome to Undergraduate Research

The goal of college isn’t to offer you knowledge – it’s to teach you how to find it, how to use it, how to create it.  Undergraduate research is at the heart of that goal, because it gives you a chance to ask questions.  You came to a big school, in a big city, connected to a global university.  Undergraduate research helps you take advantage of that size, while making the college experience feel small and personal.  When you participate in undergraduate research, you work closely with the world’s greatest faculty on their cutting edge projects – and your own.  You make connections between your experiences and your classes.  You apply that learning to the real world. You share what you find. And then you start all over again.

Undergraduate Research at NYU.  Come for the answers, stay for the questions.
summary: Undergraduate Research

name: Anand Khubani Business, Technology and Entrepreneurship
description:
NYU Stern’s Anand Khubani BS in Business, Technology and Entrepreneurship (Khubani BTE) program is a four-year, STEM-certified degree that develops the next generation of business leaders with the entrepreneurial mindset and business and technology competencies to play transformational roles in the 21st century.

The degree merges liberal arts, business fundamentals, social impact learning, a Global Experience requirement, entrepreneurship immersion, and relevant tech competencies, while still offering you the flexibility to pursue elective courses and minors across New York University.

BTE students will go through the program as a cohort, taking experiential courses in technology and entrepreneurship to gain both technical knowledge in analytics and coding along with an aptitude for managing uncertainty and ambiguity. Some of your required courses, as well as your electives, will tap talent from across NYU, including faculty from NYU’s Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences and Tandon School of Engineering.
summary: The Khubani BTE program is a four-year, STEM-certified degree that develops the next generation of business leaders with the entrepreneurial mindset and business and technology competencies to play transformational roles in the 21st century

name: BS/MS in Accounting
description:
The BS/MS in Accounting dual degree program provides you with the opportunity to earn two distinct degrees. The BS degree is typically completed in four years or less at the Stern Undergraduate College. Upon completion, you transition to the Stern Graduate School to earn an MS Degree in one accelerated summer. In doing so, you will have acquired the 150 credits required to become a licensed CPA in New York State, as well as in most other states.

With the power of two Stern degrees, you will join a global network of alumni who enjoy exciting careers in accounting, advisory, consulting, investment banking, and entrepreneurship.  

Admission is open as early as spring of your first year.
summary: The BS/MS in Accounting dual degree program provides you with the opportunity to earn two distinct degrees, an undergraduate BS degree and a MS degree in Accounting.

name: BlackGen Capital
description: BlackGen Capital is a student investment fund. We close the access gap by creating a pipeline for talented students both unrepresented and represented in the financial services industry. BlackGen Capital members are provided with a standardized 10-week educational series, where students learn the fundamentals of finance. After receiving this thorough training, students have a unique opportunity to propose equity investment ideas to the BlackGen Capital investment fund. Along with our education, our students have direct access to corporate sponsors and exclusive career opportunities.
summary: BlackGen Capital is a student investment fund. We close the access gap by creating a pipeline for talented students both unrepresented and represented in the financial services industry.

name: LaLaHaHa Musical Comedy
description: Lalahaha is a musical sketch comedy group. Come audition for us in the fall! There will be audition information on our instagram @lalahahanyu for any questions about the club email lalahahanyu@gmail.com
summary: LaLaHaHa is NYU’s first ever musical comedy group!

name: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc - All-University
description:
Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated is a historically Black Greek-letter organization founded January 16th, 1920, by five ladies at Howard University in Washington, DC. These five women, also known as our Five Pearls, dared to depart from the traditional coalitions for black women and sought to establish a new organization predicated on the precepts of Scholarship, Service, Sisterly Love and Finer Womanhood. Although membership is granted exclusively by invitation; Zeta Phi Beta Sorority upholds its motto of being a “community conscious, action oriented organization.”

 

It was the ideal of the Founders; Arizona Cleaver, Myrtle Tyler, Viola Tyler, Pearl Neal and Fannie Pettie, that the sorority would reach college women in all parts of the country who were sorority-minded and desired to follow the founding principles of the organization. As so famously stated by the Most Honorable Triumphant Founder Viola Tyler, “There is a Zeta in every girl regardless of race, creed or color, who has high standards and principles, a good scholarly average and an active interest in all things that she undertakes to accomplish.”

 

As an organization who has achieved many firsts, such as the first to be constitutionally bound to a fraternity Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated, the first sorority to charter chapters in Africa, the first sorority to form adult and youth auxiliary groups, and much more; Zeta Phi Beta Sorority continues to live out its commitment to uplift their community by exemplifying the goals set forth by their Founders.

 

Contact Information

Email: zetaphibetanyu@gmail.com

Headquarters:

1734 New Hampshire Avenue, NW

Washington, DC, 20009
summary: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated is a historically Black Greek-letter organization founded January 16th, 1920, by five ladies at Howard University in Washington, DC.

name: La Unidad Latina, Lambda Upsilon Lambda Fraternity, Inc. - All-University
description:
La Unidad Latina, Lambda Upsilon Lambda Fraternity Incorporated primarily seeks to take a leadership role in meeting the needs of the Latino community through academic achievement, cultural awareness, brotherhood, community service, and promotion of the Latino culture.

Since the Spring of 1994, the Omicron Chapter at New York University has coordinated and executed cultural events and service initiatives seeking to address the plight of Latinos within the New York City community while also promoting academic and professional development for students of color. Our Hermanos have taken key leadership roles on campus and have won the very distinguished NYU 'Presidents' award for leadership & programming, an award given to us on four different occasions.
summary: La Unidad Latina, Lambda Upsilon Lambda Fraternity Incorporated primarily seeks to take a leadership role in meeting the needs of the Latino community through academic achievement, cultural awareness, community service and promotion of the Latino culture

summary: Knife Dog Play Festival pairs teams of playwrights, directors, and actors to produce small one acts that are presented in a one-night festival in November.

name: Counselors for Social Justice | NYU Chapter
description: Counselors for Social Justice are counselors or future counselors who recognize the systemic barriers that counselors, students, clients, schools, communities, and minorities face both within the profession of counseling and in society as a whole. This chapter aims to use its power, privilege, and passion to help address disparities, inequities, and other obstacles that our members and the NYU counseling community may face in the profession. There will also be a focus on social justice at large, to be able to include those who are not represented within the CSJ NYU and to be able to tackle widespread, pressing issues of social injustice. We will use personal and collective activism to approach issues; collaboration will be key in addressing these matters, and the involvement of CSJ NYU members will aim to establish a partnership, curiosity, and influence towards the social injustices addressed, both inside and outside this chapter. We will learn how to incorporate our activism into our roles as students, future clinicians, clinicians, clients, educators, researchers, or other applicable positions through the community of CSJ NYU, building relationships with others and impacting those around us through our passion for social justice, social change, and mental health counseling. Further, this chapter will try to establish a community that fosters connection for students. It will also allow students opportunities to take part in furthering their education, training, research, social justice activism, meetings, volunteer positions, and others that go beyond what is taught in classes at NYU. The purpose of which is to construct professional identities within mental health counseling that promotes social justice advocacy. Lastly, this chapter commits itself to social justice, equal opportunity, equity, multiculturalism, other progressive causes, and most importantly- its members. All members are encouraged to share their identities and work towards their own individual achievements regarding how they perceive social justice. The CSJ NYU will do its absolute best to create a space that is safe, welcoming, encouraging, and fosters growth for everyone involved for ALL members.
summary: Counselors for Social Justice is a club for counselors and future counselors who are interested in broadening their views on how to integrate social justice into their practice.

name: Mou Theatre
description: Mou Theatre is a production-oriented Mandarin theatre club that aims at promoting theatre co-creation and cultural communication. As a student club at Steinhardt, we recognize the importance of culture and the unique community bonding that theatre provides. Thus, Mou recognizes the necessity to gather our own club to allow for creative co-creations in our own language.
summary: Mou Theatre is a production-oriented Mandarin theatre club that aims to promote theatre co-creation and cultural communication. We host monthly play-reading events and theatre production-related workshops.

SCRIBE acts as the liaison between the programs and the SPS student council and helps students build community, network, and prepare for careers in their respective fields.
summary: SCRIBE represents and supports the students in the School of Professional Studies’ (SPS) Publishing, Professional Writing, and Translation and Interpreting programs.

description:
The Public Health and Medicine (PHAM) Society aims to create a dialogue around the pivotal intersection of public health and clinical medicine in the prevention and control of the disease. The PHAM Society also aims to help both pre-medical and post-medical students to achieve their career goals by providing scientific research and community service opportunities. We are committed to raising awareness of health inequality and inequity in marginalized populations by collaborating with humanitarian NGOs and hosting informative guest lectures.

All GPH club events are open to all, inclusive of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: The Public Health and Medicine (PHAM) Society aims to create a dialogue around the pivotal intersection of public health and clinical medicine in the prevention and control of the disease.

Our ultimate goal is to share the uniqueness of traditional Chinese music with the Steinhardt community.
summary: The mission of the organization is to build a community at NYU that provides students with the opportunities to connect with those who share the same passion for Chinese traditional music and to experiment with different styles of music.

name: Tandon Consulting Club
description: The Tandon Consulting Club is a student-led organization dedicated to helping all NYU students launch and grow careers in consulting. We bridge the students’ strong technical and analytical foundation with the problem-solving and strategy skills required in the consulting industry. Through case interview prep, career workshops, alumni panels, and networking sessions, we provide members with the tools and confidence to navigate recruitment and excel in interviews. Our specialized programming is tailored for students pursuing opportunities in technology, engineering, and beyond, while fostering a close community of peers, mentors, and professionals. We also host case competitions and collaborate with leading consulting firms to bring real-world perspectives and opportunities directly to our members. TCC has been recognized with awards for Outstanding Academic Collaborations and Best New Initiative, highlighting our commitment to innovation and impact within the NYU community.
summary: The Tandon Consulting Club is a dynamic and student-driven organization committed to offering a unique and enriching experience in the field of consulting to all NYU students.

Our events are open to everyone, just fill out the RSVP form.
