name: Center for Latin American and Caribbean Studies
description:
The Center for Latin American and Caribbean Studies (CLACS) was founded in 1966, as the first area studies center at NYU. Today it is one of the premier centers of its kind in the country.

The Center’s national and international reputation derives from the strength of its approximately 130 research-active affiliated faculty. Among them are presidents of national professional associations, members of the National Academy, finalists for the Pulitzer Prize, recipients of Guggenheims, NEH, ACLS, Ford, Rockefeller, MacArthur and other prestigious fellowships and honors.

Together with Columbia University’s Institute of Latin American Studies, CLACS received federal funding through the Title VI Foreign Language and Area Studies Fellowships Program. CLACS has been a past awardee of Title VI National Resource Center funding. CLACS was also selected as an inaugural member of the Tinker Foundation Field Research Collaborative.

CLACS hosts faculty and student research abroad, as well as faculty working groups and faculty-directed area studies initiatives: The Caribbean Initiative, the Brazil Initiative, and the Colombian Studies Initiative. CLACS hosts extensive public programming, from public lectures to film series and a range of events and workshops for K-12 teachers.

The Center is highly regarded for its rigorous, interdisciplinary MA program, one of the oldest and most successful in the field. We have joint MA programs with Global Journalism, Museum Studies, Law, and Library Science. The Center’s Quechua and Haitian Creole language programs offer rigorous language training through the intermediate level.
summary: The Center for Latin American and Caribbean Studies is a nexus for scholars, students and community in New York and beyond.

name: Biomimetic Society
description: Our mission is to advance the practice of Biomimetic dentistry at NYU College of Dentistry by promoting conservative and minimally invasive dental techniques among students and faculty. Biomimetic dentistry is a branch of restorative dentistry that uses treatments to maintain and save the natural tooth structure using materials that mimic the natural teeth. We are creating the Biomimetic Society at NYU College Of Dentistry to serve as a platform to deepen the understanding of biomimetic principles and techniques, bring students who share a common interest together, pool resources and expertise together, explore innovative solutions to dental challenges, and raise awareness about minimally invasive dentistry in the college. The Biomimetic Society would provide students with valuable educational and professional opportunities while making a positive impact on the college community and the field of dentistry. Through lectures, literature reviews, hands-on sessions, and case presentations, our club wants to provide high-quality Knowledge patient care with a modern approach to dentistry, to promote research and education in this field, and foster collaboration among dental professionals to improve oral health outcomes and patient satisfaction. Our club would focus on discussing and promoting the use of adhesive chemistry to return teeth to original strain harmony.
summary: Description

NYU Campus Recreation serves 15,000+ students annually through a variety of offerings, opportunities, and experiences that transforms, inspires, educates, and supports the student experience at New York University. Through our club sports, intramural leagues, fitness offerings, esports, and more, we aspire to be a key component of your individual well-being and development every day at NYU. description: Rogue Aerospace is NYU’s leading rocketry association. At Rogue Aerospace students learn and practice how to design, build, and launch reusable rockets. As a Vertically Integrated Project at NYU’s Tandon School of Engineering, we educate and train future aerospace engineers on a variety of skill sets from mechatronics to computer-aided designs and dynamics simulation software.
summary: NYU’s rocketry association dedicated to the advancement and exploration of aerospace

name: Lifestyle Medicine Interest Group
description:
The NYUGSOM Lifestyle Medicine Interest Group is a chapter of the American College of Lifestyle Medicine, the medical professional society for physicians and other professionals dedicated to clinical and worksite practice of lifestyle medicine (LM) as the foundation of a transformed and sustainable health care system. Practice involves the use of evidence-based therapeutic approaches, such as eating a predominantly whole food, plant-based diet, getting regular physical activity, adequate sleep, managing stress, avoiding use of risky substances and pursuing other non-drug modalities, to treat, reverse, and prevent chronic disease.

General Objectives of the Lifestyle Medicine Interest Group:

- Foster a space for students, faculty, and professionals who are interested in LM for connecting and professional advancement

- Promote healthier lifestyles in the NYUGSOM community and local community through LM education and activities

- Establish or improve LM curriculum at NYU Grossman through advocacy.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The NYUGSOM Lifestyle Medicine Interest Group is a chapter of the American College of Lifestyle Medicine, the medical professional society for physicians and other professionals dedicated to clinical and worksite practice of lifestyle medicine.

name: NYU Student Wellbeing
summary: NYU Student Wellbeing facilitates two peer-to-peer support, peer-led, programs. Listening Labs and RADical Health. Both programs are designed to help students connect and gain the skills and tools to move towards flourishing at NYU.

name: CAS Class of 2027
description: Our board is here to serve all seniors in the College of Arts and Science through advocacy and programming. We also strive to act as a liaison between you and administration. We have created monthly newsletters that are organized so there can be a direct line of communication between us and you; we will keep you all updated with what we are working on, early announcements from administration, and upcoming events. name: Women in Medicine (WIM) Association at NYU - All-University
summary: The Women in Medicine Association at NYU provides a safe space to learn and discuss issues of female-identifying representation in the healthcare industry. The mission of this club is to empower women to pursue their career goals in healthcare.

name: She is The Music at NYU - All-University
description: As an organization, we aim to provide new opportunities to students interested in varying degrees of the industry, through our multi-faceted programming. While we are an organization rooted in empowering women and non-binary individuals, we open our doors to any members interested in pursuing a career in the music industry. We hope this can create not only professional connections but also a support system of lifelong friendships. With our unique array of professional programming stemming from panel discussions featuring music industry executives, collaborative songwriting roundtable workshops, and post-grad information sessions, we hope to break down the many barriers that have resulted in the current state of the industry. At She Is The Music: NYU, we firmly believe that change begins with us, and we are ready to make big waves in the industry.
summary: She is The Music at NYU provides opportunities to students interested in the music industry, through our multi-faceted programming. While we are an organization rooted in empowering women and non-binary individuals, we open our doors to any members.

name: Haus of Violets - All-University
description:
The mission of the Haus of Violets shall be to provide a safe, inclusive space for LBGTQIA+ students to participate and learn about queer ballroom culture. We are dedicated to creating a Haus that fosters queer creativity and pride through the arts of voguing, drag, runway and stage performance.

We welcome any and all students who are interested in participating in ballroom culture or learning more about the beautiful queer movement started in NYC's very own West Village.
summary: The Haus of Violets provides a safe, inclusive space for LBGTQIA+ students to participate and learn about queer ballroom culture. We are dedicated to creating a house that fosters queer creativity and pride through the arts of voguing, drag, and more.

name: Mexican Folklor at NYU - All-University
description:
Welcome to Mexican Folklor at NYU! With weekly dance practices as a club we will work together to choreograph, practice and hopefully perform dances from throughout Mexico.

**No experience is required to join the club and anyone can join**
summary: The mission of this organization is to bring to life Mexican Folklorico at NYU. Through learning, choreographing and performing, participants get to partake in the various dances that are part of Mexican culture.

name: Laya at NYU - All-University
description: The mission of LAYA @ NYU shall be to provide all dancers with Indian Classical Dance training an outlet to practice, perfect, and perform their art while simultaneously fostering a community of dancers to share, embrace, and preserve Indian heritage and respective backgrounds.
summary: LAYA at NYU provides all dancers with Indian Classical Dance training an outlet to practice, perfect, and perform their art while simultaneously fostering a community of dancers to share, embrace, and preserve Indian heritage and respective backgrounds.

name: Black in STEM - All-University
description: Our mission is to empower, support, and amplify the voices of black students pursuing STEM fields at NYU and beyond. We are dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive community that provides mentorship, resources, and opportunities for black scientists across all STEM disciplines. Through educational outreach, professional development, advocacy, and networking, we aim to inspire the next generation, advocate for equity in STEM, and drive positive change in the scientific community. Together, we strive to create a more inclusive and equitable future for black scientists, where their contributions are celebrated, valued, and recognized.
summary: Our mission is to empower black STEM students at NYU, providing mentorship, resources, and advocacy to promote equity and celebrate their achievements in STEM fields.

name: Urban Naturalist Club at NYU - All-University
description:
Our mission is to help students reconnect with the natural world around them, through guided experiences of New York City's parks, gardens, and other natural wonders. We hope to build an inclusive environment for all students, where they are free to learn about and discover the flora and fauna which whom we share the urban jungle. We also hope to provide formal and informal resources to students interested in the natural world, whether they want a career in related fields, or simply enjoy learning about our non-human neighbors.

Check out our event calendar for Spring Semester 2026: https://calendar.google.com/calendar/embed?src=urbannaturalistclub%40gmail.com&ctz=America%2FNew_York/

Join our iNaturalist Bioblitz project!: https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/urban-naturalist-club-nyu?tab=species
summary: Our mission is to help students reconnect with the natural world around them, through guided experiences of New York City's parks, gardens, and other natural wonders.

name: Yarn it Forward at NYU - All-University
description: Yarn it Forward provides an opportunity for NYU students to use knit and crochet crafts to give back to their community and support the New York City Rescue Mission. We are dedicated to educating students about the housing crisis and poverty within NYC, as well as teaching students how to knit and crochet. Yarn it Forward will provide a community for students of all backgrounds to create warm garments to donate.
summary: Yarn it Forward provides an opportunity for NYU students to use knit and crochet crafts to give back to their community and learn about the housing crisis and poverty within NYC.

name: The Piano Society at NYU - All-University
summary: The Piano Society aims to create opportunities for pianists to connect in a common desire to explore piano music. Through various piano-related events and initiatives, we aspire to cultivate a vibrant and inclusive community of NYU piano enthusiasts.

name: Pizza Club at NYU - All-University
description: Rating pizza to find the ultimate New York City slice.
summary: Pizza Club at NYU is for all students who are interested in discovering and exploring the great pizza in New York City.

name: Ukrainian Club at NYU - All-University
description: The mission of the Organization shall be to acquaint the NYU community with the culture, traditions and mentality of one of the largest Eastern European countries. The club is dedicated to expand the knowledge and provide an immersive experience of the heartwarming Ukrainian culture through teaching language, history, modern culture of the Ukrainian people and tasting cuisine. In our club we will be happy to see NYU students of all backgrounds interested in Ukrainian culture as well as the Ukrainian students themselves.
summary: Ukrainian Club is dedicated to expand the knowledge and provide an immersive experience of the heartwarming Ukrainian culture through teaching language, history, modern culture of the Ukrainian people and tasting cuisine.

name: Point of Care Ultrasound (POCUS) Club
description:
The POCUS Club will have a dual mission of teaching all interested students hands-on POCUS skills and preparing teams of interested students to compete in local/national POCUS competitions (such as ZonoSlam and SonoSlam). POCUS is a useful bedside diagnostic modality across multiple organ systems and clinical scenarios, including checking for free fluid in trauma patients (eFAST exam), checking for deep venous thromboses, and evaluating for volume status. It is also a useful tool in procedures, including ultrasound guided IV and nerve blocks. Through teaching and hands-on practice with workshops, we will improve students' POCUS skills. Through coaching and mock POCUS competitions, we will prepare students to compete in teams at local/national competitions.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The POCUS Club will have a dual mission of teaching all interested students hands-on POCUS skills and preparing teams of interested students to compete in local/national POCUS competitions (such as ZonoSlam and SonoSlam).

name: Psi Upsilon - All-University
description:
Psi Upsilon is an international co-ed philanthropic and social fraternity on the Interfraternity Council.

Psi Upsilon was founded on November 24, 1833 by seven students at Union College. Today, over 45,000 members have been initiated into Psi U on 50 campuses in the United States and Canada promoting our values of Lifelong Friendship, Moral Leadership, Intellectual Engagement, Responsible Social Conduct, and Service to Society. 

The Delta Chapter was founded on Febuary 11, 1837, making us the oldest Fraternity Chapter at NYU! Our mission statement: "United in friendship, Psi Upsilon members aspire to moral, intellectual, and social excellence in themselves as they seek to inspire these values in society." 

Our Chapter's philanthropic efforts focus on fighting food insecurity in the New York City metropolitan area, and we often work with non-profit organizations to forward this cause.
summary: International Co-ed Fraternity on the Interfraternity Council

description:
The Society For Women in Sport at NYU is dedicated to fostering a supportive and inclusive community that empowers students interested in sports.
Our mission is to advance gender equality in sports, both on and off the field, by advocating for fair pay, equal job opportunities, and increased representation of women. We exist to bridge the gap between aspiring professionals and accomplished women in the field, creating opportunities for growth, networking, and discussion.
summary: The Society For Women in Sports fosters a community that empowers students interested in sports. We aim to bridge the gap between aspiring professionals and accomplished women in the field, creating opportunities for growth, networking, and discussion.

We are dedicated to creating an environment that promotes cultural experiences, exchange, awareness, and appreciation by exploring and sharing Nepal's traditions, customs, and heritage.

name: The Chemists' Club - NYU Washington Square Chapter
description:
Founded in 1898, The Chemists' Club is a non-profit 501(c)(3) educational organization whose membership is open to anyone with an interest in the chemical sciences, including students (graduate and undergraduate), chemists, chemical engineers, consultants, security analysts, attorneys, and other professions including academia.
 
Student members are welcomed and are assisted in: making informed career choices; preparing them for the professional world; and, helping them get jobs. Students are given many opportunities to interact with members of the industry to foster their growth. Such interaction gives students insight into how they can better prepare themselves to be contributing members of the scientific community. Young professional members benefit from networking experiences and learning from more senior members. Insight into industry and academia reaches past what can be taught in formal school settings, preparing our young members for fulfilling and successful careers.
summary: The Chemists' Club is a city-wide networking organization open to anyone with an interest in the chemical sciences, including students (graduate and undergraduate). The club hosts several events a year both on campus and throughout the city.

name: Medical Students with Disability and Chronic Illness
description:
The goal of this organization is to bring together a community of medical students passionate about supporting and empowering medical students with disabilities and/or chronic medical illnesses and advancing the improvement of healthcare for those with disabilities and/or chronic illnesses.

Disability remains deeply stigmatized in modern-day society, and consequences of this stigmatization echo around us even now. Roughly half of all NYC subway stations are accessible. Persons with disability are a minority and oftentimes vulnerable population; they are also underrepresented in many professions, including medicine and science. In the pursuit of equality and equity, as healthcare workers we should treat all patients equally and to the best of our abilities; similarly, we should use our privilege and resources to uphold and advocate for those who may not be able to themselves. We must also educate ourselves about our patients and patient populations in order to be best equipped to help them. In that light, this organization hopes to disseminate accurate and original information (e.g. personal accounts) about disability in both a general societal and professional setting, and encourage our members to use that knowledge to better help persons with disability in whatever avenue they see fit.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The goal of this organization is to bring together a community of medical students passionate about supporting and empowering medical students with disabilities and/or chronic medical illnesses, to educate and encourage ourselves to advocate for others.

name: NYU Tel Aviv
description: A multidimensional Mediterranean city, Tel Aviv is the cultural, financial, and technological center of Israel. When you study abroad at NYU Tel Aviv, you will gain a sophisticated understanding of Israel and the Middle East. It is an ideal place to study not only politics and the Middle East but also emerging media and the sciences. Additionally, through the academic internship program, NYU Tel Aviv partners with an internship provider, allowing students to intern for course credit in many industries.
summary: A multidimensional Mediterranean city, Tel Aviv is the cultural, financial, and technological center of Israel. When you study abroad at NYU Tel Aviv, you will gain a sophisticated understanding of Israel and the Middle East.

name: SPS Integrated Marketing & Communications Department
description:
The NYU Integrated Marketing & Communications Department in the School of Professional Studies is where academic excellence meets practical, real-world preparation amidst the energy and dynamism of New York City.

 

In the heart of the media, marketing, and public relations capital of the world, our experienced team of practitioner faculty members empowers graduate students as they launch, change, and advance their careers in marketing and public relations, with three powerhouse graduate degree programs. Access life-changing networking opportunities, earn internships at top New York City agencies and organizations, engage in active student organizations, interact with leading industry leaders, and gain best practices from experienced professionals at the top of their game.

 

Own your place in the center of everything. Advance your career. Enroll in one of our three graduate degree programs.

 

For Experienced, Senior-Level Professionals

Executive MS in Marketing and Strategic Communications

Designed by senior-level professionals for marketing and PR executives on the rise, this 5-semester program features 1 week in-person at the start + 4 days in-person at the end. The rest is online, affording you the flexibility to complete your degree while working full time.

 

For Rising Professionals

MS in Integrated Marketing

Marketing is a growth driver with a broad range of applications—brand management, digital marketing, content marketing, marketing analytics, and search engine optimization—to name a few. Integrating all of these skills, this degree prepares you for a wide range of marketing jobs, teaching you to translate business concepts into competitive and differentiated products and services. Flexible part-time and full-time study options. Can be completed in as little as 18 months.

 

MS in Public Relations and Corporate Communications

This degree provides you with the skills and the hands-on experience needed to develop the public relations and communication strategies that elevate brands, build reputations, and manage crises across industries. Unlike conventional master’s in public relations, this degree stands at the crossroads of marketing and public relations, honing your writing and communications skills across a variety of digital platforms. Flexible part-time and full-time study options. Can be completed in as little as 18 months.
summary: The NYU SPS Integrated Marketing & Communications Department offers three graduate degree programs: MS in Integrated Marketing, MS in Public Relations and Corporate Communication and Executive MS in Marketing and Strategic Communications.

name: Child Life Uplifting Children + Kids
description:
The aim of this student club is to organize and implement a new volunteer program for NYUGSOM medical students to have longitudinal, impactful volunteer experiences with Bellevue's Child Life Program. Medical students will not only contribute to the holistic wellbeing of patients, but also learn about the importance of therapeutic play and child development. Volunteer sessions are generally Tuesday and Thursday from 10am-12pm or 2pm-4pm, and volunteers are expected to volunteer twice a month. 

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The aim of this student club is to organize and implement a new volunteer program for NYUGSOM medical students to have longitudinal, impactful volunteer experiences with Bellevue's Child Life Program.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. - All University
description:
Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority Incorporated® is comprised of more than 360,000 initiated members in graduate and undergraduate chapters located in 12 countries including the United States, Bahamas, Bermuda, Canada, Dubai, Germany, Japan, Liberia, Nigeria, South Korea, South Africa and the U.S. Virgin Islands. Led by International President & CEO Danette Anthony Reed of Dallas, Texas, Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, often is hailed as “America’s premier Greek-letter organization for African American women.”

Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated® was founded on a mission of five basic tenets that have remained unchanged since the sorority’s inception. Our mission is

  • to cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards,
  • to promote unity and friendship among college women,
  • to study and help alleviate problems concerning girls and women in order to improve their social stature,
  • to maintain a progressive interest in college life,
  • and to be of “Service to All Mankind.”
summary: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated, an international service organization, was founded on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C. in 1908. It is the oldest Greek-letter organization established by African American college-educated women

name: Gallatin Parkinson's Pals
summary: Our mission is to facilitate inter-generational connections between college students and people with Parkinson's Disease through weekly 1-on-1 conversations.

name: The American Classics Theater (TACT)
description: The American Classics Theater (TACT) redefines what it means to produce “American Classic” plays by staging significant one-acts and full-lengths by American playwrights. Our goal is to provide opportunities for students in all Tisch disciplines to practice theater making at a professional level. We strive to incorporate contemporary pieces by a broad range of playwrights into our programming while challenging and reimagining the works of past generations. TACT is committed to cultivating a team that reflects the intersectionality found at NYU, with diversity, equity, and inclusion embedded in every aspect of our work.
summary: TACT members explore the contexts, content, and connotations of classic American plays in a contemporary world through full-length performances, extensive text work, and open discussion.

name: Interdisciplinary Encounters
description: Interdisciplinary Encounters is a series of round-table discussions organized and facilitated by XE students to foster dialogue with NYU faculty about engaging in research that does not neatly fit within conventional disciplinary boundaries. Such discussions are meant to provide insight into interdisciplinarity as both a method and field of study that engages a plurality of critical and creative practices. These student-moderated conversations will address the potentials and challenges that come from crossing disciplinary boundaries, the status of hyper-specialization in academia today, as well as the possible futures of interdisciplinary thought and action.
summary: A series of round-table discussions organized and facilitated by XE students to foster dialogue with NYU faculty about engaging in research that does not neatly fit within conventional disciplinary boundaries

name: The Office of Undergraduate Academics
description: The Office of Undergraduate Academics is your go-to resource for information about undergraduate policies and procedures. We assist students, faculty, and staff with interpreting University and School policies and resolving academic issues. In addition, our office is dedicated to enhancing the undergraduate experience by providing opportunities for academic enrichment, including the Global Leaders and Scholars in STEM(GLASS) Program, BS/MS Track, Undergraduate Summer Research Program, Study Away, and Prehealth services. We are continually developing new initiatives tailored to meet the unique needs of engineering students. Whether you're seeking assistance with understanding academic policies or finding opportunities for research and study away, our team is here to help you navigate your academic path and achieve your goals.
summary: Welcome to UGA at NYU Tandon School of Engineering! At UGA, we believe in the power of hands-on learning and real-world problem-solving. Our mission is to support undergraduates as they explore new frontiers and tackle challenges that matter.

name: Special Situations Investing Group
description: SSIG is an undergraduate pre-professional organization focused on special situations investing, a part of the finance industry. Our mission is to raise awareness for the field, and create real-world career opportunities through our programs supported by close collaboration with the Finance Department and our unique Advisory Board.
summary: The Special Situations Investing Group (SSIG) provides students with the opportunity to learn more about areas of alternative investing such as restructuring and distressed debt, while also providing opportunities to develop connections in the field.

name: SPS BIPOC Student Union
description: The SPS BIPOC Student Union (SPS BSU) seeks to unite students of color at the NYU School of Professional Studies and the greater BIPOC community in New York City for connection, collaboration and continuous personal, professional and community growth and development. We provide an inclusive space for students of all identities to celebrate the power and potential within our varied cultures and empower one another through equitable access to opportunities, fostering wealth creation, and promoting social empowerment.
summary: The SPS BIPOC Student Union (SPS BSU) seeks to unite students of color at the NYU School of Professional Studies and the greater BIPOC community in New York City for connection, collaboration and continuous personal, professional and community growth.

name: SPS Cyber Club
description: The SPS Cyber Club at NYU's School of Professional Studies is a student-led organization dedicated to enhancing the academic and professional experiences of graduate students in the MS in Global Security, Conflict, and Cybercrime program. Our collective focuses on providing practical workshops, facilitating information sharing, and hosting career-focused sessions. Through these initiatives, we aim to equip members with the essential skills and knowledge required for success in the cybersecurity and global security fields. We plan to collaborate with industry experts, alumni, and faculty to ensure that our activities are relevant, impactful, and aligned with current industry trends.
summary: The Cyber Club at NYU's School of Professional Studies is a student-led organization dedicated to enhancing the academic and professional experiences of graduate students in the MS in Global Security, Conflict, & Cybercrime program.

name: All With Worldly Social Change
description:
All with Worldly Social Change aims to create an anti-oppressive space for NYU students to engage in radical political education and action. It is a coalition across NYU with roots in social work. Our mission is to critically examine and dismantle the oppressive roles social workers have historically played by providing education grounded in radical leftist and abolitionist traditions.

We are committed to centering the voices and experiences of marginalized students and communities through reciprocal knowledge sharing, critical pedagogy, and accessible organizing. Our collective upholds anti-racist, anti-capitalist, anti-colonial values and the self-determination of all oppressed peoples. We envision alternative practices of transformative justice, mutual aid, and community care that are collaborative, compassionate, and accountable to those we serve. Through political action, critical discussion, and imaginative community building, we seek to foster liberatory practices of social work education and organizing.

Our Core Values are Abolition, Advocacy, Ambition, Accountability, Accessibility, Wisdom, being (W)holistic, Social Justice, Sustainability, Community, Collectivism, Cultural Humility, and Creativity; all modeled after the letters “AWSC”.
summary: All with Worldly Social Change aims to create an anti-oppressive space for NYU students to engage in radical political education and action.

The Persian Student Dental Association intends to unify the Persian community on campus in pursuit of a deeper sense of cultural,
historical, and communal identity. We hope to provide a forum, composed of individuals interested in Persian culture and community
despite their difference in backgrounds. We are looking to create an environment where students can educate one another through
educational, cultural, and social events. NYU is a global school that is focused on creating a seamless connection between cultures and
nationalities. The Persian Dental Student Association aims to do just that, while also being a proud, informed voice to link student concern
and experiences with those in the broader community.

summary: The Gies Academic Society facilitates academic enrichment, cultural activities, and social events that promote the academic society’s goals and objectives through an academic and a social event each semester.

The Italian Dental Society at NYU College of Dentistry.

The members of this organization will consists of dental students with an interest in Italian
culture, language, and dentistry.

The Italian Dental Society at NYU College of Dentistry.

name: NYU Sounds
description: The mission of NYU Sounds is to create a community hub where student performers, musicians, and those with a passion for music can come together to connect and create in ways never before possible. We stand by the 3 Cs: community, connection, and collaboration. We create live music events around campus featuring artists in the NYU community. We create an open and friendly environment where students can connect and support each other through the power of music and performance, and create lifelong friendships and creative bonds. Finally we build a platform for NYU artists to collaborate and make newly envisioned music and sounds.
summary: The mission of NYU Sounds is to create a community hub where student performers, musicians, and those with a passion for music can come together to connect and create in ways never before possible.

name: Chinese Artist Association
description: Chinese Artists Association (CAA) at the Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development is a vibrant community that promotes interdisciplinary collaboration and cultural exchange with Chinese artists, art educators and enthusiasts. Engage with us through exhibitions, performances, workshops, and seminars across various artistic and arts management disciplines, such as music, dance, theatre, visual arts, music business and art administration, while building valuable networks within and beyond the NYU community.
summary: The mission of the Chinese Artists Association (CAA) is to serve as a platform for Chinese artists at NYU, fostering collaboration, promoting growth, showcasing diverse talents and advising career development.

name: Network of Black Clinicians
description: The Network of Black Clinicians (NBC) promotes interdisciplinary discourse and scholarship amongst ​Black students in Steinhardt's​ many clinical health programs (occupational therapy, physical therapy, ​counseling, art therapy, music therapy, communicative sciences and disorders etc).​ We aim to build bridges between the professions, create platforms for discussion and research pertaining to racial disparities in health care​, while creating interdepartmental support systems for the wellbeing of future​ Black healthcare providers.
summary: The Network of Black Clinicians (NBC) promotes interdisciplinary discourse and scholarship amongst ​Black students in Steinhardt's​ many clinical health programs.

name: Playback Theatre Troupe
description: The Playback Theatre Troupe (PTT) at NYU is an organization for current graduate students and alumni of NYU’s Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development. The troupe practices and performs playback theatre, an interactive form of improvisational theatre in which audience members tell stories from their lives and watch them be artistically enacted or “played back” on the spot. We are committed to co-creating inclusive spaces where diverse voices and stories are shared, honored, and reflected back with creativity and care. We strive to cultivate experiences that spark meaningful dialogue, foster deep connections, promote empathy and understanding, and inspire positive change on our campus and beyond. Through creative collaboration, PTT also seeks to foster a sense of community among students and alumni.
summary: Our mission is to promote individual and societal change through art and storytelling. We are committed to co-creating supportive and inclusive spaces where diverse voices and stories are shared, honored, and reflected back with creativity and care.

name: Craniofacial Orthodontics Society
description: To provide more representation for patients with craniofacial deformities in relation to orthodontic treatment and therapeutic approaches.
summary: To provide more representation for patients with Craniofacial deformities in relation to Orthodontic treatment and therapeutic approaches.

name: Global Dentistry Club
description: To enlighten and empower dental students about the profound impact dentistry can have in under represented communities on a global scale.
summary: To enlighten and empower dental students about the profound impact dentistry can have in under represented communities on a global scale.

name: Senior Smiles
description:
The mission of Senior Smiles at NYU Dentistry is to cultivate the passion, knowledge, and expertise
needed to work with geriatric patients. Given the aging population, future dentists must understand how to
best serve elderly patients, considering their complex medical histories, differing care priorities, and need
for compassion and understanding. We aim to foster a community where students can learn about geriatric
care by engaging with experts and participating in outreach events with the greater NYU Dentistry
community. Our goal is to spread awareness about the specialized needs of the geriatric population and to
prepare dentists to provide knowledgeable and compassionate care.
summary: A club designed to increase recognition of a critical and growing need for specialized dental care among the older population, both globally and within the NYU Dentistry community.

name: Vietnamese Student Dental Association
description: The Vietnamese Student Dental Association (VSDA) at NYUCD is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and inclusive environment where dental students can come together to promote Vietnamese cultural awareness and enhance the overall community at NYUCD. Our mission is to embrace diversity, celebrate Vietnamese heritage, and engage in cultural exchange. We welcome all individuals, regardless of background, who share our passion for learning about and appreciating Vietnamese culture. We are committed to equality and inclusivity and thus do not discriminate based on race, gender, religion, age, or nationality. VSDA is a platform for cultural enrichment and community enrichment, united in our dedication to academic excellence and cultural appreciation.
summary: The Vietnamese Student Dental Association (VSDA) at NYUCD is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and inclusive environment where dental students can come together to promote Vietnamese cultural awareness.

name: Music Business Ambassadors
description: Providing professional development, creative opportunities, and academic support to students interested in pursuing a career in the music industry.
summary: By hosting cross-disciplinary creative workshops, future-focused industry panels, and field-centric career seminars, the Music Business Ambassadors welcome all Steinhardt students to a community built around collaboration and mutual empowerment.

name: MPAP Community Club
description: The purpose of the MPAP Community Club is to provide a platform for MPAP majors to connect and collaborate with each other across programs! Often our curriculums result in the separation of each study, and in an industry where having a diverse network is extremely important, we want to facilitate a space dedicated to commiseration and collaboration through a shared love of music and performance. We want to give students easy and efficient access to one another and provide opportunities for them to work on unique and fulfilling projects that reflect the reality of the industry!
summary: The purpose of the MPAP Community Club is to provide a platform for MPAP majors to connect and collaborate with each other across programs!

name: SheComputes
description:
We serve as an empowering space for women-identifying theoretical natural scientists to engage in mentorship, community, and networking as we work towards gender equity. This organization support the needs of our community, including guest speakers, professional development, social events, and more!

Join our Mailing List
summary: Theoretical and Computational Natural Sciences Women's Collective

name: Pakistani Dental Students Association
description:
The PDSA is dedicated to supporting Pakistani dental students who aspire to become future oral
healthcare doctors in the United States. Our mission is to foster an inclusive environment that
celebrates diversity while providing a supportive community for our members. We believe that
our cultural heritage offers a rich tapestry of traditions, values, and perspectives that can greatly
enhance the NYU experience for all students.
summary:
The PDSA is dedicated to supporting Pakistani dental students who aspire to become future oral
healthcare doctors in the United States.

name: End Overdose
description: End Overdose sees a future where every individual impacted by drug use finds support, resources, and a pathway to recovery. Our vision encompasses a society that prioritizes health, compassion, and evidence-based solutions to address the drug overdose epidemic. This is the NYU Chapter of the national organization with the same name.
summary: End Overdose sees a future where every individual impacted by drug use finds support, resources, and a pathway to recovery.

name: New York University College of Dentistry 2028 Class Council
summary: This page is designated for the class council of the class of 2028

name: Students for Street Wellness
description:
The Street Health Outreach and Wellness Program will be dedicated to addressing the overlooked health needs of individuals experiencing homelessness, particularly focusing on foot health. Recognizing the critical role that foot care plays in overall well-being, our program provides foot soaks and basic care to vulnerable populations at Bellevue Hospital’s Primary Care Safety Net Clinic. Through compassionate service, we aim to not only alleviate physical discomfort but also foster trust and connection between patients and caregivers. Our mission is twofold: to improve the health outcomes of those facing the harsh realities of homelessness, and to provide medical students with invaluable experience in serving marginalized communities in a non-medical setting. By engaging in direct, empathetic care, students gain deeper insights into the complex challenges our patients face, from chronic illnesses to the psychological toll of living on the streets. This hands-on involvement helps cultivate essential skills in trauma-informed care and clinical empathy. We will be committed to bridging the gap in healthcare for our city’s most vulnerable residents while enriching the education of future healthcare professionals. Through our foot soak program, we aim to offer a moment of relief and dignity to our patients, alongside an opportunity for students to learn and grow as compassionate, culturally sensitive clinicians.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Street Health Outreach and Wellness Program will be dedicated to addressing the overlooked health needs of individuals experiencing homelessness, particularly focusing on foot health.

name: Community Health Access Initiative
description:
Our mission is to improve healthcare access and outcomes for underserved populations in New York City by focusing on preventative care, health screenings, and education. By partnering with local organizations, we aim to address the unique health challenges these groups face, including heightened risks for hypertension, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. We aim to provide regular screenings and culturally sensitive care in community centers, complemented by multilingual educational resources on managing common health conditions. By empowering individuals with knowledge, we encourage proactive health management and guide them toward appropriate healthcare services. Through education and consistent follow-up, we ensure that people are both informed and supported in seeking further care when necessary. Ultimately, our goal is to foster a healthier population in New York City by linking education with actionable healthcare access.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Our mission is to improve healthcare access and outcomes for underserved populations in New York City by focusing on preventative care, health screenings, and education.

name: Poly Programming Club
description:
We promote competitive programming at NYU Tandon! In the fall, we send teams to represent our school at ICPC, and in the spring, we host our own competitions. Whether you’re just starting out or already an experienced competitor, you’ll find your place here. We host meetings every Thursday from 5:00 to 6:30pm in 370 Jay Street Room 834.

The best way to stay in touch with us is through our discord: https://discord.gg/9TMY8pK4SW
summary:
The official competitive programming club at NYU Tandon!

Join our Discord! https://discord.com/invite/9TMY8pK4SW

name: Psi Chi The International Honor Society in Psychology at NYU
description:
Psi Chi, the International Honor Society in Psychology, was founded in 1929 to promote excellence in scholarship and advance the field of psychology. The New York University chapter, established in 1933, builds on this legacy by facilitating a community where psychology students can excel academically and professionally. 

Members can engage in diverse activities, including:

  • Workshops and Skill-Building Events: Sessions that equip members with research, clinical, and academic skills essential for success in psychology.
  • Research and Mentorship Opportunities: Collaborations with NYU faculty and alumni offer valuable hands-on experience.
  • Networking and Professional Development: Events designed to connect members with professionals, alumni, and industry leaders.

Our community is ideal for psychology students eager to learn, lead, and contribute to the field. Join Psi Chi at NYU to connect with passionate individuals and take meaningful steps towards a rewarding career in psychology.
summary: The Psi Chi Chapter at NYU is part of the International Honor Society in Psychology, dedicated to promoting excellence in the field. Members benefit from recognition, scholarships, & supportive community, offering opportunities to foster academic growth.

name: Tandon Office of Student Advocacy
summary: The Office of Student Advocacy plays a vital role in supporting students facing challenging situations during their time at Tandon.Our office works with various other offices at NYU to help students solve problems and develop self-advocacy skills.

name: Forensic Dentistry Club
description:
The Forensic Dentistry Club is dedicated to foster a tight knit community and provide those interested with a thorough understanding of the interprofessional relationship between forensic science and dentistry, through teaching of age estimation, bite mark analysis, maxillofacial trauma, and litigation involving malpractice.

 

The Forensic Dentistry Club objectives:

1. Develop a working knowledge of the role of a forensic dentist

2. Understand how to pursue a career in forensic dentistry

3. Learn the techniques a forensic dentist utilizes to identify human remains

4. Advance the field of forensic dentistry and forensic science as a whole
summary: Forensic Dentistry Club

name: The Black Care Collective
description: The Black Care Collective warmly welcomes and supports all Black-identifying students at the NYU Silver School of Social Work, along with their allies. Our goal is to foster a sense of community among all students interested in enhancing awareness of pressing health and social welfare issues impacting the Black community. We prioritize creating safe spaces that encourage self-reflection and understanding, empowering the Black community to raise their voices for meaningful change in society.
summary: The goal of the Black Care Collective is to foster a sense of community among Black students and their allies while enhancing awareness of pressing health and social welfare issues impacting the Black community.

name: feminist theatre company
description: The feminist theatre company is a student-driven club at Tisch School of the Arts. We are dedicated to creating a safe, inclusive environment for women and non-binary artists to explore and produce feminist-driven performance pieces. We aim to engage students from across Tisch's diverse departments, Drama, Film, TV, Dance, Performance Studies, and beyond, by fostering collaboration, creativity, and dialogue around issues of gender, identity, and social justice. Whether you’re interested in acting, directing, writing, design, or production, the collective is a space where artists can experiment with bold ideas and challenge traditional narratives. Through our productions, we hope to provoke thought, inspire action, and ultimately create change within both the artistic community and society at large.
summary:
a club dedicated to celebrating and staging performances by women for women ❤️‍🔥💋

View our calendar of events and join us for an upcoming program.

Please reach out to us for any questions at gph.alumni@nyu.edu.
summary: Our alumni are global leaders in health departments, not-for-profits, non-governmental organizations, consulting firms, and ministries of health.

name: Romanian and Moldovan Students Association - All-University
description: The Romanian and Moldovan Students Association is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community for students of Romanian and Moldovan heritage and those interested in our cultures. We aim to promote cultural awareness, provide educational resources, and create a supportive environment through events, networking opportunities, and collaborative efforts that celebrate our shared heritage and build connections.
summary: The Romanian and Moldovan Students Association is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community for students of Romanian and Moldovan heritage and those interested in our cultures. We aim to celebrate our shared heritage and build connections.

name: Central Asian Hub - All-University
description:
Central Asian Hub, or CAH, is a community at NYU dedicated to preserving and promoting the cultures of Central Asia while building meaningful connections among students. Our organization creates a welcoming space where Central Asian students can find community, share their identities, and feel represented on campus.

Through cultural, social, and educational events, we provide opportunities for students from all backgrounds to engage with Central Asian traditions, history, and contemporary experiences. From celebrations and discussions to collaborative campus events, Central Asian Hub serves as a platform for cultural exchange and unity.

Central Asian Hub is about belonging. We aim to strengthen community, support student voices, and create spaces where culture is not only preserved, but shared and celebrated.

"The mission of the organization shall be to preserve and promote Central Asian culture, foster a sense of community among Central Asian students, and provide a platform for cultural exchange at New York University"

– Meruyet, the Founder of CAH
summary: Uniting, sharing, and celebrating on of the most diverse regions of the world – the Central Asia – at New York University.

name: North African Society - All-University
description: Salam and welcome! We are NAS and our primary focus is on establishing a vibrant North African community on campus. We prioritize the showcasing of North African traditions and cultural heritage through innovative means that both inform and engage the NYU community. Inclusivity, knowledge sharing, community spirit, and the creation of memorable experiences within NYU's North African community are values we hold in high regard.
summary: Our primary focus is on establishing a vibrant North African community on campus. We prioritize the showcasing of North African traditions and cultural heritage through innovative means that both inform and engage the NYU community.

name: Students of Color for Change - All-University
description: Our mission for Students of Color for Change focuses on uniting people of color on campus. We will do this by focusing on social justice movements, connecting with local and international communities, and creating a safe space for multicultural events and teachings. Our goal is to fight for the enrichment of life, labor, dignity, and general conditions of POC students on campus and in the city.
summary: Students of Color for Change focuses on uniting POC on campus. We focus on social justice movements, connecting with local and international communities, and creating a safe space for multicultural events and teachings.

name: Lasers in Dentistry
description: Lasers in Dentistry
summary: Lasers in Dentistry
