description: Our board is here to serve all members in the College of Arts and Science Class of 2028 through advocacy and programming. We also strive to act as a liaison between you and administration.
summary: CAS Student Council's Class of 2028 Class Board

name: Organizational Development Association/Washington Square Management Consulting
description:
What is ODA? 

ODA is the I/O Psychology student organization that serves as a centralized hub for the current student body, NYU, and alumni. 

 

ODA provides the opportunity to connect with alumni and experts in various I/O Psychology fields to help facilitate outside the classroom learning and make connections to allow for career placement and growth following the program. 


What is WSMC?

WSMC is a Masters-led consulting organization, aimed at providing I/O Psychology students with the necessary tools and experiences to assume consulting careers post graduation. 

 

WSMC strives to develop strong career and networking opportunities for all consultants. Coupled with the extensive training program and client projects, WSMC consultants seek challenging positions post graduation.
summary: ODA is the I/O Psychology student organization that serves as a centralized hub for the current student body, NYU, and alumni. WSMC is a Masters-led management consulting group of I/O Psychology. Both groups provide opportunities to graduate students.

name: Pre-Veterinary Society
description: The mission of the organization shall be the Pre-Veterinary Club at NYU, which provides opportunities for students on the pre-vet track to get support for their veterinary school application, find volunteering and job opportunities, help the local community, and provide a space where pre-vet students can build community with one another, support each others professional development and discuss their future career path.
summary: Please add a summary here.

name: East Asian Culture & Language Lounge - All-University
description: The mission of East Asian Culture & Language Lounge at NYU is to create an inclusive and relaxed environment where NYU students can explore and appreciate East Asian cultures and languages, fostering connections across diverse backgrounds through shared cultural experiences. Our club is dedicated to promoting cultural exchange, building a community of East Asian enthusiasts, and enhancing mutual understanding through engaging and enjoyable activities.
summary: Welcome to EAC, a welcoming East Asian culture club celebrating languages, traditions, and arts through fun events, discussions, and community-building for all enthusiasts.

name: Slavic Society at NYU - All-University
description: The Slavic Society at NYU is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and inclusive community that unites Slavic students with all NYU students interested in exploring Slavic cultures. We aim to promote and preserve the rich traditions, languages, and histories of the Slavic region by providing opportunities for cultural exchange, educational events, and networking opportunities for members of the Slavic Society with professionals at home and abroad. Our meetings and events are open to all NYU students, creating a welcoming space where everyone can connect, learn, and celebrate the diverse heritage of the Slavic world.
summary: The Slavic Society at NYU is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and inclusive community that unites Slavic students with all NYU students interested in exploring Slavic cultures.

name: Alzheimer's Association at NYU - All-University
description: The mission of the Alzheimer's Association at NYU is to provide a platform for students, whether they have family or friends affected by Alzheimer’s or dementia, want to give back to their community, or are pursuing a healthcare career, the chance to raise awareness and support in the fight against the disease. The club partners with the NYC Alzheimer's Association to fundraise for patient care, advocacy, and the Walk to End Alzheimer's event.
summary: Welcome to Alzheimer's Association at NYU! A platform for students who want to give back to the community and raise awareness about Alzheimer's.

name: Polish and Eastern European Society
description: The Polish and Eastern European Society's mission is to create a supportive and welcoming community for anyone interested in learning about Slavic culture at NYU, as well as a means of connection for students of Polish or Eastern European heritage. We aim to promote Polish heritage, providing a space both for the study of culture and cultural exchange. Through academic and social programming, we will broaden the NYU community's understanding of Poland's rich history, traditions, art, and its contemporary role in the world. Additionally, we strive to educate about Eastern European culture as a whole, recognizing how interconnected the region is and providing a broader platform for European students to participate and learn from.
summary: update information here.

name: Department of East Asian Studies
description:
The Department of East Asian Studies at New York University offers courses on China, Japan and Korea. The focus of the undergraduate program is primarily on language, history and literature and the way in which these three civilizations have interacted with the Western world to reconstitute received cultures into modern societies. 

 

Get ready for a fantastic lineup of events...

  • Movie Nights: Join us for cinematic adventures under the stars. Grab your popcorn and enjoy great films with friends.
  • Language and Reading Tables: Sharpen your language skills and dive into captivating books at our language and reading tables. It's a fantastic opportunity for intellectual exploration.
  • Academic Workshops: Expand your academic horizons with specialized workshops for graduate students. Dive deep into your field of study and enhance your skills.
  • Lectures: Stimulate your mind with thought-provoking talks from experts in various fields. These sessions will broaden your academic horizons and inspire your graduate journey.

...and more!
summary: The Department of EAS offers courses on China, Japan and Korea. The focus of the undergraduate program is primarily on language, history and literature and the way in which these three civilizations have interacted with the Western world.

name: Bern Dibner Library of Science and Technology
description:
The library workers of the Bern Dibner Library of Science and Technology seek to educate and support our NYU Brooklyn and Tandon community as they research within and transform their information ecosystems. We accomplish this by contributing our expertise to building collections, teaching information literacy, creating welcoming spaces, and facilitating knowledge creation.

Library Hours
summary: Stay updated, get inspired, and have some fun at NYU Dibner Library! 📚

name: 19 Washington Square North
description: 19 Washington Square North is a gateway for scholarly and institutional connectivity between NYU's Abu Dhabi and New York campuses. Its programs offer opportunities for New York and Abu Dhabi faculty to collaborate and its local team of administrators supports the day-to-day business operations of NYU Abu Dhabi in New York. From its central location on the Washington Square campus, 19 WSN shares NYUAD's achievements and global impact with the greater NYU community and the general public, and provides a home away from home for NYUAD students, faculty, and staff.
summary: 19 Washington Square North is a gateway for scholarly and institutional connectivity between NYU's Abu Dhabi and New York campuses

name: Asian Student Support Group
summary: This group provides a safe space for Asian students to explore identity as developing Asian social workers and to seek support with other Asian Silver School students and allies.

name: NYU Student Mindfulness Group
summary: The NYU Student Mindfulness Group creates a safe and welcoming space for all NYU students to explore together in the community the philosophy and practice of mindfulness.

name: Human Genomics Interest Group
description:
The Human Genetics Interest Group is dedicated to fostering an understanding and appreciation of genetics within the medical field. Our mission is to provide medical students with opportunities to explore the rapidly evolving field of genetics, promote awareness of its clinical applications, and facilitate discussions on how genetic knowledge can impact patient care, disease prevention, and treatment strategies. Through educational events, collaborations, and hands-on experiences, we aim to inspire future physicians to integrate genetic principles into their practice, advancing personalized medicine and improving healthcare outcomes.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The Human Genetics Interest Group is dedicated to fostering an understanding and appreciation of genetics within the medical field.

name: Business of Storytelling
description:
The Business of Storytelling at NYU Tisch builds a thriving community for aspiring filmmakers interested in both commercial and narrative storytelling, bridging academic training and real-world industry demands to foster sustainable creative careers.

Club activities focus on rigorous dialogue, brainstorming, and collaboration through regular concept-development sessions. Two cornerstone events—industry engagement and pitch nights—equip members for professional success. Industry engagement includes lectures, workshops, and panel discussions with experienced professionals in marketing, production, and content creation. Pitch nights offer practical experience, feedback, and exposure to industry practices.

Additionally, the club actively expands networks beyond campus, emphasizing multicultural perspectives and diversity by engaging international students. The club prioritizes collaboration and idea exchange especially between international students, fostering a globally minded creative environment. Connections with technology firms, advertising agencies, and governmental organizations further provide unique opportunities, innovative project ideas, and diverse career pathways, preparing members for successful careers beyond traditional campus boundaries.
summary: The Business of Storytelling aims to foster strong collaboration between local and international students, eventually connecting them with industry leaders to build sustainable and globally minded creative careers.

name: Critical Social Work Collective
description: The NYU Silver Critical Social Work Collective (CSWC) is a student-led group dedicated to creating spaces where social work students critically engage with clinical practices, research, traditions, and interventions through an anti-oppressive, decolonial, and justice-oriented lens. We aim to challenge dominant paradigms in clinical social work by interrogating power, positionality, and systemic inequities while amplifying alternative approaches centered on liberation, healing, and community care.
summary: The NYU Silver Critical Social Work Collective (CSWC) is a student-led group dedicated to creating spaces where social work students critically engage with clinical practices, research, traditions, and interventions.

name: NYU Campus Safety
summary: NYU's Department of Campus Safety is committed to providing safety and security for our community and property, enabling the pursuit of academic and professional goals.

name: Interprofessional Healthcare Students' Alliance
description:
Our mission is to build a new generation of healthcare providers at NYU that have the confidence and competence needed to lead interprofessional teams that serve a patient’s whole health, rather than as isolated systems. We believe that such collaboration can be the means for enhanced and more sustainable health at the individual, population, and environmental levels. In collaboration with the NYU dental and nursing schools, we aim to accomplish this mission by fostering networking opportunities and curating educational events that encourage meaningful connections across disciplines to enhance professional growth. We also seek to empower students with the knowledge and skills needed to advocate for healthcare policy reform and drive systemic change in public health as a unified front.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: Our mission is to build a new generation of healthcare providers at NYU that have the confidence and competence needed to lead interprofessional teams that serve a patient’s whole health, rather than as isolated systems.

name: SPS Consulting and Business Strategy Society
description: The Consulting & Business Strategy Society at NYU SPS provides a platform for students to explore careers in Consulting through case competitions, skill-building events, alumni panels, and live projects with real-world clients. Our goal is to bridge academic learning with practical application, nurturing a community of curious problem-solvers and innovative thinkers.
summary: CBSS helps students build practical strategy and problem solving skills through case workshops, industry exposure, and competitions.

name: Asian Leadership Initiative
summary: ALI seeks to empower the next generation of Asian American leaders by fostering a supportive, professional community. Our mission is to bridge the gap between aspiring students and accomplished AAPI leaders

name: Climate and Energy Club
summary: We help NYU undergrads pursue impactful careers in the climate and energy space by democratizing educational and professional resources. Through our events and programs we provide the knowledge, skills, and connections needed to drive meaningful change.

name: Social Work Book Club
description: The Social Work Book Club is dedicated to exploring the intersection of young adult literature and social justice. We aim to deepen our understanding of marginalized communities’ challenges, triumphs, and unique experiences by reading and discussing a curated selection of diverse YA and middle-grade novels. As social workers, we are committed to uplifting diverse voices, promoting empathy, and fostering meaningful dialogue that reflects the real-world issues faced by youth today. By sharing these stories, we seek to enhance our practice, broaden our perspective, and advocate for positive change in the lives of those we serve. Through our shared love of books, we strive to build a supportive community where members can connect and grow both personally and professionally.
summary: The Social Work Book Club is dedicated to exploring the intersection of young adult literature and social justice.

name: AI in Dentistry Club
description: We, the students of NYU College of Dentistry, in order to promote awareness, discussion, and innovation in the rapidly evolving field of artificial intelligence (AI) as it relates to dentistry, do hereby create the AI in Dentistry Club of NYU Dentistry, enacting this constitution for its government.
summary: This club is established to provide a collaborative space for students and faculty to explore how AI is transforming dental education, clinical care, diagnostics, and research.

name: Eastern European Dental Society
description: We, the students of NYU Dentistry, recognize the importance of fostering a community that addresses the dental school challenges and cultural practices of Eastern Europe. By creating the Eastern European Dental Society at NYU Dentistry, we aim to provide a platform for cross-cultural exchange, enhance educational opportunities, and promote collaboration among dental professionals. This society will benefit the NYU Dentistry population by offering valuable insights into diverse dental practices, encouraging networking and professional growth, and advocating for global oral health initiatives.
summary: We, the students of NYU Dentistry, recognize the importance of fostering a community that addresses the dental school challenges and cultural practices of Eastern Europe.

name: The Gujarati Society
description:
The Gujarati Society at NYU Dentistry aims to celebrate, preserve, and share Gujarati culture within
the dental community by organizing inclusive events, promoting cultural awareness, and fostering a supportive
environment for all students interested in Gujarati traditions, language, and community engagement.
summary: The Gujarati Society at NYU Dentistry aims to celebrate, preserve, and share Gujarati culture within the dental community.

name: Head and Neck Oncology Society
description: The Head and Neck Oncology Society is dedicated to enhancing awareness, education, and research concerning head and neck cancers. The Society will focus on non-surgical and surgical cancer diagnosis, the study of pathologies, and the support of cancer patients, with an emphasis on aspects of oncology. We are proud to have been recognized by The Office of the Mayor of New York City under Mayor Eric Adams.
summary: The Head and Neck Oncology Society is dedicated to enhancing awareness, education, and research concerning head and neck cancers.

name: Racket and Paddle Club
description:
The mission of the Racket & Paddle Club at NYU Dentistry is to foster a vibrant and inclusive community where dental students of all skill levels can meet new people, stay active, and enjoy playing racket sports.


Click on the globe icon below to access all of our links!
summary: The mission of the Racket and Paddle Club at NYU Dentistry is to foster a vibrant and inclusive community where players of all skill levels can meet new people, stay active, and enjoy playing racket sports.

name: Nutrition and Dentistry Club
description: The mission of the "Nutrition & Dentistry" Club at NYU Dentistry is to promote awareness and understanding of the intricate connection between dietary choices, oral health, and oral/gut microbiomes.
summary: The mission of the "Nutrition & Dentistry" Club at NYU Dentistry is to promote awareness and understanding of the intricate connection between dietary choices, oral health, and oral/gut microbiomes.

name: Punjabi Student Dental Organization
description: The Punjabi Student Organization aims to foster an inclusive environment that celebrates the rich tapestry of Punjabi culture. Through cultural events, educational initiatives, community outreach, and mentorship, we aim to build bridges between diverse communities, enhance cultural awareness in healthcare, and contribute meaningfully to the advancement of oral health both locally and globally.
summary: The Punjabi Student Organization aims to foster an inclusive environment that celebrates the rich tapestry of Punjabi culture. Through cultural events, educational initiatives, community outreach, and mentorship.

name: Pre Professional Advising
summary: Pre Professional Advising Team

name: GenART
description:
GenART is a student-run Tisch club at NYU that brings together students passionate about the creative possibilities of artificial intelligence. We focus on AI-driven artistic expression—particularly in video, image, and other emerging digital forms—by hosting workshops, inviting guest speakers, and fostering collaborative projects that push the boundaries of creativity.

Our club has proudly collaborated with innovative industry leaders such as Pika, Hailuo AI, Livepeer, Flora, Alibaba Cloud’s WanAI, and Tencent. By connecting students with these partners, we aim to bridge the gap between academia and industry, giving our members access to cutting-edge tools, mentorship, and opportunities to experiment with the future of creative technology. GenART is more than just a club—it’s a community where ideas, technology, and art come together to inspire the next generation of creators.
summary: GenART is a student-run Tisch club dedicated to exploring the full potential of AI in creative expression. We host workshops and invite guest speakers to provide a platform for students interested in AI technology and its artistic applications.

name: The FalseSettos NYU
description: The FalseSettos is NYU’s premier musical improv & sketch troupe! We strive to make people laugh, cry, or anything in between (we don’t really care as long as you feel).  Since our establishment, we have put on four shows: “Sneak Peek”, “The Official Show”, “Egg Hunt”, & “Who’s Your Daddy” that have been at different venues across campus, including Riese Lounge in the Tisch School of the Arts! We have students ranging across multiple different schools such as the College of Arts and Sciences, the Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development, and the Tisch School of the Arts. Overall. Come check out one of our shows and follow us on instagram @thefalsesettosnyu.
summary: The FalseSettos is NYU’s premier musical improv & sketch troupe! We specialize in audience interaction through various games, scenes, and musical bits.

name: Sigma Chi - All-University
summary: Sigma Chi is NYU’s newest fraternity, founded on Friendship, Justice, and Learning. We build brotherhood, develop leaders, and serve our community. Join us to make history and create a lasting legacy on campus.

name: Stern Class of 2029
summary: Stern Class of 2029

name: The Radiology club at NYU Dentistry
description: The Oral and Maxillofacial Radiology Club at NYU College of Dentistry is dedicated to enhancing student education in radiographic interpretation and diagnostic decision-making.
summary: The Oral and Maxillofacial Radiology Club at NYU College of Dentistry is dedicated to enhancing student education in radiographic interpretation and diagnostic decision-making.

name: NYU Blood Partners
description:
NYU Blood Partners is a student-led organization dedicated to increasing awareness, accessibility, and participation in blood donation within our medical school community. In partnership with New York Blood Center (NYBC), we aim to educate students on the importance of blood donation and empower active participation in this life-saving practice.

Our mission is rooted in the belief that, as future physicians, we have both the privilege and the responsibility to lead by example in advancing public health. Giving blood is one of the simplest yet most impactful ways to save lives, and cultivating this habit early fosters a lifelong commitment to service, empathy, and community engagement. Through targeted outreach and visible campus engagement, we aim to normalize routine giving among our peers and ensure the medical school community plays a meaningful role in meeting the blood needs of patients in New York City and beyond.

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: NYU Blood Partners is a student-led organization dedicated to increasing awareness, accessibility, and participation in blood donation within our medical school community.

name: Data Science Club
description: The Data Science Club at NYU is a venture to foster a tight-knit community of young data scientists at NYU, aimed especially at undergraduate DS Majors, Joint-Majors and Minors. They host events, invite established speakers from inside & outside the NYU circle and organize teams for hackathon/datathons/Kaggle Competitions – all to maximize learning opportunities in the sphere of Data Science. If you have any suggestions for the DSC or want to get involved, please email cds-dsc@nyu.edu.
summary: We empower undergraduate students to explore, learn, and grow in data science through collaborative projects, competitions, and expert-led events.

name: Music Therapy Graduate Student Club
description: The Music Therapy Graduate Student Club’s mission is to cultivate a vibrant and inclusive community for graduate students passionate about music therapy. We strive to support personal and professional growth through collaborative learning, advocacy, creative expression, and community outreach. By fostering connections among students, faculty, and professionals, we aim to enrich our understanding of music’s therapeutic potential. Through shared experiences and meaningful dialogue, we hope to empower one another to explore, innovate, and contribute to the field of music therapy with empathy, integrity, and purpose.
summary: The Music Therapy Graduate Student Club’s mission is to cultivate a vibrant and inclusive community for graduate students passionate about music therapy.

name: NYU Sex Therapy Club
summary: At the NYU Sex Therapy Club, we are committed to fostering a brave, inclusive, and sex-positive space where future clinicians, researchers, and advocates can explore the intersections of sexuality, mental health, pleasure, identity, and justice.

name: CAS Class of 2029
description: Our board is here to serve all members in the College of Arts and Science Class of 2029 through advocacy and programming. We also strive to act as a liaison between you and administration.
summary: CAS Student Council's Class of 2029 Class Board

name: The Catholic Center at NYU
description: The Catholic Center at NYU is the center of Catholic university life for students in New York City. We host events that are open to the public, and to undergraduate, graduate students, and faculty, especially. Come and visit our space at 238 Thompson Street (ground floor). We are open M-F 11am-9pm and Sat/Sun 1pm-9pm.
summary: The center of Catholic university life in New York City.

name: Mariachi Violetas de NYU
description: Mariachi Violetas is dedicated to celebrating and sharing the rich tradition of mariachi music (the music of Mexico). Our mission is to create an inclusive, family-like community where students from across NYU connect through music-making, performance, and cultural exchange. Through concerts, collaborative events, and outreach within NYU and beyond, we aim to honor mariachi’s cultural roots, foster musical growth, and provide an inviting space for all who wish to learn and participate.
summary: Mariachi Violetas is dedicated to celebrating and sharing the rich tradition of mariachi music (the music of Mexico).

name: NYU Video Game Orchestra
description:
The mission of NYU Video Game Orchestra is to celebrate the cultural and artistic value of video game music through the creation of an inclusive, student-run performance and community space for the study, adaptation, and live performance of music from video games and game-adjacent media. To students across Steinhardt, we commit to performance opportunities, professional and leadership experience, and spaces for creativity; to the broader NYU Steinhardt community, we commit to public concerts and other community events to enrich campus culture.

Interested? Sign up by clicking here!

Rehearsals are in Paulson every Sunday from 5:00–6:30 PM.
summary: We celebrate the cultural and artistic value of video game music through the creation of an inclusive, student-run performance and community space .

name: Courant Computer Science
summary: Courant Institute of Mathematical Science Department of Computer Science events

name: Formula One Club
description: First of its kind, this is a Formula One club. This club aims to bring together people with an interest in this sport. We will host parties inviting people to enjoy the races in a large group. During such gatherings, we will stream the race with food and trivia games. Apart from hosting watch parties, we will also have regular events with members to discuss news from the world of Formula One and its implications for the races, the teams, and the drivers. Furthermore, the members will be invited to present their analyses of races, giving their views on the many facets of Formula One racing. Such an event will be hosted to encourage the members to pursue their passion for the sport professionally, if they’re interested. As for the future of this club, we hope to garner enough support to invite F1 professionals to speak about their experience and share some key insights about the sport that would help those who want to be a part of Formula One.
summary: The F1 Club aims to bring together people with an interest in this sport. We will host watch parties inviting people to enjoy the races in a large group. We will also have regular events with members to discuss news from the world of Formula One.

name: Middle Eastern and North African Student Association
description: The Middle East North African (MENA) Student Association is committed to fostering a supportive, inclusive, and empowering community for students of Middle Eastern and North African descent, as well as all those interested in appreciating the region's diverse cultures and experiences. Our mission is to celebrate MENA heritage, promote cultural understanding, and advocate for the unique challenges and contributions of MENA-identifying individuals in medicine. Through education, mentorship, community outreach, and collaboration with peers and faculty, we aim to enhance representation, cultural competence, and equity within the medical profession.
summary: MENA Student Association is committed to fostering a supportive, inclusive, and empowering community for students of Middle Eastern and North African descent.

name: Unity of Many Minds
summary: Our mission is to foster inclusive community engagement through a social work lens, integrating culturally diverse mindfulness practices to promote creativity, holistic well-being , and intercultural understanding.

name: Brooklyn Research Bloc
description: Our mission is to foster a strong sense of community among PhD students, postdocs, and researchers at NYU Tandon’s Brooklyn campus by creating consistent opportunities for social connection, collaboration, and mutual support. Through informal gatherings, cultural exploration, peer-to-peer networking, professional development workshops, and mentorship programs, we aim to make the Brooklyn campus a vibrant, inclusive, and intellectually inspiring environment that supports both personal growth and academic success.
summary: Our mission is to foster a strong sense of community among PhD students, postdocs, and researchers at NYU Tandon’s Brooklyn campus by creating consistent opportunities for social connection, collaboration, and mutual support.

name: NYU Sonic Collective
description: NYU Sonic Collective is a student-run, orchestral-based ensemble open to all NYU students—no audition, specific skill level, instrumentation, or major required. The Collective offers unique opportunities for student composers, from creating original works and arranging existing pieces to orchestrating new parts for familiar compositions. Dedicated to reimagining the orchestral experience, the Sonic Collective strives to build an adaptive, collaborative, and inclusive musical community.
summary: NYU Sonic Collective is a student-run, orchestral-based ensemble open to all NYU students—no audition, specific skill level, instrumentation, or major required.

name: Gallatin Supper Club
description: The Gallatin Supper Club is a student-led organization that seeks to foster deeper connections between the Gallatin Community. The club will host recurring supper nights where anyone is welcome to come share a meal with the club. Throughout the supper, we will have themed discussions and opportunities for the community to interact with each other. We hope to not only allow the Gallatin Community to share a meal together but also allow people to be vulnerable, feel safe, and foster more meaningful relationships amongst themselves. The club will be open to Undergraduate and Graduate students.
summary: The Gallatin Supper Club is a student-led organization that seeks to foster deeper connections between the Gallatin Community. The club will host recurring supper nights where anyone is welcome to come share a meal with the club.

name: New York University College of Dentistry 2029 Class Council
summary: Council for the Class of 2029

name: Study Away at NYU Abu Dhabi
description:
Based in 19 Washington Square North, New York. Our office is dedicated to empowering NYU students who wish to study away at NYU Abu Dhabi. We provide comprehensive guidance on all aspects of the study away journey whether it’s navigating logistics, understanding application processes or preparing for life in Abu Dhabi. Through regular events, cultural programming and personalized peer support, we help you connect with UAE culture and the NYUAD community even before you arrive. Our goal is to make every transition seamless, enriching and welcoming so that students from across NYU can fully immerse themselves in the unique opportunities offered at NYU Abu Dhabi.

https://www.instagram.com/studyawayatnyuad?igsh=MWFrMDRkcjEzazBwdA==

www.youtube.com/@studyawayatnyuad9739
summary: Based in New York, we help NYU students study away at NYU Abu Dhabi, We offer NYU New York students a chance to immerse themselves into UAE by the numerous diverse events we host, we equally offer full support with logistics and the transition process.

name: Wilderness Medicine Interest Group
description:
The mission of the NYUGSOM Wilderness Medicine Club is to provide students with opportunities to learn, practice, and explore the principles of medical care in remote and challenging environments. Through workshops, simulations, guest speakers, and outdoor experiences, we aim to cultivate confidence and competence in managing medical emergencies anywhere—from urban parks to backcountry expeditions. Our interdisciplinary approach highlights how all medical specialties contribute to the dynamic field of Wilderness Medicine.

 

This organization and its events are open to all currently enrolled NYU Grossman School of Medicine Students.
summary: The mission of the NYUGSOM Wilderness Medicine Club is to provide students with opportunities to learn, practice, and explore the principles of medical care in remote and challenging environments.

name: Violet Vault
description:
Violet Vault is a student run fashion collective at NYU dedicated to creativity, collaboration, and the celebration of emerging talent. Our mission is to provide an inclusive and supportive space where students across all disciplines and experience levels can explore fashion as a form of artistic expression. We produce original photoshoots, curate student-designed looks, and develop creative projects that allow members to gain hands-on experience in styling, design, modeling, photography, creative direction, and production.

At Violet Vault, we believe fashion exists at the intersection of art, identity, and storytelling. Our collective encourages members to work together to bring innovative concepts to life from initial idea to final execution. We actively support experimentation, individuality, and creative risk taking, offering members the freedom and resources to develop their skills while contributing to a larger creative vision.

Open to all students regardless of background or experience, Violet Vault is committed to amplifying diverse voices and perspectives within the fashion and art communities at NYU. As a growing organization, we aim to cultivate a vibrant creative network on campus, foster professional development, and shape a new space for fashion innovation one look, one concept, and one collaboration at a time.
summary: Violet Vault is a student-run NYU fashion collective centered on creativity, collaboration, and individuality, by producing photoshoots and student-designed work in an inclusive creative space. We are open to all levels of experience.

name: South Asian Affinity Group
description: We invite anyone interested in exploring South Asian heritage and helping build a safe community space to bond, share, and advocate for equitable and inclusive mental health practices. Our vision is to create an interconnected community where South Asian students and others can support one another, grow together, and learn from each other’s experiences. Members will have a welcoming space to express their hopes and concerns, connect through events and programming, and find solidarity in shared identities. We welcome anyone who cares about mental health and South Asian culture. Together, we aim to build a collaborative environment where we can uplift one another academically and personally while celebrating the richness of our heritage.
summary: We invite anyone interested in exploring South Asian heritage and helping build a safe community space to bond, share, and advocate for equitable and inclusive mental health practices.

name: Afghan Student Organization [New Club in Development Candidate]
description: The Afghan Student Organization (ASO) is a space to experience Afghan culture through food, music, and community. Open to everyone! If you are looking for good vibes, great food, and a place to feel welcome, you will find a home here :)
summary: The Afghan Student Organization (ASO) is a space to experience Afghan culture through food, music, and community. Open to everyone! If you are looking for good vibes, great food, and a place to feel welcome, you will find a home here :)

summary: The Ahlul Bayt Student Association at NYU aims to create opportunities for Shia students at NYU to affirm their identity, engage in spiritual development, and build a community supportive of their personal and professional growth.

summary: As NYU’s first Azerbaijani student organization, AAYF at NYU celebrates culture, heritage, and identity through community gatherings, national holiday celebrations, and programs that foster belonging and intercultural exchange.

summary: BridgeUSA at NYU brings together students of diverse viewpoints to talk about current issues productively and empathetically. Join one of our biweekly meetings -- we're open to everyone, regardless of your level of political knowledge or experience!

summary: Central Americans for Empowerment builds community for Central American students and allies through culture, advocacy, and collective growth. We create safe spaces to connect, celebrate identity, and uplift leadership, wellness, and access on campus.

name: Christian Orthodox Student Association at NYU [New Club in Development Candidate]
description: The Christian Orthodox Student Association at NYU is a community dedicated to fostering spiritual growth, friendships, and engagement with campus life. We welcome all Orthodox Christian students and anyone interested in learning about the faith and tradition.
summary: The Christian Orthodox Student Association is a community dedicated to fostering spiritual growth, friendships, and engagement with campus life. We welcome all Orthodox Christian students and anyone interested in learning about the faith and tradition.

description: The Human Connection Club at NYU invites students to rediscover real-world connection through adventurous, collaborative, tech-free experiences. Inspired by The Anxious Generation, we foster face-to-face friendships, ideas, and community.
summary: The Human Connection Club at NYU invites students to rediscover real-world connection through adventurous, collaborative, tech-free experiences. Inspired by The Anxious Generation, we foster face-to-face friendships, ideas, and community.

description: We are a group dedicated to celebrating everything Irish Culture has to offer. Join us for community, tradition, history, and a little Irish Luck (even if you're not Irish!) ☘️
summary: We are a group dedicated to celebrating everything Irish Culture has to offer. Join us for community, tradition, history, and a little Irish Luck (even if you're not Irish!) ☘️

description: JAMSA at NYU preserves Jamaican culture through unity, education, and tradition. Guided by “Out of many, One people,” we build a vibrant community for students of Jamaican heritage and all who connect with Jamaica’s rich identity.
summary: JAMSA at NYU preserves Jamaican culture through unity, education, and tradition. Guided by “Out of many, One people,” we build a vibrant community for students of Jamaican heritage and all who connect with Jamaica’s rich identity.

description:
GenAudio at NYU is an interdisciplinary research group studying the real-world music applications of AI, DSP, and other artistic media through research projects, industry panels, and hackathons. All majors and levels of experience are welcome!

(Join our discord server for updates)

Meetings: Monday 5pm-6:45pm (35 W 4th St 8th floor Studio C)
summary: GenAudio at NYU is an interdisciplinary research group studying the real-world music applications of AI, DSP, and other artistic media through research projects, industry panels, and hackathons. All majors and levels of experience are welcome!

name: PETAL at NYU [New Club in Development Candidate]
description: PETAL is NYU's only club dedicated to the intersection of policy, ethics, technology, and law. We welcome students from all majors and years. Join us for guest speaker events, writing opportunities, policy discussions, and more!
summary: PETAL is NYU's only club dedicated to the intersection of policy, ethics, technology, and law. We welcome students from all majors and years. Join us for guest speaker events, writing opportunities, policy discussions, and more!

name: Turning Point USA at NYU [New Club in Development Candidate]
description: TPUSA at NYU aims to provide an outlet on campus for those interested in learning about or advocating for the principles of freedom, free markets, and limited government. We are nonpartisan and open to all students who wish to engage in civil dialogue.
summary: TPUSA at NYU aims to provide an outlet on campus for those interested in learning about or advocating for the principles of freedom, free markets, and limited government. We are nonpartisan and open to all students who wish to engage in civil dialogue.

name: Undergraduate Women in Law Association at NYU [New Club in Development Candidate]
description: NYU’s pre-professional group for women pursuing a career in law. Our mission is to build a community among current and aspiring women in the legal profession, offering tailored advice about law school and careers, LSAT prep, and networking events.
summary: NYU’s pre-professional group for women pursuing a career in law. Our mission is to build a community among current and aspiring women in the legal profession, offering tailored advice about law school and careers, LSAT prep, and networking events.

name: Yemeni Student Association at NYU [New Club in Development Candidate]
description: Yemeni Students Association (YSA) is a student-led organization that celebrates, shares, and preserves Yemeni culture and heritage at NYU by fostering a welcoming space for students of Yemen background and for those interested in Yemeni heritage.
summary: Yemeni Students Association (YSA) at NYU is a student-led organization that celebrates, shares, and preserves Yemeni culture and heritage at NYU by fostering a welcoming space for students of Yemen background and for those interested in Yemeni heritage.

name: Young Americans for Freedom at NYU [New Club in Development Candidate]
description: YAF at NYU is a community for students who believe in limited government, individual freedom, free enterprise, and traditional values. We host speakers, discussions, and campus activities to promote free speech, civic duty, and open dialogue.
summary: YAF at NYU is a community for students who believe in limited government, individual freedom, free enterprise, and traditional values. We host speakers, discussions, and campus activities to promote free speech, civic duty, and open dialogue.

name: UX Club NYU
summary: The UX Club at NYU is a student-led community that connects aspiring designers with industry professionals through mentorship, workshops, and events, fostering growth in product design.

name: SPS Black Professionals Collective
description: The Black Professionals Collective (BPC) supports the academic, professional, and personal growth of its members through intentional programming centered on professional development, mentorship, cultural celebration, and community engagement. By fostering collaboration, excellence, and meaningful connections, the Collective creates a strong sense of belonging and provides Black graduate students with the tools, networks, and support needed to thrive at NYU and beyond.
summary: Our mission is to empower and uplift Black graduate students at NYU through professional development, cultural celebration, mentorship, and community, fostering excellence, collaboration, and a strong sense of belonging across disciplines.

name: SPS Workforce Innovation Leadership Network
description: The SPS Workforce Innovation Leadership Network (WILN) is a professional student organization that bridges education with real-world, industry-aligned experience. WILN connects students with short-term, project-based initiatives, micro-internships, and innovation sprints in partnership with organizations, nonprofits, and global institutions. In collaboration with the Workplace Learning Innovation Lab, members collaborate in small teams to address real challenges in strategy, communications, leadership, and innovation, supported by faculty mentors and industry advisors. Through peer consulting circles, case workshops, and high-impact events, students build portfolio-ready deliverables, expand professional networks, and gain hands-on experience that complements their academic studies. WILN is open to SPS undergraduate students across disciplines who are interested in leadership, innovation, and the future of work.
summary: Our mission is to connect NYU SPS undergrads with real-world projects through micro-internships and innovation sprints, helping them build portfolio-ready work, strong professional networks, and the skills needed to lead the future of work.

name: Model Congress [New Club in Development Candidate]
description: Model Congress at NYU intends to foster a community of students passionate about learning how change is accomplished in modern America. Simulating how a bill becomes a law, students discuss issues ranging from NYU legislation to real-world dilemmas.
summary: Model Congress at NYU intends to foster a community of students passionate about learning how change is accomplished in modern America. Simulating how a bill becomes a law, students discuss issues ranging from NYU legislation to real-world dilemmas.

name: Oral Biology Graduate Student Council
description: An active community for pre-dental students to meet each other! We plan to host dentists, and current dental students to do "lunch and learns," host study sessions for DAT prep and OSHA certification courses, and incorporate service events as well as mixers!
summary: An active community for pre-dental students to meet each other!

name: John Brademas Center of NYU
description: Inspired by its founder, former Congressman and NYU President Emeritus, the John Brademas Center of New York University pursues a collection of initiatives in the areas which formed the core of John Brademas' life in public service: the state of Congress and the legislative process in democracies; the shifting dynamics in foreign policy and international affairs; and, the present state and future prospects for higher education, the humanities, arts and culture. With a growing reputation as a home for informed and civil debate on politics, public policy and other major issues facing the nation and global community—the Brademas Center undertakes programs at NYU's campuses in New York City and Washington, D.C., and increasingly around NYU’s global network.
summary: Bringing "thinkers and doers" together. The Center becomes a place where scholars and students can intersect with politicians and policymakers around issues of the day.

name: AStudio at NYU
description: AStudio at NYU supports students interested in design across disciplines, including graphic design, branding, motion graphics, and UI/UX design. We identified the lack of a centralized design community at NYU and set out to create a community centered on practicing design skills, and sharing opportunities. AStudio has extensive experience running panels, workshops, and studio visits, bringing students direct exposure to the design industry. Past programming includes speaker panels with industry professionals, studio tours at Studio Karun and Salotto Studios, and hands-on workshops focused on creative tools and workflows. We also collaborate closely with NYU IT to support and co-host design-related events and educational programming for the broader student body.
summary: AStudio at NYU supports students interested in design across disciplines, including graphic design, branding, motion graphics, and UI/UX design.
